races.race_name: Darley EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Earth Shot
runners.spotlight: Kept on for 2l second to even-money stablemate and fellow newcomer at Newbury (1m, soft) last October; this Oaks/Irish Oaks entry is bred to be suited by the step up in trip (dam 1m6f Group 3 winner); leading claims.
runners.comment: Runner-up over 1m at Newbury on sole 2yo run; this trip can suit & she has leading claims

runners.name: Malika
runners.spotlight: 20-1 and no match for the front three when fourth of 13 on debut at Kempton (1m, AW) last November; others have achieved more but no surprise if she takes a sizeable step forward today for connections who won this last year.
runners.comment: Improvement needed but connections won this last year; could take sizeable step forward

runners.name: Blue Noon
runners.spotlight: Backed into 15-2 when promising fourth of 12 on debut at Leicester (7f, good to soft) last September; this 420,000euros sister to useful 1m winner Naepoint is open to improvement and could be in the mix if staying 1m2f.
runners.comment: 420,000euros yearling who was a promising fourth on sole 2yo start; could be in the mix

runners.name: Velvet Vega
runners.spotlight: 240,000euros yearling; first foal; dam French 1m/9.5f winner (including Listed), sister to 1m4f winner Pappini, half-sister to French 7f Listed winner Lucie Manette, is granddaughter of Irish/Yorkshire Oaks winner Petrushka; this Lope De Vega newcomer is in top hands and could be a contender.
runners.comment: 240,000euros yearling; dam 9.5f Listed winner; in top hands; could be a contender on debut

runners.name: Wild Violet
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 7f AW 2yo winner (RPR 79), half-sister to 2m/2m1f hurdle winner Kilcummin, out of French 9.7f winner; refused to enter stalls and withdrawn from intended debut at Newbury on April 17 (40-1 at the time).
runners.comment: 40-1 when withdrawn from intended debut at Newbury, having refused to enter the stalls

runners.name: Mokata
runners.spotlight: Lost a lot of ground at the start when tenth of 11, beaten just under 13l, on debut at Yarmouth (1m, good; 125-1) three weeks ago; has since left Robert Eddery (remains in same ownership) and new trainer is in fine form; however, it's likely she remains best watched for now.
runners.comment: Lost a lot of ground at the start when down the field on debut; remains best watched

runners.name: Anthena
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at 250-1 on debut at Southwell (7f, AW) last October; has since left Lawrence Mullaney (remains in same ownership); she would be a shock winner on stable debut.
runners.comment: Tailed off at 250-1 on debut at Southwell last October; has since left Lawrence Mullaney

races.race_name: JCB Newmarket Stakes (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings)

runners.name: Poseidon's Warrior
runners.spotlight: Easy win at Goodwood (1m1f, good) last October on second start and he reappeared with a 1l Listed second here (1m1f, good) 16 days ago, staying on well; the first-time hood could help him settle better and he holds leading claims for top trainer who has won three of the last four runnings.
runners.comment: Stayed on well for 1m1f Listed second here on reappearance and holds leading claims

runners.name: My Love Is King
runners.spotlight: Won last November on sole 2yo start (7f, AW) and reappeared with a near-miss in a hot novice contest at Newbury (1m2f, good) a fortnight ago; this Dante/Derby entry is from a classy family and should continue to progress for his top stable; could have a part to play.
runners.comment: Went close in hot novice at Newbury on recent reappearance and could have a part to play

runners.name: Archers Bay
runners.spotlight: Ex-Ballydoyle colt who got off the mark in first-time cheekpieces (retained since) on his stable debut in a Wolverhampton novice (9.4f, AW) in December and followed up over this C&D (good) 16 days ago on his first handicap start; could continue to progress but does need another step forward today.
runners.comment: Made it 2-2 for Marco Botti with C&D handicap win; needs another step forward today

runners.name: Lyneham
runners.spotlight: Went close on debut at Doncaster (1m, soft) last September then kept on well to win a 1m maiden here (good) in October; this is a much stiffer test of his credentials but his dam is from a classy family and he's in excellent hands; entered in the Dante and Derby and could be set for big 3yo campaign.
runners.comment: 1m course maiden winner; in excellent hands and could be set for big 3yo campaign

runners.name: Ancient Egypt
runners.spotlight: Won fast-ground novice races at Beverley (7.4f) and Goodwood (1m) last summer on his first two starts; he's better than he showed when 14l seventh of eight in the Group 2 Royal Lodge here in September (1m, good to firm; 16-1) and has Dante/Derby entries but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: He's better than he showed in the Royal Lodge here but others have more pressing claims

races.race_name: Betfred "The Classic Bookmaker" Handicap

runners.name: St Anton
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on final two of his three 2yo starts (7f/1m, good to soft/good) and improved form on recent seasonal/handicap debut when close third over C&D (good); well regarded and open to further improvement; one to consider.
runners.comment: Close third in C&D handicap on reappearance and he's open to further improvement

runners.name: Lake Como
runners.spotlight: Won Newcastle novice (7f, AW) in December on fourth start and followed up in four-runner nursery at Southwell (1m, AW); returned from break with 50-1 third in warm conditions race at Kempton (1m, AW) in March, when conceding weight all round, and on that evidence he looks well treated off an unchanged mark; down the field on sole turf start but that was his debut last June.
runners.comment: Third in warm conditions race at Kempton and looks well treated off an unchanged mark

runners.name: Comic Hero
runners.spotlight: Went close in two fast-ground novice races last summer and returned with a novice win at Kempton (7f, AW) in February; 15-8 favourite and denied a clear run when 2l fifth of six on handicap debut at Musselburgh (1m, good to soft) four weeks ago; retains potential and is not ruled out but he lost places close home once in the clear at Musselburgh and has to raise his game.
runners.comment: Retains potential but lost places once in the clear at Musselburgh & has to raise his game

runners.name: Zennor Storm
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Kempton (7f, AW) last October and followed up there (1m, AW) in December; his opening mark is no gift but it remains to be seen where his limitations lie and he's respected for his top yard on turf debut.
runners.comment: 2-2, both AW; his opening mark is no gift but remains to be seen where his limitations lie

runners.name: Royal Bodyguard
runners.spotlight: Ran seven times last year and sole win came in four-runner nursery at Kempton (1m, AW); also effective on turf and gelded since last seen; however, unless he finds improvement he's vulnerable to less-exposed types.
runners.comment: One win (1m, AW) from his seven 2yo starts; looks vulnerable to less-exposed types

runners.name: Vincenzo Peruggia
runners.spotlight: Won at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm) last July on second start then well beaten in Group 2 at Goodwood (7f, good) later that month; reappeared with close second of six in handicap at Musselburgh (1m, good to soft) four weeks ago but it's likely he needs a much bigger run in this hot contest.
runners.comment: Reappeared with good second at Musselburgh but likely needs bigger run in this hot contest

runners.name: Fort Rock
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f on Kempton AW last autumn on his first two starts; those were narrow victories and improvement is needed on this handicap debut if he's to remain unbeaten, but he could have plenty more to offer for his top trainer, especially over this extra furlong.
runners.comment: 2-2, both narrow wins on AW, but could have plenty more to offer for his top trainer

runners.name: Objector
runners.spotlight: 3l win on debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in December and had 11l to spare when 1-8 there in January (8.6f); faces much tougher calibre of opponent on this first handicap start but he's brother to a Group 3 winner and his trainer is eyeing up a Royal Ascot handicap; could give a good account.
runners.comment: 2-2, easily in AW novices races; trainer eyeing up Royal Ascot handicap; could go well

runners.name: Elan d'Or
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at the eighth attempt in Chelmsford nursery (7f, AW) last November on his second AW start, and he again had plenty in hand when following up at Kempton (1m, AW); needs to transfer that improvement back to turf but he did show promise on grass last season; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Things very much clicked on AW at end of 2025; needs to transfer improvement back to turf

races.race_name: Betfred Jockey Club Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: Eydon
runners.spotlight: Had 1m4f wins last season in a Group 3 at Newbury (good to firm) in May and Group 2 in Italy (good; made all for career-best form) in October; tailed off in Hong Kong on final start but has a shout if back to his best.
runners.comment: Tailed off in Hong Kong on final start last term but has a shout if back to his best

runners.name: Sunway
runners.spotlight: Peak efforts were as a 3yo but reappearance at Meydan this January saw his first win since 2023; nearly won another 1m6f Group 3 there next time, before a headstrong display (set up big lead) saw him tail off in 2m Dubai Gold Cup five weeks ago; the latter was in blinkers (first time since September) which are unsurprisingly left off today; thereabouts if bouncing back.
runners.comment: Headstrong with blinkers over 2m latest start, after two solid 1m6f Group 3 runs at Meydan
runners.owner: Guy Pariente Holding & Qatar Racing Ltd

runners.name: Santorini Star
runners.spotlight: Upped to 2m for a handicap win at York Ebor meeting (good to firm) last August; tried front-running to positive effect against her own sex over about 1m6f on her subsequent two starts when winning the Group 2 Park Hill at Doncaster (good to soft) and second in Group 1 at Longchamp (very soft); has a form chance on this return but the stayer's mantle will have to be shed.
runners.comment: Progressive 2025; form chance on this return but the stayer's mantle will have to be shed

runners.name: Paradias
runners.spotlight: In the form of his life this winter with a win at Lingfield (1m4f) in February and close calls in his other three outings; those were on AW, which does seem to suit him particularly well, and also in handicaps, which leaves him with something to find in his first Group venture.
runners.comment: In the form of his life this winter but that was on AW and in handicaps; first Group race

runners.name: French Master
runners.spotlight: Won two 1m6f handicaps last term, latterly in taking style from off the pace at Royal Ascot (good to firm) when blinkered first time; slowly away on both subsequent starts, looking as if he was stretched by 2m in the Group 1 Goodwood Cup on first occasion (creditable) but tailed-off second favourite in the Ebor last time (visored; raced wide and made most); back to 1m4f, with headgear removed and needs overall improvement but the gelding operation may have helped.
runners.comment: Back down to 1m4f, headgear removed and needs overall improvement; gelding may have helped

runners.name: Lion's Pride
runners.spotlight: 7l Listed win over C&D (good to firm) last September; there are also plenty of lesser shows to be considered and, after he was not discredited in Qatar in February, he managed only fourth of six when favourite for Group 3 at Newbury 13 days ago; in the mix on peak efforts.
runners.comment: Plenty of lesser shows but in the mix on peak efforts, including 7l Listed win over C&D

races.race_name: Betfred "Nifty 50" Handicap

runners.name: Golden Redemption
runners.spotlight: Placed on all four handicap starts last year (7f/6f, good to firm/good), on the final occasion keeping on for 1l third here when the drop back to 6f looked against him; returned with solid third at Kempton (7f, AW) in March; major player for trainer who has won two of the last four runnings.
runners.comment: Admirably consistent in h'caps, including here; conditions should be right up his street

runners.name: Billyjoh
runners.spotlight: His losing sequence goes back 22 races to February 2024; however, he's often run well in the interim and he's been given a bit of help from the handicapper ahead of this reappearance; can be in the mix if fully tuned up.
runners.comment: On a long losing sequence but often runs well; can be in the mix if fully tuned up

runners.name: Elarak
runners.spotlight: Made it 3-6 when winning a C&D handicap (good to firm) last September, making virtually all; gelded subsequently; reappeared with close fourth of eight in conditions race at Thirsk (7f, good) three weeks ago and could show the benefit of that outing today; this 4yo is firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Won C&D h'cap last autumn; this lightly raced 4yo could build on his recent comeback run

runners.name: Benacre
runners.spotlight: Close third from the front at Musselburgh (7f, good) on April 12 and didn't lead when drawn wide and fifth of 11 there (7f, good) last Sunday; could give a good account if usual prominent tactics are applied but there's a suspicion he's vulnerable up in grade.
runners.comment: Close third at Musselburgh and drawn wide there since; might be vulnerable up in grade

runners.name: Silver Ghost
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good) last May on her second start of last season and followed up at Goodwood (7f, good) later that month; she failed to kick on subsequently but perhaps a break will have helped and she's not discounted on her reappearance.
runners.comment: Won two in a row last May (including C&D) but she failed to kick on subsequently

runners.name: Leadman
runners.spotlight: He has a modest strike-rate for one of his ability (2-19) and needs everything to drop right; however, he's well treated on last year's best efforts and may have needed his recent reappearance run at Thirsk; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Modest strike-rate for one of his ability but he's on a handy and is not ruled out

runners.name: Misunderstood
runners.spotlight: Won 1m 2yo Group 3 at Longchamp in 2024; reappeared with good third in Group 3 there last April; form went the wrong way subsequently and he changed hands for 37,000euros last November; gelded since; however, his new yard does extremely well with French recruits and this 4yo has an attractive mark to work with if tapping back into his historic promise; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Lost his form in France but new trainer does very well with French recruits; watch betting

races.race_name: Pertemps Network King Charles II Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Stellar Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Won in good style in a big field on nursery debut at York (7f, good to firm) last August and soft ground may not have suited in the Group 3 Horris Hill at Newbury in October; returned with commanding handicap win at Newbury (7f, good) a fortnight ago and holds leading form claims.
runners.comment: Reappeared with commanding handicap win at Newbury and has leading form claims

runners.name: Jel Pepper
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who had nearly 2l to spare when winning 26-runner sales race over C&D (good); held in fourth of seven on reappearance in Listed race at Newcastle (1m, AW) but could benefit from that run and the drop back in trip; no surprise to see a bold bid back on the Rowley Mile.
runners.comment: Won big-field sales race over C&D last October; no surprise to see a bold bid back here

runners.name: Ellusive Butterfly
runners.spotlight: 7f AW maiden winner who was fourth of 14 in the Group 3 Fred Darling at Newbury (7f, good) a fortnight ago; she's raced five times and others may have greater potential, while she could do with settling better, but she appears to be improving and might not be far away.
runners.comment: 4th in the Group 3 Fred Darling; could do with settling better but might not be far away

runners.name: Maximized
runners.spotlight: Won his first two starts last May/June then fifth in a pair of Group 2 races; twice placed at Meydan early this year but beaten about 6l when third of five in conditions race over this C&D (good) recently and Cerro Blanco may well be the yard's chief hope under Billy Loughnane.
runners.comment: Held in third of five over C&D recently and Cerro Blanco may well be the stable number one

runners.name: Cerro Blanco
runners.spotlight: Sent off the 4-9 favourite when winning on debut over C&D (good) 17 days ago; his dam was a Listed winner and he's entered in the St James's Palace Stakes; could have lots more to offer for leading stable; strong claims.
runners.comment: Odds-on favourite when winning over C&D on recent debut; very bright prospect

runners.name: Saber Strike
runners.spotlight: 230,000gns yearling who was backed into 5-2 when readily making a winning debut at Redcar (6f, good) last November; he's entered in the 6f Group 1 Commonwealth Cup but there's every chance he'll be suited by 7f and this highly likeable prospect is in good hands to progress; one to consider.
runners.comment: Readily made a winning debut at Redcar last November and he's a highly likeable prospect

races.race_name: Close Brothers Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Lady Roxby
runners.spotlight: Progressive last year, a campaign that culminated with a neck second of 11 over C&D (good); reappeared with solid third at Kempton (6f, AW) 25 days ago and could build on that effort today; respected.
runners.comment: Progressive last year and went close over C&D September; positive effort on reappearance

runners.name: Cuban Lady
runners.spotlight: Not seen to best effect last October on final 3yo start but she'd previous gone close twice in a row, on the first occasion on the other Newmarket course (6f, good to firm); reappears with Oisin Murphy booked and this lightly raced filly may have more to offer this season; possible contender.
runners.comment: Ran some solid races in handicaps last season and this 4yo remains lightly raced

runners.name: Mercury Day
runners.spotlight: Down the field at Kempton (6f, AW) 25 days ago but she was back from an absence and had a wide trip; close third on the other Newmarket course (6f, good to firm) last June on her final outing of last year, when tackling a sprint trip for the first time, and she's not written off.
runners.comment: Close third over 6f last June and remains unexposed at sprint trips; not written off

runners.name: Powdering
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent on AW this year but she was short-headed at Lingfield (7f) in February and denied a clear run when third at Southwell (6f) last time; has won here and she might not be far away if she's on a going day.
runners.comment: Inconsistent this year but ran well last time and this course winner might not be far away

runners.name: Lady Kodiac
runners.spotlight: Built upon last August's promising debut at Ripon (5f, good to firm) to post two novice wins at Chelmsford (6f, AW) in the autumn; only fifth of seven in conditions race at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) three weeks ago but may have needed that comeback run and she retains potential on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Underwhelming on reappearance but may have needed it and she was 2-3 previously

runners.name: Cinque Verde
runners.spotlight: Three wins last year and campaign concluded with good fourth of 11 over this C&D (good); down the field on reappearance at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in March but may have needed the run and is not ruled out.
runners.comment: Three wins last year as well as a good fourth over C&D; may have needed March comeback run

runners.name: Angel Love
runners.spotlight: Placed last September on first two 2yo starts (6f, AW) and easily made all in a maiden at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in November when odds-on; brings potential to this handicap/turf debut and could have a part to play.
runners.comment: Easily made all in AW maiden last November and brings potential to this handicap debut

runners.name: Slay Queen
runners.spotlight: Won Newbury novice (6f, good) last August on third start and followed up in three-runner nursery at Goodwood (6f, heavy); faced some tough tasks subsequently and she reverts to a handicap for her reappearance in a race her trainer won last year; this 3yo could give a good account.
runners.comment: Two 2yo wins last August/September and faced some tough tasks after; now back in handicap

runners.name: Paradise Walk
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on all three starts (7f/6f, AW), most recently at Wolverhampton in March following an 82-day break; improvement is needed off her opening mark but there's potential in her pedigree and she's in the right hands to progress; wears first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Runner-up on all three starts; improvement needed but she's in the right hands to progress

runners.name: Lightning Polka
runners.spotlight: Looked good when winning on debut at Haydock (6f, good) last September; didn't kick on subsequently and came third of four on handicap debut at Lingfield (6f, AW) in November last time, but maybe a break will have helped this half-sister to 6f Group 3 winner Light Refrain; yard in fine form.
runners.comment: Looked good on debut last September; didn't kick on but maybe a break will have helped
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Anaisa
runners.spotlight: She ran 11 times last year and won twice, including Listed race at Ripon last August (6f, good; modest form for the grade); reappeared with 2l fourth of seven against males in handicap at Pontefract (6f, good) ten days ago and she has an each-way chance if building on that.
runners.comment: 2yo Listed winner (modest form for the grade); respectable comeback run; each-way chance

runners.name: Fantasy Obsessor
runners.spotlight: 4yo who won last summer on her first two starts (6f/7f, AW); four lesser runs followed last year (turf/AW), however, and there was no return to form on her recent reappearance at Southwell (6f, AW).
runners.comment: 4yo who won last summer on her first two starts but she's been underwhelming since

runners.name: Orchid
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-3 on AW when winning on handicap/seasonal debut at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) last April; then off until the autumn, when running some respectable races on turf; has to better that form to win off her current mark but this 4yo remains lightly raced and is not discounted.
runners.comment: 2-3 on AW; 0-4 on turf but some respectable runs and remains lightly raced; not discounted

races.race_name: Ascot Shop Royal Ascot Two-Year-Old Trial EBF Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Adaay Of Scarlett
runners.spotlight: Mehmas colt who cost 110,000euros as a yearling; strong in the betting (11-8) and strong in the finish when completely dominating a 5f novice at Newmarket's Craven meeting (5f, good); Royal Ascot was talked about afterwards and this colt looks well above average already.
runners.comment: Strong in the betting when dominating a 5f novice at Newmarket's Craven meeting

runners.name: A Bear Affair
runners.spotlight: 9-2 chance when fending off Dance A Jig by just under a length in the Brocklesby at Doncaster (5f, good to soft); showed a good attitude and the Hannon youngsters often improve with racing, but he does have to concede 3lb to the runner-up whose effort was a tad more eyecatching.
runners.comment: 9-2 chance when fending off Dance A Jig by just under a length in the Brocklesby
runners.owner: A Bit On The Side & Partner

runners.name: Dance A Jig
runners.spotlight: Home-bred 2yo who hails from a well-established sprinting family; got behind early in the Brocklesby (5f, good to soft; 6-1) but made up a significant amount of ground to give A Bear Affair something to think about; should be all the wiser this time and he's 3lb better off with the winner.
runners.comment: 3lb better off with A Bear Affair on their Doncaster clash; should be wiser this time

races.race_name: Longines Sagaro Stakes (Group 3) (A Gold Cup Trial)

runners.name: Sweet William
runners.spotlight: Headgear and Rab Havlin all starts; not straightforward but he nearly always gives his running, including at this track; he did not have the top stayers to worry about last September when winning his second Doncaster Cup and the same seems to apply today, so he's a leading player.
runners.comment: Nearly always gives his running, Ascot included; does not have top stayers to worry about

runners.name: Caballo De Mar
runners.spotlight: Much improved last year, best effort to land the Group 1 Cadran at Longchamp (2m4f, soft) in October; close second in the Dubai Gold Cup at Meydan (2m, good) was a fine start to this campaign and his run on good to firm at Royal Ascot last June, although subsequently eclipsed, looked excellent at the time; needs respect but concedes weight all round.
runners.comment: Group 1 Cadran winner and close second in Dubai Gold Cup, but he concedes weight all round

runners.name: Tabletalk
runners.spotlight: No win since Melrose Handicap at York (1m6f, good to firm) in 2024 but he ran very well in 1m4f Group races on first three starts last season and returned to that form when a close second in Saudi Arabia (1m7f) 11 weeks ago; that puts him back in the picture even though he's unraced over this far.
runners.comment: No win since 2024; returned to form when close second in Saudi Arabia (1m7f) 11 weeks ago

runners.name: Tarriance
runners.spotlight: Won the Melrose Handicap at York (1m6f, good to firm) last August, before the St Leger proved too tough; gelded soon after and ran well when third in Saudi Arabia (1m7f) in February; runner-up Tabletalk conceded him 11lb that day and it's only 1lb today, but this 4yo should retain potential for 2m+.
runners.comment: Held by Tabletalk on Saudi Arabia form latest but this 4yo should retain potential for 2m+

runners.name: Ascending
runners.spotlight: Landed the Cork Derby (1m4f) last May and a Royal Ascot handicap (2m4f) last June, before his excellent second in the Ebor Handicap at York (1m6f) in August; not disgraced on final start but needs a serious advance to mix it with the best of these in today's Group 3.
runners.comment: Fine season in 2025 handicaps; serious advance needed against best rivals in this Group 3

runners.name: Miss Alpilles
runners.spotlight: Won 1m6f handicap at Goodwood (good to firm) last August and a first crack at 2m suited her even better when she led over 1f out to score at 20-1 in a Listed race at Newmarket (good) in September; today's Group 3 should demand plenty more but she could still have untapped potential at 2m+.
runners.comment: First crack at 2m was 20-1 win in a Listed race at Newmarket in September; potential still

runners.name: Deira Mile
runners.spotlight: A novice event remains his only win from ten races but he was fourth in the 2024 Derby and St Leger; well held in Saudi Arabia and Dubai on his only two starts last year, left Owen Burrows for 16,000gns last October and gelded last month; some caution has to be advised.
runners.comment: 4th in the 2024 Derby and St Leger; left O Burrows for 16,000gns last October and gelded

runners.name: Dubai Future
runners.spotlight: Has delivered smart efforts almost every season since 2020 (missed 2024) and last year's win in the 2m Dubai Gold Cup at Meydan was one of his best; that campaign went the wrong way and he did not perk up when returning for Dubai Gold Cup (20-1) five weeks ago, but holds a cracking chance if returning to his best.
runners.comment: 2025 went wrong way; below form in Dubai Gold Cup five weeks ago but won it last year

races.race_name: Commonwealth Cup Trial Stakes (Registered As The Pavilion Stakes) (Group 3)

runners.name: Wise Approach
runners.spotlight: On the up when last seen, rounding off his 2yo campaign with a comfortable come-from-behind win in the Middle Park in September (6f, good to firm); may confirm his superiority over Brussels and Coppull despite the weights turnaround and he is not fully exposed just yet.
runners.comment: Picked up well to win the Middle Park when last seen; penalised but not fully exposed

runners.name: Brussels
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f maiden at the Curragh on debut before a string of good efforts in defeat in good races, notably second-place finishes in the Middle Park (0.75l behind Wise Approach and 5lb better off) and the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint; should be in the shake-up.
runners.comment: Twice 2nd in Group 1 company as a 2yo; should be in the shake-up under suitable conditions

runners.name: Ardisia
runners.spotlight: Progressive as a 2yo, winning five times and also second of 11 in the 2yo race over C&D on Champions Day; should appreciate the return to a stiff 6f having weakened over 7f in the Greenham two weeks ago; one to consider.
runners.comment: Five wins as a 2yo; more exposed than his rivals but still likely to give a good account

runners.name: Division
runners.spotlight: Promise on good to firm on debut; won his next three starts, two novice events and a York Listed race, before pulling his chance away over C&D on Champions Day; improvement required now upped to a Group 3 but he's in excellent hands and still has some potential.
runners.comment: Pulled his chance away over C&D last October; had won 3 times beforehand, including Listed

runners.name: Ghost Mode
runners.spotlight: Five runs as a 2yo; made debut over 6f but campaigned at 7f afterwards, running away with a maiden at Chester (good to soft) before a good second in a York sales race last October; absent for five months prior to a facile success in a 6f handicap at Southwell in March, racing freely and hanging left in the straight but in a different class to some fair handicappers; in much deeper today and looks to face a stiff task at the weights but the Southwell performance smacked of a hugely talented, if slightly wayward, colt and this stiff 6f should suit; unraced on faster than good.
runners.comment: Left 2yo efforts behind with striking, if erratic, Southwell win in March; unexposed at 6f

runners.name: Coppull
runners.spotlight: Third in the Coventry Stakes over C&D before winning the Group 2 Richmond Stakes at Glorious Goodwood (6f, good); rounded off his juvenile campaign with a close third in the Group 1 Middle Park Stakes (6f, good to firm), finishing on the heels of the reopposing Wise Approach and Brussels; stable won this race with Jasour, also a Group 2 winner as a 2yo, in 2024; one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Group 2 winner as a 2yo; good third behind two of these in the Middle Park when last seen

runners.name: Crown Relic
runners.spotlight: All races at 7f, winning a maiden and a novice at Wolverhampton in good style before his creditable fifth in a Deauville Group 3 last month (good to soft); drops in trip with a tongue-tie added for a trainer who this in 2020 and 2023.
runners.comment: Two 7f AW wins before a good 5th in a French Group 3; down in trip with tongue-tie added

runners.name: Midnight Tango
runners.spotlight: Ran well in Listed/Group 3 company last summer following her maiden win at Hamilton and her two lesser efforts at the end of the campaign can both be excused; this free-going filly shouldn't mind the return to sprinting but she will need to raise her game a notch.
runners.comment: Useful 2yo whose final two runs last year can both be excused; others bring stronger form

races.race_name: Memberships At Ascot Paradise Stakes (Listed Race) (A Queen Anne Stakes Trial)

runners.name: Jonquil
runners.spotlight: 2025 seemed to establish that he's better over 1m than 6f or 7f and the best of those efforts, including at the top level, give him a good chance in today's company; reappearance in Abu Dhabi in February was not of the same order but he could bounce back.
runners.comment: Abu Dhabi show in February was not of the same order but best 1m efforts give good chance

runners.name: Holloway Boy
runners.spotlight: His strike-rate is an unimpressive 3-26 but he's seldom far away and last October's excellent second in a top handicap over C&D preceded an AW Listed win; came up short at Doha and Meydan early this year but showed enough to suggest he should be on the premises.
runners.comment: Strike-rate is unimpressive 3-26 but seldom far away; excellent 2nd in a top C&D handicap
runners.owner: N White, S White, T White & E Burke

runners.name: La Botte
runners.spotlight: Went close in the Britannia at Royal Ascot (1m, good to firm) last June on his fourth start; not seen again until a good fourth at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) this March, despite a slow start and having to come very wide in the straight; disappointing favourite in the Lincoln at Doncaster three weeks later, perhaps with the good to soft ground against him but overall improvement is required moving from handicaps into this Group 3.
runners.comment: Good to soft ground perhaps against him in the Lincoln but this is a move from handicaps
runners.owner: Halo Racing N Greenhalgh J Degenhardt

runners.name: Rashabar
runners.spotlight: The 2024 Coventry at Royal Ascot as 2yo remains his only win; runner-up to Jonquil on 2025 reappearance and ran creditably at 1m/1m2f on most subsequent starts, before a fruitless go at 1m4f at the Breeders' Cup in USA; each-way chance if he's tuned up but yard is not yet off the mark this term.
runners.comment: Each-way chance if he's tuned up but yard is not yet off the mark this term

runners.name: Bowmark
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo from top yard; headstrong up front in the Group 1 Prix du Jockey Club (10.5f) last June and also failed to settle when held up in York Group 3 (1m1f) last August on his only subsequent start; returns to 1m for reappearance but whether he's calmed down after being gelded may be the key.
runners.comment: Headstrong in his two Group races last term; returns to 1m, having been gelded

runners.name: Seacruiser
runners.spotlight: Looked a good prospect when making all in a 1m Newmarket maiden (good to soft) in September 2024; reappeared only three weeks ago but made eye-catching late gains to finish a close third in that strong conditions stakes at Thirsk (7f, good; 12-1); this race demands far more but return to 1m (he looks bred for further still) will help and he should be capable of better.
runners.comment: This race demands far more but his recent return from a long absence was promising

runners.name: Remmooz
runners.spotlight: Group 3 Jersey at Royal Ascot last June was the only time he's failed to improve his rating in six-race career but even then he managed fourth of 15; close third in handicap at York Ebor meeting (1m, good to firm) preceded win number four in a 7f Listed race at Newbury; while better is needed in this Group 3, he looks the type to find it in this second season.
runners.comment: While better is needed in this Group 3, he looks the type to find it in this second season

runners.name: Naqeeb
runners.spotlight: Has raced as far as 2m1f in 2024 and never shorter than 1m2f; third at last year's Royal Ascot came over 1m4f but he broke a losing sequence when diverted from top handicaps to a 1m2f Listed race at Goodwood last September; the prospect of 1m now doesn't seem so ridiculous but a career best is needed.
runners.comment: Diverted from top handicaps for a 1m2f Listed win at Goodwood last September

races.race_name: Naas Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Combustion
runners.spotlight: His first three starts saw him well beaten over 7f but he has not looked back since dropped to 6f, winning seven times and placed on his other three runs; could only manage second at Chelmsford five weeks ago but the horse to beat him is now rated 17lb higher and that shouldn't be used as evidence he is in the handicapper's grip; more to prove back on turf and at a new trip but few arrive in better form.
runners.comment: Prolific since dropped to 6f; latest 2nd well franked by the winner; turf/5f the 2 queries

runners.name: Rage Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Not without his quirks but he has plenty of ability for one of his rating and everything clicked in a Class 6 handicap at Salisbury last June (6f, good to firm); three second placings followed and he should win more races this year; the drop back to 5f for this habitual slow-starter is a concern though.
runners.comment: Quirky but talented; can win races off this mark but 5f a concern for this slow-starter

runners.name: Pixie Diva
runners.spotlight: Narrow Newcastle win on her penultimate start; did best of the rest behind an emphatic all-the-way winner at Yarmouth three weeks ago (5f, good); this race should be more conventional and she is more appealing than many.
runners.comment: AW win in March and ran well in defeat at Yarmouth last month; one for the shortlist

runners.name: Hockney
runners.spotlight: Down the field in three maiden/novice runs but he proved a different proposition on his handicap debut last September (6f, AW; first run after wind surgery), landing a gamble in emphatic fashion; followed up, again easily, in a selling handicap at Thirsk one week later; came up short off his revised mark at Newcastle five weeks ago (first run for 193 days); better than that but dropping to 5f asks a new question of him.
runners.comment: Two easy 6f wins last September; down the field on return five weeks ago; now drops to 5f

runners.name: El Bufalo
runners.spotlight: Had a troubled trip when down the field at Wolverhampton in February but he had been running well beforehand, including a couple of AW wins; others appeal as being better handicapped.
runners.comment: Two AW wins this winter but others look better handicapped in this field

runners.name: Existent
runners.spotlight: Ended a lengthy losing run at Southwell in January and he is just 1lb higher today; solid effort at Newmarket 17 days ago when not ideally positioned; each-way claims at a track where he has run well in much better races than this one.
runners.comment: Ended losing run in January (5f AW); fair run at Newmarket latest; each-way shout

runners.name: Sam's Hope
runners.spotlight: The winner of five AW handicaps (5f-6f) since September for her new stable; 6lb higher than for the latest of them and others appeal as being better treated.
runners.comment: Five AW wins for this yard since September; high in the weights as a result
runners.owner: D Morgan And K J Parris

runners.name: Far Above The Law
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on his best form for Alice Haynes and he made an encouraging stable debut when a close third at Southwell three weeks ago; one to be interested in.
runners.comment: Good mark on 3yo best; promising stable debut at Southwell last month; shortlisted

runners.name: Mesaafi
runners.spotlight: Ran a fine race when third over C&D last July and he turns up here off an 8lb lower mark; poorly drawn when down the field at Lingfield on Good Friday; could bounce back with a big run.
runners.comment: Placed off 8lb higher over C&D last summer; poor draw at Lingfield latest; not ruled out

runners.name: Lethal Nymph
runners.spotlight: Not handicapped out of things and his latest run at Pontefract, his first start for 12 weeks, was creditable; twice a course winner and likely to make a bold bid.
runners.comment: Two-time course winner; fair mark on recent best; should give a good account

runners.name: Another Abbot
runners.spotlight: Effective at 5f but last summer's two wins came over 6f; not beaten far at Chelmsford (5f) when last seen in December; still has relatively low mileage.
runners.comment: Effective at 5f but his two wins have come over an extra furlong; still has low mileage

runners.name: Artagnan
runners.spotlight: Two solid placed efforts have followed his AW win (6f; dead-heated) at Chelmsford last October; still has relatively low mileage and a promising claimer has been booked; first 5f run since his 2yo days.
runners.comment: Three good runs since tongue tie was fitted; drops to 5f for the first time since 2yo days

runners.name: Duran
runners.spotlight: Second in this race last year before running out the ready winner of a 5f AW handicap at Haydock last May (good to firm) off a 1lb lower mark; made an encouraging return to action at Beverley 16 days ago; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Second in this race last year off 1lb lower; encouraging reappearance; each-way shout
runners.owner: D Caslin, P Wilcock & M Cantillon

runners.name: All Ways Glamorous
runners.spotlight: Second of 16 in a C&D handicap last September; followed that with a good run at Haydock but he went up the weights and was well beaten at Lingfield when last seen; ran well after an absence last year but perhaps a shade high in the weights for this.
runners.comment: Ran well in one course run last year but he's now 5lb higher than for that second place

runners.name: Gallant
runners.spotlight: Both wins and most of his best RPRs have come on AW but his turf runs have come in stronger company than this; drops to 5f for the first time in his career; others look safer.
runners.comment: Capable off this mark but the drop to 5f asks a new question of him

runners.name: King's Call
runners.spotlight: Ran quite well when an unconsidered 100-1 shot at Royal Ascot last summer but that didn't prove a springboard to better things; down in the weights but best watched back from a 245-day break.
runners.comment: Generally struggled for this yard last summer; down in the weights but not  a solid option

runners.name: Emperor Spirit
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut for this yard at Newmarket last July (5f, good to firm) and he is now 3lb lower; patchy record since but he can go well fresh and he has a good C&D run on his record.
runners.comment: Conditions no bother and well handicapped on 2025 best; can go well fresh

races.race_name: Darley British EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: So Regal
runners.spotlight: Top-class pedigree and holds a Coronation Stakes entry; clear promise on debut and again went off market leader when making no mistake in a 1m maiden at Newmarket's Craven meeting (good), emerging from off the pace to quicken past Lyrics Of Life under no more than hands and heels encouragement from Ryan Moore; penalised accordingly but she's highly promising.
runners.comment: Penalised for her ready maiden win at Newmarket but she looks highly promising

runners.name: Lyrics Of Life
runners.spotlight: Quality 2yo form on all sorts of ground, as her seconds where behind smart horses and she wasn't beaten too far in a French Group 3; she did have the run of things up front on her Newmarket return (1m maiden, good) but was still unable to contain the less exposed So Regal; a 6lb pull with the winner could make it interesting.
runners.comment: No match for So Regal at Newmarket but a 6lb pull with the winner may make it interesting

runners.name: Machadadorp
runners.spotlight: Mehmas filly and the second foal out of a Group-placed 1m winner; bred to have a future but she'd need to be decidedly useful to take this on debut.
runners.comment: Bred to have a future but she'd need to be decidedly useful to take this on debut

runners.name: Light Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to five winners; defied market weakness to go close in a 7f Kempton maiden in early April and was reportedly struck into when well held at Newbury (1m2f); this is no easy opportunity even with no excuses this time.
runners.comment: Struck into at Newbury latest but this looks no straightforward opportunity

runners.name: Gesayed
runners.spotlight: Debut winner over 6f at Hamilton (good to soft); failed to justify a shade off odds-on when penalised at Southwell (7f AW) and that was disappointing, for all that a very slow pace might not have helped; bred to improve for this longer trip.
runners.comment: Debut winner over 6f; they went very steady when a beaten favourite over 7f

runners.name: Annastarzy
runners.spotlight: Confined to 1m last season and probably ran her best race last time out when fourth behind a very promising filly from the Haggas yard; seems sure to win races this term but this might not be one of them unless she can take a step forward.
runners.comment: Sufficient promise last season to believe she'll be winning soon; this looks tough, though

runners.name: Crownright
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly; 15-2 for November's debut at Kempton (1m) where she kept on nicely once getting the hang of things in a race won by 1,000 Guineas entrant Abashiri; trainer is wasting no time in turning to headgear but brings potential into her second season.
runners.comment: Promising third in November; brings potential into her second season; hood added

races.race_name: Ascot Racecourse Supports Debra Manny Mercer Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Storm Star
runners.spotlight: All four wins have been on the AW at Newcastle, including a hat-trick early last year; back on his last winning mark and he did chase a strong pace last time (in a race he won 12 months earlier) before folding late on; lightly raced on turf.
runners.comment: Not fired this year on AW, though a feasible excuse last time; lightly raced on turf

runners.name: Dosman
runners.spotlight: Only 2-16 but his third trainer has him in career-best form and didn't get the breaks when a closing fourth at Newcastle last time (1m); hold-up performer who needs a good pace to chase and if getting one he'll be dangerous.
runners.comment: Hold-up performer who needs a good pace to chase and if getting one he'll be dangerous

runners.name: Greek Order
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in three successive quality 1m handicaps last summer, including the Royal Hunt Cup here (good to firm); however, that campaign rather petered out and he was only midfield in the Lincoln a month ago (tried a visor); he's still dangerous to dismiss off this mark in a lower grade.
runners.comment: Below par last four races but he's down in grade here off a potentially nice mark

runners.name: Joolianoss
runners.spotlight: Won three handicaps on the AW in January 2025 (1m-1m2f) and showed that he's still on a fair mark with a second at Kempton on final run this January; however, this is tougher than he's accustomed to and has to prove himself on turf.
runners.comment: This is tougher than he's accustomed to and has to prove himself on turf

runners.name: Dapper Guest
runners.spotlight: Effective in the reapplied cheekpieces but last season's career-best form came once fitted in a visor; needs to be right on his game to win this and that wasn't the case on his Newmarket comeback.
runners.comment: Needs to be right on his game to win this and that wasn't the case at Newmarket

runners.name: Herculeus
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in two races since winning off 7lb lower at Newbury last August (1m; in first-time cheekpieces which are now absent); might be high enough in the ratings even if all the better for being gelded; absent since October.
runners.comment: Might be high enough in the ratings even if all the better for being gelded

runners.name: Angel Hunter
runners.spotlight: Behind Dosman at Southwell when tried in new blinkers two runs ago but left that form behind with a narrow verdict at Lingfield (1m), which seemed unlikely from where he was turning in; only 3lb higher and he's a C&D winner.
runners.comment: C&D winner who did well to win at Lingfield from where he was; 3lb higher now

runners.name: Saytarr
runners.spotlight: Five AW wins include this year's hat-trick on the Tapeta; raised a further 4lb for what was a narrow latest success (1m) but he's on a roll and no doubt a better horse now than when last seen on turf.
runners.comment: Progressive AW profile and no doubt a better horse now than when last seen on turf

runners.name: Tilted Kilt
runners.spotlight: Went 0-14 after his winning debut at two but belatedly doubled his tally when comfortably accounting for his five rivals after a break at Southwell (1m); this is tougher, but he was runner-up in last year's Silver Bowl at Haydock which was a Class 2 handicap on turf; only 3lb higher than that day.
runners.comment: Impressed on the AW last time and this tougher scenario won't faze him; respected

runners.name: Dingle
runners.spotlight: Had the run of things when winning over Newcastle's 1m in January and has since finished behind both Dosman and Angel Hunter; goes to work today off his 8lb lower turf mark but that's because he's 0-15 on grass.
runners.comment: Goes to work today off his 8lb lower turf mark but that's because he's 0-15 on grass

runners.name: Colombier
runners.spotlight: Won four times in France (7f-1m2f), twice in the Juddmonte silks and twice in these; absent since finishing ninth in Qatar in early 2025 so his wellbeing is a complete guessing game; best to let the betting guide; has form on soft and fast ground.
runners.comment: Four wins in France; off since early 2025 so best to let the betting guide

races.race_name: Racing To School Celebrating 25 Years EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Qual) (C/D) (GBB/IRE Incentive)

runners.name: Harry Knows
runners.spotlight: 17-2, shaped promisingly on his debut when beaten a head in nine-runner maiden at Newbury (5f, good) two weeks ago, finishing well having been hampered at the start; this son of Mehmas has a fair bit more to offer so rates a big player.
runners.comment: Promising debut second at Newbury, finishing well after hampered start; big player
runners.owner: Thames Boys, J Henwood & J Clay

runners.name: Rollthedicebaby
runners.spotlight: £38,000 yearling; Soldier's Call filly; dam, 7f AW winner (RPR 60), is a sister to 6f/7f winner Emily Post and a half-sister to 8.6f-1m4f winner Dubai Bounty (dam of smart sprinter Kachy); appeals on paper and must enter calculations for her in-form stable.
runners.comment: Soldier's Call filly; appeals on paper so must enter calculations for her in-form stable

runners.name: Armor Supreme
runners.spotlight: 4,000euros yearling; Armor filly; half-sister to four French winners, notably Daimyo (6.5f-1m; RPR 96) and Bijin (1m; 93); dam, 1m/1m2f winner (81), is a half-sister to 1m 2yo Group 3 winner Fantastic View; the betting can prove an accurate indicator.
runners.comment: Armor filly; half-sister to four French winners; the betting can prove an accurate guide

runners.name: Ballisty
runners.spotlight: 35,000euros foal, £32,000 yearling; Lucky Vega colt; dam, French 1m winner (RPR 78), is out of a French Listed-placed 7f winner; stable is going well so he's a much respected newcomer.
runners.comment: Lucky Vega colt; stable is going well so he's a much respected newcomer

runners.name: Moriarty Moon
runners.spotlight: 35,000gns yearling; Starspangledbanner colt; half-brother to four winners, notably She Do (6f Listed; RPR 104) and Sydney Street (6.5f-1m; 94); dam, US 1m winner, is a half-sister to Listed-placed 7.5f winner; the market can guide.
runners.comment: Starspangledbanner colt who is a half-brother to four winners but the market can guide

runners.name: Super Tuscan
runners.spotlight: 22,000gns yearling; half-brother to 7f winner Monfrid (RPR 85); dam, Group-placed 7f-8.3f winner (109), is out of useful 5f-7.5f winning half-sister to 1m1f Grade 2/1m2f Group 2 winner Triple Threat; yard took this 12 months ago so this son of Dark Angel is very much one to consider.
runners.comment: Yard took this 12 months ago so this son of Dark Angel is very much one to consider

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs John Dunlop EBF Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Pacific Avenue
runners.spotlight: A useful 7f/1m winner at 2yrs and he has acquitted himself well in Qatar and Meydan over the winter, runner-up in 1m Listed event in February; he holds solid form claims.
runners.comment: Useful 7f/1m winner; good runner-up in 1m Meydan Listed event in February; solid claims

runners.name: Allegresse
runners.spotlight: Looked on the up when making all in a pair of 1m Kempton novices in the autumn but he failed to get home when beating only one in Zetland Stakes at Newmarket (1m2f) on final 2yo run; since gelded and it would come as no surprise to see this son of Oasis Dream bounce back.
runners.comment: Won two Kempton novices but beat just one in Zetland at Newmarket final run; can rebound

runners.name: Pathein
runners.spotlight: Son of St Mark's Basilica who created a good impression when making a winning start in 1m Doncaster maiden in September; built on that form when fifth of seven in Newmarket's Autumn Stakes (1m) following month, fading only late on; big shout with better still to come.
runners.comment: Debut Doncaster winner before excellent fifth in Newmarket's Autumn Stakes; big shout

runners.name: Protection Act
runners.spotlight: 5-1, impressed when going in at the first time of asking in novice at Haydock (7f, good) last July, showing a good turn of foot; off since but this son of Starspangledbanner remains with plenty of potential.
runners.comment: Impressive debut winner at Haydock in July; off since but remains with lots of potential

races.race_name: Fitzdares Official Partner Of Season Opener Handicap

runners.name: Rosario
runners.spotlight: Captured this prize last year and largely in good form after, despite his customary slow starts; should strip sharper for his reappearance sixth in 5f Newmarket handicap 17 days ago so he's no forlorn hope in refitted cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Took this last year; solid sixth at Newmarket on return so he's no forlorn hope

runners.name: Lexington Blitz
runners.spotlight: Gained a deserved success at Catterick (5f) in October and resumed from five months off with a good third of 14 in 5f Musselburgh handicap 27 days ago; a likely player off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Resumed from five months off with good third at Musselburgh; likely player off same mark

runners.name: Clearpoint
runners.spotlight: Completed a 5f AW hat-trick last backend and has continued in fine form this spring, runner-up in 5f Southwell handicap last time; must enter calculations back on turf.
runners.comment: In fine form this spring, runner-up at Southwell latest; enters calculations back on turf

runners.name: Rhythm N Hooves
runners.spotlight: Notched three 5f handicaps in 2025 and he has continued in decent form this season, fifth of 14 at Musselburgh last month; ought to be thereabouts once more off an easing mark.
runners.comment: In decent form, fifth of 14 at Musselburgh latest; ought to be thereabouts once more

runners.name: Tropical Storm
runners.spotlight: Bagged 5f York listed event last May; off seven months/gelded before posting an encouraging eighth of 14 in 5f Musselburgh handicap 27 days ago, nearest finish having lost ground when hampered soon after start; not without interest off a reduced mark.
runners.comment: Encouraging return at eighth at Musselburgh given hampered start; not without interest

runners.name: Spartan Arrow
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed an excellent 2025 when a four-time scorer, including in 5f Group 3 at Dundalk in October; yet to fire this term and failed to beat a rival in 5f Pontefract handicap last month; tried in a change of headgear here.
runners.comment: A four-time scorer in 2025 but yet to fire this term; tried in a change of headgear here
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Ltd XXXI And Partner

runners.name: Trefor
runners.spotlight: A three-time winner (around 6f) last season but he ended the term with a rare poor effort in the Portland at Doncaster in September; has gone well fresh though so no surprise to see him bounce back here.
runners.comment: A three-time winner (around 6f) last term; has gone well fresh so in the mix on return
runners.owner: Julie Martin & David R Martin & Partner

runners.name: Democracy Dilemma
runners.spotlight: Useful front-runner who returned with a good third in 5f AW listed event at Lingfield in February; not at his best on soft ground at Musselburgh since but can bounce back here.
runners.comment: Good Lingfield listed third; not at best at Musselburgh (soft) since but can bounce back

runners.name: Fandom
runners.spotlight: A useful 5f winner in the US in 2024; failed to fire for Joseph O'Brien subsequently and twice finished runner-up for Olly Murphy in Saint Moritz in February; starts out for another new yard here.
runners.comment: A useful 5f US winner in 2024; twice second for Olly Murphy in Saint Moritz in February

runners.name: Rogue Enforcer
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful 5f winner at 2yrs for Peter Chapple-Hyam; also scored three times in France/Italy in 2025 (signed off with 5f Listed success in Milan); since gelded and he can't be ruled out now starting out with Archie Watson.
runners.comment: Scored three times in France/Italy in 2025; gelded and not ruled out on debut for new yard

runners.name: King Of Light
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 better than ever (cheekpieces on and tongue tied) with Haydock victory and fourth at Ascot (both 5f); yard is going well so he's very much one to consider on his return.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 better than ever; yard is going well so very much one to consider on his return

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Daisy Warwick Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Crepe Suzette
runners.spotlight: Improved by the run last season and ended with an excellent third of 11 in Doncaster's Group 2 Park Hill Stakes in September; yard took this 12 months ago and she's very much the one to beat.
runners.comment: Ended season with excellent third in Doncaster's Park Hill; very much the one to beat

runners.name: Galaxy Star
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced filly who showed fair winning form in bumpers before making a successful Flat debut in 1m3f maiden at Kempton in March; capable of better but this demands considerable improvement.
runners.comment: Made a successful Flat debut in Kempton maiden; considerably more is needed however

runners.name: Incensed
runners.spotlight: Underwent breathing surgery before making an encouraging return from six months off when runner-up in 1m4f Kempton handicap last month; this fairly useful 1m4f winner can take a step forward now.
runners.comment: Encouraging return with runner-up at Kempton; can take a step forward now

runners.name: Ryka
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed an excellent 2025 and she signed off with a 1m4f listed success at Toulouse in October; remains quite low mileage, so can do better still this season.
runners.comment: Signed off for 2025 with listed Toulouse win; remains quite low mileage, so can do better

runners.name: Coedana
runners.spotlight: Proved most progressive last season when a dual 1m4f AW handicap scorer in the autumn; steps up in grade here but she's capable of further improvement.
runners.comment: Proved very progressive last season; steps up in grade but capable of further improvement
runners.owner: Julie And David R Martin And Dan Hall

runners.name: Kissmehoneyhoney
runners.spotlight: Crystal Ocean filly who looked a good prospect when making a winning start in 11-runner maiden at Leicester (1m2f, good to soft) in September; takes a big step up in grade but this longer trip promises to suit her well, so she shouldn't be passed over at all lightly for leading yard.
runners.comment: Looked good prospect with Leicester debut win; shouldn't be passed over at all lightly

runners.name: Tattycoram
runners.spotlight: Largely progressive sort (1m2f winner) last season but she wasn't seen out after posting just a fair fifth of seven in 1m6f Newmarket handicap in August; this demands a clear personal best after eight months off the track.
runners.comment: Largely progressive last season; this demands a clear personal best after eight months off

runners.name: Miss Wong
runners.spotlight: Looked firmly on the up when landing a pair of handicaps (around 1m3f) at Kempton and Yarmouth last autumn but she ended 2025 on a low note when tailed off in the St Simon Stakes at Newbury; this is no easy reappearance.
runners.comment: Tailed off in St Simon Stakes at Newbury on her final run; this is no easy reappearance

runners.name: Crystal Flyer
runners.spotlight: Winless in 2025 for Tom Clover but she largely performed with credit; changed hands for 70,000 guineas subsequently but this C&D scorer faces no easy task on her seasonal/yard debut.
runners.comment: Winless in 2025 for Tom Clover; this C&D scorer faces no easy task on seasonal/yard debut
runners.owner: Castle Racing Co Ltd & Mps Racing Ltd

races.race_name: Fitzdares Offers Top Prices At Goodwood Handicap

runners.name: Uncle Don
runners.spotlight: A fair 5f scorer for Richard Fahey; made a promising start for Robert Cowell after four months off when second of 12 in 6f Lingfield handicap four weeks ago; up 3lb but he must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Promising start for Robert Cowell with Lingfield second latest; must enter calculations

runners.name: Many A Star
runners.spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with all-the-way 6f Wolverhampton win in February; up 2lb but this veteran C&D winner can't be discounted after a break.
runners.comment: Kickstarted 2026 with all-the-way 6f Wolverhampton win in February; not discounted

runners.name: Evening Saigon
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who got back on the up when making a winning return in 6f Newcastle handicap 18 days ago; up 3lb but had a bit in hand there and merits serious consideration.
runners.comment: Back on the up when scoring at Newcastle latest; up 3lb but merits serious consideration

runners.name: Kiss And Run
runners.spotlight: Course scorer who largely ran well without winning in 2025; she has a good record fresh too, so needs considering on her seasonal return.
runners.comment: Course scorer who largely ran well without winning in 2025; has a good record fresh too

runners.name: Dr Strangelove
runners.spotlight: Gelded and off six months before a never-dangerous fourth of five in 6f Lingfield handicap 11 days ago; this dual 6f winner in 2025 can take a step forward now.
runners.comment: Never-dangerous fourth of five at Lingfield 11 days ago; he can take a step forward now

runners.name: Spring Bloom
runners.spotlight: On a long losing sequence but he made a good start for his new yard (formerly with Darryll Holland) when second of nine in 6f Leicester handicap 18 days ago; more is needed from 3lb out of the handicap here though.
runners.comment: Good start for new yard with second at Leicester 18 days ago; 3lb out of handicap here

runners.name: Diligently
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 on the up and he returned from seven months off (gelded) with an encouraging fourth of 13 in 6f handicap at Newmarket 16 days ago; well in the mix off the same mark.
runners.comment: Back from seven months off with encouraging Newmarket fourth; well in mix off same mark

runners.name: Far Above Dream
runners.spotlight: A three-time winner for James Owen in 2025, signing off with a second C&D success in October; has a career-high mark to overcome on his seasonal return but not taken at all lightly.
runners.comment: Signed off with C&D success in October; career-high mark but not taken at all lightly

runners.name: Moving Force
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful 5f juvenile winner for Richard Fahey; changed hands for 17,000 guineas in January 2025 and he has his fitness to prove after 19 months off (also gelded) for his new yard here.
runners.comment: A fairly useful 5f winner for Richard Fahey; has his fitness to prove after 19 months off

races.race_name: Fitzdares Dedicated Personal Betting Service Handicap

runners.name: Lux Aeterna
runners.spotlight: Much improved after three months off (also gelded) when making a winning start in handicaps at Wolverhampton (9.5f) last month, quickening clear impressively off a steady pace; up 6lb but he remains one to be very interested in on his turf debut.
runners.comment: Impressed with winning handicap start at Wolverhampton; one to be very interested in

runners.name: Morbeh
runners.spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with success at Lingfield (1m2f) in January and backed it up with a very good fourth of eight in 1m2f Doncaster handicap five weeks ago; enters calculations off the same mark with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Won at Lingfield before a very good Doncaster fourth last time; enters calculations

runners.name: Foothold
runners.spotlight: Without a win but got back on track in a first-time hood when fourth of 11 in 1m Bath handicap two weeks ago; he's no forlorn hope on his first go over 1m2f with headgear retained.
runners.comment: Back on track in a hood with Bath fourth latest; no forlorn hope stepped up to 1m2f

runners.name: Too Hot To Tango
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but she's proving a reliable sort and comes here on the back of a good third of nine in 9.5f Wolverhampton handicap in March; handicap debutante.
runners.comment: 0-8 but reliable and good third in Wolverhampton handicap in March; handicap debutante

runners.name: Kokoluna
runners.spotlight: Ulysses filly who took a big step forward on her handicap debut when scoring at Nottingham (1m) last month; showed a good attitude there, so she's not taken lightly despite a 5lb weights rise.
runners.comment: Big step forward when scoring at Nottingham last month; up 5lb but still not taken lightly

runners.name: Veritable
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of his three runs as 2yo, cheekpieces retained when runner-up in 1m3f Kempton maiden in December; subsequently gelded and this son of Dubawi can do better still on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Runner-up in Kempton maiden in December; gelded since; can do better on h'cap debut now

runners.name: Bridge Of Eagles
runners.spotlight: Failed to build on earlier promise when sixth of 13 in 1m Kempton maiden last autumn; has since been gelded and he remains with potential now going into handicaps.
runners.comment: Failed to build on earlier promise when Kempton sixth last autumn; remains with potential
runners.owner: C & L Washbourn And Mrs S Woodward

races.race_name: Track Radio Launches On Monday Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Diamond Bay
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner who stays well but has failed to beat a rival in his last two races, with and without a visor; now tries blinkers.
runners.comment: Multiple winner who stays well but has failed to beat a rival in his last two races

runners.name: Fair Dinkum
runners.spotlight: Four wins divided between turf and the AW; capable on his day and this trip on this ground is fine, but he's always had two ways of running; well below his best on last two outings.
runners.comment: He's always had two ways of running and well below his best on last two outings

runners.name: Peking Opera
runners.spotlight: Listed Flat winner for Aidan O'Brien and initially looked a good prospect for hurdling; however, has struggled badly under both codes since returning from an absence.
runners.comment: Formerly smart but has struggled badly under both codes since returning from an absence
runners.owner: S Packham, A Austin And B Austin

runners.name: Market House
runners.spotlight: Running well on the AW in just a tongue-tie and has continued the good work over hurdles, finishing second (cheekpieces added) before a win 12 days ago (first-time visor); should have a part to play here.
runners.comment: Had been running well on the Flat before a second and first over hurdles

runners.name: Autumn's Breeze
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back at Lingfield last August (2m/1m5f; good) after the cheekpieces went on but the last of those wins was by 12l and she's struggled since, presumably because of her new mark; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Hasn't looked well handicapped since winning by 12l at Lingfield last August

runners.name: Golden Garden
runners.spotlight: 1m6f AW winner for Jamie Osborne but returns to this scene after two heavy defeats over hurdles, the latest after a wind operation; has a bit to prove off this mark even if he's back on song.
runners.comment: Has a bit to prove off this mark even if he's back on song after two defeats over hurdles

runners.name: Arcturus Flame
runners.spotlight: 8lb higher than for his only handicap win gained nearly a year ago; kept on for a closing fifth at Newbury last time but that was over 1m2f and there are doubts about him over this far.
runners.comment: Fair last run but was over 1m2f and there are doubts about him over this far

runners.name: Gallivanted
runners.spotlight: Progressive juvenile hurdler this winter and returned to the Flat with a close third at Thirsk (1m4f, good); had no excuses but looks sure to win more races in this scene.
runners.comment: Progressive juvenile hurdler and returned to the Flat with a close third at Thirsk (1m4f)

runners.name: Scarlet Moon
runners.spotlight: The last of four Flat wins was over this trip at Sandown last summer (good; 5lb lower); has since racked up a solid sequence over hurdles; in form, consistent and respected.
runners.comment: Last Flat win was over this trip at Sandown last summer; solid over hurdles since

runners.name: Cruden
runners.spotlight: Flat/hurdle winner Jessica Harrington and ran well in defeat over hurdles for James Ewart; beaten 4l (1m6f) and 6l (2m) in two AW races for current outfit, the latest in early January; others safer.
runners.comment: Successful in Ireland; beaten 4l (1m6f) and 6l (2m) in two AW races for current outfit

races.race_name: Stanley Asphalt Hunters Chase for the Bishopscourt Cup

runners.name: Last Round
runners.spotlight: Won soft-ground point early last year; fair placed form in a pair of hunter chases on good ground last season, one of them won by Asphalt Cowboy; career-best when a 10l winner of a maiden hunter chase at Limerick last Christmas; sights lowered now after hunter chase runs at Thurles and Naas.
runners.comment: Has run in good hunter chases since 10l maiden win at Limerick, solid place prospect

runners.name: Squire Ohara
runners.spotlight: Form has gone downhill since second of four finishers behind Fountain House in this race 12 months ago; saddle slipped when rider was unseated last time; previously last of six behind Hearts And Spades at Tyrella.
runners.comment: Form had gone downhill since second of four finishers in this race 12 months ago

runners.name: Asphalt Cowboy
runners.spotlight: Winner of two hunter chases on good ground over longer distances at Downpatrick; placed in three points since last October; has struggled in hunter chases at Down Royal and Fairyhouse, and concerning that he was pulled up in this race last year when a 5-1 chance; blinkered.
runners.comment: Placed in three points this term, has struggled in hunter chases, pulled up here last year

runners.name: Irina's Path
runners.spotlight: Twice placed in maiden hurdles in 2024; weak form last year; good enough to win a recent maiden point at Castletown-Geoghegan, but the form does not amount to much.
runners.comment: Twice placed in maiden hurdles in 2024, did not have much to beat in a recent maiden point

runners.name: Jetbob
runners.spotlight: Looked quite promising in points last season; below that standard this term; two poor runs since third here in February, when 16l behind runner-up Heartbreak Weather; same connections as 2024 winner Private Ryan.
runners.comment: Quite promising in points last season, below that standard this term, yard had 2024 winner

runners.name: Clonsast
runners.spotlight: Has completed only once from six starts in points; remote sixth behind Dacman on latest; can be ruled out.
runners.comment: Has completed only once from six starts in points, can be ruled out

runners.name: Heartbreak Weather
runners.spotlight: Respectable form in points this season, notably when second to the subsequent Ladies' Cup winner Fountain House in a trial for this event here; in touch when falling three out at Ballycahane; made a couple of slight mistakes on the way to Monksgrange win.
runners.comment: Respectable form in points this season, including a second to the smart Fountain House

runners.name: Hearts And Spades
runners.spotlight: Outstanding form in points this term, winning six from seven; no disgrace in neck Portrush defeat to Winged Leader, the most prolific Irish points winner ever (37 wins); raced a bit too keenly when only third (beaten favourite) on hunter chase debut at Cork; can repeat stable's 2025 win with the talented Fountain House, an easy winner under Rob James here on Tuesday.
runners.comment: Excellent form in points this term, too keen in a hunter chase at Cork, looks the part now

runners.name: Gethegoodtimesroll
runners.spotlight: Weak form over hurdles and in points; remote fifth behind Heartbreak Weather at Monksgrange in March; the trainer of the 2018 winner also runs Hey Sir.
runners.comment: Weak form over hurdles and in points, poor fifth behind Heartbreak Weather at Monksgrange

runners.name: Dacman
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in a bumper two years ago on only start for Willie Mullins; has made gradual improvement in points; opened his account at Tattersalls Farm after remote third behind Heartbreak Weather at Monksgrange; more on his plate now.
runners.comment: Recent point winner, previously a remote third behind Heartbreak Weather at Monksgrange

runners.name: Hey Sir
runners.spotlight: Trainer won this with Alpha Male in 2018, but this one appears to have little chance; pulled up in this race last year and finished behind three of these rivals in a hunt race here two months ago.
runners.comment: Pulled up in this race last year, behind three of these rivals in a hunt race this season

runners.name: Fr Gilligansvoyge
runners.spotlight: Sprang a 25-1 surprise in this race in 2023, his only success from 67 appearances on the track, placed eight times; moderate fifth here in 2024 and pulled up in last year's edition; should be better for a recent point run, but up against it at the age of 13.
runners.comment: Sprang a 25-1 surprise in this event in 2023, pulled up in last year's race, up against it

runners.name: Keenaghan Lass
runners.spotlight: Two wins in points early in her career; three-figure odds when pulled up here 12 months ago; remote fourth in a recent point.
runners.comment: Two wins in points early in her career, three-figure odds when pulled up here last year

races.race_name: Uniquely Champion Hunters Chase

runners.name: Its On The Line
runners.spotlight: Won this for the third time last year; contrasting profile in the Cheltenham equivalent now having finished second in four consecutive years; easily the best of the Irish this year, with Panda Boy fifth and Wonderwall (his conqueror last year) seventh; never got competitive in the Aintree Foxhunter, a rare blot on his record; has his quirks, but as tough as they come.
runners.comment: Three-time winner of this, four-time Cheltenham runner-up, had a rare lapse at Aintree

runners.name: Asian Master
runners.spotlight: Promising early in his career for this stable and initially did well for Willie Mullins, winning twice over hurdles and taking fourth in the Supreme Novices'; also placed in good company over fences; has prospered again in points this season; Journey With Me got a better jump at the last to beat him in a hunter chase at Cork last month.
runners.comment: Has prospered in points this season, gave another solid display in a hunter chase at Cork

runners.name: Panda Boy
runners.spotlight: Smart staying handicap chaser in his prime; has found a new lease of life, winning two hunter chases at Thurles (beat Lifetime Ambition by 9l) and Naas (beat Hunters Yarn by 4l); might have found the ground a bit too quick when finishing fifth behind Barton Snow at Cheltenham; could play a featuring part.
runners.comment: Has beaten Lifetime Ambition and Hunters Yarn this term, not quite at best at Cheltenham

runners.name: Wonderwall
runners.spotlight: Has run only three times since beating Its On The Line at Cheltenham last season; winner of points at Dromahane in November and 19 days ago, that recent display providing reassurance after a disappointingly lifeless Cheltenham defence; leading contender for the stable that has dominated the point-to-point scene this season.
runners.comment: 2025 Cheltenham winner from Its On The Line, eclipsed this year, still a prime contender

runners.name: Lets Go Champ
runners.spotlight: Won a competitive handicap chase at the 2024 festival for Henry de Bromhead (2m4f, good to yielding); hunter chase victory over Hunters Yarn at Gowran Park showed that he retains considerable ability; confirmed that impression when beating all bar star hunter Barton Snow at Aintree; goes well for Sophie Carter.
runners.comment: Fine effort beating all bar top-class Barton Snow at Aintree, goes well for Sophie Carter

runners.name: Cause For Comment
runners.spotlight: Twice runner-up in hunter chases last season; beaten favourite both times; beat a subsequent winner handily in a maiden point in February; had little trouble justifying favouritism in a maiden hunter chase at Downpatrick; smart long-term prospect, this may come a year too soon.
runners.comment: Emphatic winner of a maiden hunter chase at Downpatrick, smart long-term prospect

runners.name: Lifetime Ambition
runners.spotlight: 7-14 in points and a five-time winner on the track; has often run well in races at this level, notably when second to Its On The line in this race in 2024 and third in the 2025 Aintree Foxhunter; no match for Panda Boy at Thurles in January; readily outpointed by Wonderwall at Dromahane 19 days ago
runners.comment: No match for Panda Boy at Thurles; readily outpointed by Wonderwall at Dromahane recently
runners.owner: Linda Mulcahy & Exors Late Roger O'Byrne

runners.name: Wrappedupinmay
runners.spotlight: Four wins in five races in points, the defeat inflicted by Hunters Yarn at Moig South last autumn; that rival seemed to be getting the upper hand when they both fell three out in the Fairyhouse race won by Five Star Getaway; useful second-string for champion-elect points trainer.
runners.comment: Four wins in five races in points, useful second-string for champion-elect points trainer

runners.name: Dora d'Or
runners.spotlight: Four wins from five starts in points; won a Down Royal hunter chase in March; big step up in class now.
runners.comment: Four from five in points, won a Down Royal hunter chase in March, big step up in class now

runners.name: Big Interest
runners.spotlight: Looked a smart prospect when beating Its On The Line at Down Royal in December 2024; disappointing overall since then, except for an 18l Fairyhouse win last season; remote third in the same race this year; poor completion rate this season; risky.
runners.comment: Beat Its On The Line at Down Royal in 2024 has become unreliable, risky proposition

runners.name: Five Star Getaway
runners.spotlight: In the form of his life when winning four races over fences for Christian Williams in 2021; had been running well enough in points before profiting from the mishaps of fancied rivals to win at Fairyhouse.
runners.comment: Profited from the mishaps of fancied rivals to win at Fairyhouse, unlikely to repeat that

runners.name: Temptationinmilan
runners.spotlight: More than three years since his last racecourse win; pulled up in the hunter chase won by Panda Boy at Thurles in January; held by Asian Master on Cork running.
runners.comment: More than three years since his last racecourse win, no appeal in a race of this standard

runners.name: Ambitious Fellow
