runners.spotlight: Four wins over hurdles; has never won a chase; point-to-point form nowhere near good enough.
runners.comment: Four wins over hurdles, has never won a chase, form in points nowhere near good enough

runners.name: Now Where Or When
runners.spotlight: Veteran has been competing quite effectively in points; however, could manage only a moderate fourth in an incident-packed Fairyhouse race won by Five Star Getaway.
runners.comment: Moderate fourth in an incident-packed Fairyhouse race won by Five Star Getaway

races.race_name: Hanlon Concrete Irish EBF Glencarraig Lady Francis Flood Mares Chase (Grade 2)

runners.name: Dinoblue
runners.spotlight: High-class mare has won eleven of her 20 starts over fences, including this race last year; as good as ever this season and won the Mares' Chase at Cheltenham on latest (Only By Night runner-up, Spindleberry fourth); top-rated here and best treated by race conditions; the one to beat.
runners.comment: High-class, took this last year, won at Cheltenham latest, best treated by race conditions

runners.name: Only By Night
runners.spotlight: Last year's Arkle runner-up is 4-8 over fences; won a Naas Grade 3 on her seasonal return in November; disappointed in a Grade 1 at Leopardstown a month later but back on song when chasing Dinoblue home in the Mares' Chase at Cheltenham; big player again here.
runners.comment: 2025 Arkle runner up, chased home Dinoblue at Cheltenham, big player again here

runners.name: Spindleberry
runners.spotlight: Won first five chase starts, including two Listed mares' chases and a Grade 1 at Fairyhouse in April last year; came unstuck upped to 3m in the Irish Gold Cup and no match for Dinoblue or Only By Night at Cheltenham latest; badly in at the weights here and looks up against it on these terms.
runners.comment: Won first five chases, limitations seemingly exposed last twice, badly in at weights here

runners.name: Kala Conti
runners.spotlight: Talented hurdler made a bright start to her chasing career, winning two mares' chases at Cork (beat Kargese easily in the second one); fine second in the Grade 1 Scilly Isles at Sandown behind Sixmilebridge but took a heavy fall when moving into contention at Aintree last time; if that hasn't left a mark she could go well, though she's not well treated by race conditions.
runners.comment: Two chase wins at Cork, second in Scilly Isles, heavy fall latest, could go well

runners.name: Sophia Rose
runners.spotlight: Sligo maiden hurdle winner is 0-6 over fences and rated 103; out of her depth in this company.
runners.comment: 0-6 over fences and rated 103, out of her depth in this company

runners.name: Broomfield Bijou
runners.spotlight: 1-12 over fences and rated 125; hasn't won since scoring in a mares' chase at Wexford in July 2024; difficult to see her losing run ending in this company.
runners.comment: 1-12 over fences, rated 125, no win since July 2024, this looks too demanding

races.race_name: QuinnBet Novice Handicap Chase (Listed Race)

runners.name: Noble Park
runners.spotlight: British runner; commenced chase career with good-ground wins at Hereford and Lingfield; below par in two subsequent starts but bounced back to form with a 4.25l win at Newbury last time (2m4f, good to soft) and a few of those in behind have won since; up 7lb now but ground and distance look like his optimum.
runners.comment: Added third chase win at Newbury and a few of those behind have win since; chance

runners.name: He's Gorgeous
runners.spotlight: Has taken well to chasing this term, winning a 2m2f Clonmel beginners' (heavy) and good late headway for fourth behind Romeo Coolio in a Leopardstown Grade 1 (2m1f, yielding) over Christmas; good attitude when runner-up in a Down Royal novice chase in January (2m1f, yielding to soft); okay run when fifth in a Listed Naas handicap (2m4f, heavy) when last seen.
runners.comment: Has taken well to chasing this term inc' one win; okay fifth in a Naas Listed h'cap latest

runners.name: Kiss Will
runners.spotlight: One of eight for trainer; dual winning hurdler is yet to win from five starts over fences, but he looked like the one who would get closest to easy Irish Grand National (3m5f, yielding to soft) winner Soldier In Milan before taking a heavy fall three out; interesting if that hasn't left a mark, but concerning that Townend abandons ship.
runners.comment: Running a good race in Irish Grand National before falling, however, Townend abandons ship

runners.name: Soldierofthestorm
runners.spotlight: British runner is a three-time hurdles winner who has taken well to fences this season; built on positive chase debut when scoring from the front at Sandown (2m4f, soft) in March; not given an easy lead at Newton Abbott and jumping lacked fluency when last of the four runners (2m5f, good); easy lead could be difficult to find once again in this big field.
runners.comment: Front-runner has taken well to fences but jumped poorly last time; wants an easy lead

runners.name: Wingmen
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; yet to win over fences after five starts, but some positive efforts behind quality opposition, including when 0.75l behind Funiculi Funicula at Cork last time (2m1f, yielding) under this rider; place chance.
runners.comment: 0.75l behind Funiculi Funicula at Cork last time; place chance.

runners.name: Yoradreamer
runners.spotlight: One of eight for trainer; maiden hurdle winner built on a good runner-up finish at Gowran on his third chase start to win a four-runner Wexford beginners' chase (3m1f, soft) at his leisure last time; that form probably doesn't amount to much, but he's not ruled out now entering handicaps.
runners.comment: Form of easy beginners' chase win last time doesn't amount to much, but not ruled out

runners.name: Norn Iron
runners.spotlight: British runner; hurdles winner is now rated 22lb higher over fences after back-to-back wins on soft ground over 2m4f at Sandown; decisive victor of five-runner contest in January and justified 4-6 odds last month when seeing off two rivals; this big field is new for him, but he's in fine form.
runners.comment: Back-to-back winner of small field Sandown races last twice; this big field is new for him

runners.name: Kish Bank
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; point/hurdle/bumper winner was a beaten favourite in two beginners' chases before making immediate impact on handicap chase debut at Leopardstown (3m1f, yielding) off 118; never travelling when pulled up next time at Naas (2m4f, heavy); back on track when run down late on at Navan (3m, soft to heavy); up 4lb for that effort but in the mix.
runners.comment: Debut h'cap chase winner; bounced back from poor run when runner-up last time; in the mix

runners.name: Al Kalila
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner built on previous chasing promise with a 2l win at Kelso in February (2m5f, good to soft) in a four-runner field; this is a different challenge altogether and needs to improve again in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Winner of four-runner h'cap at Kelso; this is a different challenge and needs to improve

runners.name: Jet To Vegas
runners.spotlight: Unsuccessful in three chase runs in first half of the season, including when well beaten on handicap debut at Musselburgh (2m5f, good to soft, 9-4 favourite); reverted to hurdles with a poor effort in the Morebattle after wind surgery but better effort when sixth at Aintree last time (2m4f, good to soft); returns to chasing now.
runners.comment: 0-3 over fences; returns to chasing after a creditable sixth at Aintree over timber

runners.name: I Am Lorenzo
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler won his chasing debut at Fairyhouse (2m, soft) in January, staying on well; soundly beaten over 2m1f here next time (heavy); gave 5lb to Raglan Road when 7l behind that rival at Down Royal last time (2m5f, good to yielding); more needed.
runners.comment: Debut chase winner was 7l behind Raglan Road last time; more needed

runners.name: Shuffle The Deck
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner last season on good-to-yielding; safely held in quality beginners' chases; 3-1 favourite for handicap chase debut at Fairyhouse (2m1f, soft) last time when kept on for third, 11l behind the winner; step up in trip should help, as should this better ground; chance.
runners.comment: Third on h'cap chase debut latest; step up in trip and better ground in his favour; chance

runners.name: Funiculi Funicula
runners.spotlight: The fact Townend picks him from the stable's eight runners is a very big positive; maiden hurdle winner placed in Grade 1 novice company at Aintree last term (2m4f, good); beaten favourite in opening two chase runs before making the breakthrough at Cork (2m1f, yielding) last time, when defeating Wingmen by 0.75l; big player with further improvement plausible.
runners.comment: Choice of Townend from stable's eight; could improve again over beginners' win last time

runners.name: Raglan Road
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner was soundly beaten in opening three chase runs; well backed when winning a Down Royal rated novice chase last time (2m5f, good to yielding); likely capable of more.
runners.comment: Well backed to win rated novice chase last time; likely capable of more

runners.name: Ol Man Dingle
runners.spotlight: Three-time winning hurdler in 2024; immediate winner over fences at Galway (2m2f, yielding) before following up in a Cork Grade 3 novice (2m4f, good to yielding) next time, with You Oughta Know 2.75l behind in second; poor effort in Grade 1 company in November at Fairyhouse; never on terms after a bad mistake at the fifth at Cheltenham when last seen; tough task conceding weight all round.
runners.comment: Back-to-back chase wins at start of season inc' Gr.3; tough task conceding weight to all

runners.name: Anyway
runners.spotlight: Second in last season's Jack Richards Handicap Chase at Cheltenham (2m5f, good to soft, 125-1); won a Kilbeggan beginners' chase in June, 2.25l in front of You Oughta Know; left two disappointing efforts behind when a creditable fourth of seven at Cheltenham in October (3m1f, good); back chasing after moderate hurdles run last time and Kennedy rides for the first time.
runners.comment: Second in last season's Jack Richards; needs to bounce back from poor hurdles run

runners.name: Ballygunner Castle
runners.spotlight: One of eight for trainer; dual bumper and maiden hurdle winner in 2024; runner-up on chase debut at this venue (2m7f, heavy) before falling four out back here next time; failed to justify 5-6 favouritism at Naas (2m4f, soft to heavy) last time when a 0.5l second behind Spread Boss Ted; now enters handicap chase company.
runners.comment: Close second behind Spread Boss Ted in a beginners' last time; h'cap chase debutant

runners.name: Port Joulain
runners.spotlight: One of eight for trainer; point and bumper winner two seasons ago and won his maiden hurdle here early last year (2m4f, heavy); much improved display, compared to poor opening chase start here, when successful at Fairyhouse in January (2m1f, yielding), beating a better-fancied stablemate; had the run of things when third of five in a Navan Grade 3 novice last time.
runners.comment: Fairyhouse beginners' winner had no excuses when third in a Navan Grade 3 latest

runners.name: Karamoja
runners.spotlight: One of eight for trainer; Grade 1-placed over hurdles and off the mark over fences at the third attempt when successful at Thurles in March (2m2f, soft), making all for 12l win; creditable fourth in a 2m1f Fairyhouse handicap last time; step up in trip should suit.
runners.comment: Easy Thurles beginners' winner; creditable fourth in a h'cap last time; this trip may suit

runners.name: Park Of Kings
runners.spotlight: First reserve; three-time hurdles winner has been beaten a long way in five chase starts this season, including in novice handicaps the last twice; unseated at the fourth at Wexford last time; 10lb lower mark in this sphere and has won on good ground, so there are positives if returning to form.
runners.comment: Reserve; beaten a long way in five chase starts but decent mark if returning to form

runners.name: You Oughta Know
runners.spotlight: One of eight for trainer; made all to win 2m4f Wexford novice chase last summer but hit and miss since, best effort when a 2.75l runner-up behind Ol Man Dingle in a Cork Grade 3 novice in November; bounced back from two poor runs in big-field handicaps when second of four runners in a Limerick Grade 3 novice last time; big fields may not be his thing.
runners.comment: Winner of four-runner novice chase last summer; big fields may not be his thing.

runners.name: Spread Boss Ted
runners.spotlight: One of eight for trainer; bumper and dual winning hurdler had no chance when a faller on chasing debut at Navan in January (3m, soft to heavy) and was a moderate fourth of seven here next time (2m7f, heavy); much better when seeing off better-fancied stablemate Ballygunner Castle at Naas by 0.5l last time (2m4f, soft to heavy); in the mix on handicap chase debut.
runners.comment: Saw off Ballygunner Castle on third chase start last time; in the mix on h'cap chase debut

races.race_name: Alanna Homes Champion Novice Hurdle (Grade 1)

runners.name: King Rasko Grey
runners.spotlight: Didn't win a bumper but has proved a much better hurdler; comfortably landed a Limerick maiden hurdle in December; close third in 2m Grade 1 novice at the DRF in February; stepped up in trip and won the Turners in impressive fashion at Cheltenham; preferred by Townend and the one to beat.
runners.comment: Won the Turners in impressive fashion on latest, the pick of Townend, the one to beat

runners.name: Jalon d'Oudairies
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point and dual bumper winner; off the mark over hurdles on penultimate start when making all for a dominant win at Naas (18.5f, sft-hvy); 3m may have stretched him when well beaten in the Albert Bartlett last time; may do better dropped back in trip; place claims.
runners.comment: Dual bumper and maiden hurdle winner, 3m too far latest, place chance back in trip

runners.name: Lord Byron
runners.spotlight: British raider; 4yo is 0-5 over hurdles but far from disgraced the last twice when a 3.5l fifth of 20 in the Triumph at Cheltenham (2m1f, soft) and a 6.5l fourth of 12 in the Grade 1 Mersey Novices' Hurdle at Aintree; gets weight all round and rated 141, so definite place chance on the figures.
runners.comment: 0-5 over hurdles, creditable Gr'1 efforts last twice, place chance on the figures

runners.name: Echoing Silence
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point and bumper winner; won first two starts over timber, a Cork mares' maiden hurdle (2m4f, yielding) and a Listed mares' hurdle here (2m3f, heavy); mid-field in Grade 2 mares' novice at Cheltenham on latest (2m1f, good); return to further should suit and gets weight from the boys; could challenge for a place.
runners.comment: Two-time hurdle winner, trip possibly too sharp at Cheltenham, place shout

runners.name: You Proof
runners.spotlight: Runner up in sole point-to-point; off the mark on hurdles' debut at Limerick in December (21.5f, sft-hvy); soundly beaten in Grade 2 novice at Fairyhouse (2m4f, yld-sft) on latest; early days still but new tongue-tie needs to eke out lots more.
runners.comment: Limerick maiden winner, well beaten in Gr 2 latest, tongue tie needs to eke out more

runners.name: Bon Viveur
runners.spotlight: Tramore maiden hurdle winner sprang a surprise when winning a four-runner Thurles Grade 3 novice (21.5f, yld-sft) in February; that form has taken a few knocks since, however, and Paul Townend has jumped ship; more needed up in grade.
runners.comment: Sprang a surprise in Thurles Gr' 3 latest, Townend deserts, more needed up in grade

runners.name: Saint Baco
runners.spotlight: French recruit won a Navan maiden hurdle (2m, sft-hvy) on return from long absence in December; has come up well short in three starts since; beaten 26l in a Grade 1 at Naas; beaten when falling at the last in the Turners; beaten 20l in Fairyhouse Grade 2 latest; much more needed.
runners.comment: Maiden winner has come up short since stepped up in class, much more needed

runners.name: Kiely's Place
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner won on his Rules' debut in a Clonmel maiden hurdle (19.5f, sft-hvy) in February; sent off at 11-2 but made no impression upped in grade at Fairyhouse on latest (20.5f, yld-sft); needs to improve plenty on that, but he's lightly raced so that's not impossible; hood goes on.
runners.comment: Point and maiden hurdle winner, beaten in Gr 2 latest, needs to improve, hooded

races.race_name: Boodles Champion Hurdle (Grade 1)

runners.name: Lossiemouth
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; prolific and highly talented mare justified decision to line up in this year's Champion Hurdle when adding a tenth Grade 1 win to her tally (14-18 overall) in good style; had both Anzadam and Golden Ace well behind her on that occasion; 3-3 at this venue, including when 19l in front of Glen Kiln in this season's Morgiana Hurdle; this looks easier than Cheltenham and she's ground-versatile; shock result if she doesn't win.
runners.comment: Comfortable winner of the Champion Hurdle and a shock if she doesn't follow up here

runners.name: Golden Ace
runners.spotlight: Fortuitous winner of the 2025 Champion Hurdle (same story in this season's Fighting Fifth); could only manage fifth when attempting to retain her crown (9.75l behind Lossiemouth); posted another solid effort in the Aintree Hurdle last time when fourth (2m4f, good to soft); return to 2m should suit, cheekpieces go on and she was second in this contest last year (good) but may need further luck to increase Grade 1 haul.
runners.comment: Consistent mare but well behind Lossiemouth in attempt to retain Champion Hurdle crown

runners.name: Wilful
runners.spotlight: Progressive 7yo won a competitive Ascot handicap in December (15.5f, good to soft) and was second in a valuable Windsor handicap next time (2m, soft); posted career-best effort in the County Hurdle last time, keeping on well to win by three lengths (2m1f, soft); has course form after filling the second spot in a festival handicap here last season; enters Grade 1 company now and will need a good fist of improvement again, but he's clearly thriving.
runners.comment: Career best when winning County Hurdle latest; needs to improve again in Gr.1 company

runners.name: Absurde
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; dual-purpose sort who has run creditably in the last three Melbourne Cups (rated 107 on the Flat); 2024 County Hurdle winner was well below his best in that race this season back from a break (2m1f, soft); improved display at Cork when 3.25l behind Ndaawi (16.5f, yielding); a good bit to find with Lossiemouth even if running to his rating, which he hasn't done so far this term.
runners.comment: Classy dual-purpose sort hasn't been at his best in two runs this term; a bit to find

runners.name: Anzadam
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; hopes were high that he could develop into a Champion Hurdle contender following a brace of stylish Group 3 wins in his opening two starts for this yard; lost unbeaten record since upped to Grade 1 level; beaten by Golden Ace in the Fighting Fifth, then only fourth twice at Leopardstown; made a race-ending mistake at the second flight in the Champion Hurdle before running on late to finish seventh of nine (18l behind Lossiemouth); place-chance if putting it all together.
runners.comment: This season hasn't gone to plan, but he holds a place-chance if putting it all together

runners.name: Glen Kiln
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a standout season, highlighted by landing Grade 3 honours at Naas in January (2m, heavy); has been found out upped to Grade 1 level twice this term, 19l behind Lossiemouth in the Morgiana Hurdle (16.5f, soft to heavy) and no chance when unseated at the last in the Hatton's Grace (2m4f, yielding to soft); below his best in a Fairyhouse Grade 2 last time; hard to see him being involved.
runners.comment: Enjoying a standout season, but has been found out at Grade 1 level; hard to fancy

runners.name: William Munny
runners.spotlight: Unseen since taking the runner-up spot behind Kopek Des Bordes in last season's Supreme Novices' Hurdle (2m, good to soft); that was just his fourth hurdles start after placing in his opening two maiden hurdles at Leopardstown and Fairyhouse and comfortably winning a Listed novice hurdle (soft) over this C&D before his Cheltenham run; contender if running to his best but it would be a huge performance to win this after 416 days off.
runners.comment: Unseen since second in last term's Supreme Novices'; huge performance needed given absence

races.race_name: FRS Fencing Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Chichester Park
runners.spotlight: Winner of an ordinary Thurles maiden hurdle in December (2m, yielding); outpaced on handicap debut at same venue before satisfactory third in a Leopardstown novice hurdle next time (2m, soft to heavy); didn't improve for step up in trip in Down Royal handicap last time (2m5f, yielding); point-to-point win came on good ground.
runners.comment: M'den hurdle winner needs to improve over his two h'cap starts

runners.name: Arslan
runners.spotlight: Placed form in maiden hurdles before winning a weak Thurles contest in March (2m1f, soft to heavy) under positive tactics; in contrast to his win, was ridden cold for Fairyhouse handicap debut (2m, yielding) but never near to challenge despite keeping on well for seventh; worth another chance off the same mark.
runners.comment: Tactics change compared to when winning didn't work on h'cap debut; worth another chance

runners.name: Saveforarainyday
runners.spotlight: Well backed to win third maiden hurdle start following the addition of a hood at Limerick (2m, heavy); won impressively that day, but the headgear was left off on handicap debut at Fairyhouse when well beaten (2m, yielding to soft); hood goes back on now and Mark Walsh stays loyal.
runners.comment: Easy m'den hurdle winner in hood but left off when poor on h'cap bow latest, back on now

runners.name: Pioneer Pete
runners.spotlight: Authoritative Wetherby maiden winner (2m, good) last month on his fifth start over hurdles; needs to back it up now entering handicap company; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Wide-margin m'den hurdle winner on fifth hurdles start; needs to back it up now in a h'cap

runners.name: Theflyingbee
runners.spotlight: Added a second hurdling win at Downpatrick last June off 2lb lower (2m3f, yielding to soft), but recent runs had gotten progressively worse until return to form of sorts at Bellewstown last month when an 8.5l third (2m1f, yielding).
runners.comment: Now 2lb above last winning mark last June; okay effort at Bellewstown 13 days ago

runners.name: Withabitofluk
runners.spotlight: Debut Bellewstown bumper winner in 2024 (2m1f, good to yielding); showed promise in a couple of maidens last season but returned from a 380-day absence for his first handicap at Fairyhouse (2m, yielding to soft) and was tailed off; may do better with that run under his belt; cheekpieces on.
runners.comment: Bumper winner showed promise in maidens but tailed off on h'cap debut last time; headgear

runners.name: Avalo
runners.spotlight: Placed five times in a row last summer/autumn on goodish ground up to 2m4f, including in big fields; sequence ended when tailed off at Cheltenham in November (2m5f, soft); place chance if her consistent form returns.
runners.comment: Placed five times in a row in summer/autumn before poor run when last seen in Nov'
runners.owner: Seamus Perry & Brian P Larkin & Ms Bernadette Leigh

runners.name: Spirou
runners.spotlight: French hurdle winner surprised his trainer when winning on stable debut back from an 852-day absence at Newbury in March (2m1f, good); nudged up 2lb for that and could defy top weight with improvement likely.
runners.comment: Surprised trainer when winning after long absence; could defy top weight with improvement

runners.name: Dee's Lady
runners.spotlight: Some placed form in maiden hurdles on heavy ground before making immediate impact on handicap debut at Leopardstown (2m3f, soft to heavy) off 108; creditable second on chasing debut at Downpatrick last time (2m4f, yielding to soft); new headgear goes on and she's a player, although has never encountered this better ground.
runners.comment: Debut h'cap winner was second over fences latest; hasn't run on this better ground to date

runners.name: Whimsy
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-19 over timber; three-time winner on the Flat has placed in two starts in that sphere since falling over hurdles at Leopardstown in March; recent form suggests she's fit and healthy but poor strike rate over hurdles is off-putting.
runners.comment: Good Flat runs since falling over hurdles in March; poor hurdle strike rate concerning

runners.name: Nelson Muntz
runners.spotlight: 1-7 over hurdles after scoring at Kilbeggan in September (2m, good to yielding) and running some decent races in defeat subsequently, including when just 2l behind winner on handicap hurdle debut at Leopardstown (2m, yielding); yet to win from 10 Flat starts and his tailed-off finish at the Curragh last time doesn't enthuse.
runners.comment: 1-7 over hurdles inc' some decent runs in defeat but latest Curragh run doesn't enthuse

runners.name: Our Uncle Jack
runners.spotlight: Adoption of front-running tactics had transformative effect when seeing him score back-to-back wide-margin wins at Clonmel and Fairyhouse on heavy ground at start of the year; pulled up at Clonmel in February but bounced back when third at Clonmel last month (2m, soft); better ground is a concern.
runners.comment: Back-to-back front-running wins at start of year; better ground a concern

runners.name: Lincoln Du Seuil
runners.spotlight: French heavy-ground bumper winner; well beaten in three maiden hurdles and tailed off at Fairyhouse on handicap debut last time (2m, yielding to soft); an interesting jockey booking.
runners.comment: French bumper winner was tailed off on h'cap debut last time

runners.name: Tatateo
runners.spotlight: Well held in handicap hurdle outings; added a second win over timber in January when landing a Cork rated novice hurdle (2m1f, yielding to soft); followed up on AW to notch a second Flat win; cheekpieces go back on after he was tailed off in a Fairyhouse novice handicap hurdle last month (2m, yielding to soft).
runners.comment: Two wins over hurdles but below best in h'caps inc' when tailed off last time

runners.name: Bridgehead
runners.spotlight: Bellewstown maiden hurdle winner in 2024 (2m1f, good) and returned from a 632-day absence when seventh on the Flat back at that venue last month; that is something to build on.
runners.comment: Returned from 632-day absence when seventh on Flat at Bellewstown; something to build on

runners.name: Our Lucky Lady
runners.spotlight: Never outside top three in three starts over hurdles, including a Sligo maiden hurdle win (2m2f, good to yielding) in October; more needed now entering handicap company.
runners.comment: Sligo m'den hurdle winner but more needed here on handicap hurdles debut

runners.name: Spy
runners.spotlight: Killarney handicap hurdle winner in August (2m1f, good) off 113; fairly consistent hurdles/Flat form afterwards and recorded a third Flat win at Dundalk when last seen in December; this ground suits.
runners.comment: Consistent sort added fourth win under rules' when winning on AW in Dec'; ground suits

runners.name: Positive Energy
runners.spotlight: Flat winner got off the mark immediately over hurdles when winning a Navan maiden hurdle last year (2m, heavy); well beaten in a Naas novice contest next time before some consistent Flat efforts; unseen since tailed off at Naas back over hurdles on final start for Michael O'Callaghan in November; needs to bring his best.
runners.comment: New stable since tailed off over timber in Nov'; needs to return to form.

runners.name: Day Trader
runners.spotlight: Dual French Flat winner was successful over timber in a Listowel maiden hurdle in June (2m, good); fairly moderate Flat/hurdles form since and was 11th of 12 at Leopardstown last time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Fairly moderate hurdle/Flat form since winning at Listowel last June over timber

runners.name: High Court Cave
runners.spotlight: Point and bumper winner has been absent since winning a Downpatrick maiden hurdle in September (2m2f, yielding); took second off a 15lb lower mark in a big-field Galway handicap in July prior to his win (2m6f, good); player if ready to rock.
runners.comment: Absent since winning at Downpatrick in Sept'; player if ready to rock

runners.name: Teed Up
runners.spotlight: Galway specialist, four of his five Flat wins and both hurdle wins came there; way below his best away from his beloved track and has been tailed off in his last two outings.
runners.comment: Galway specialist has been poor away from his beloved track; tailed off last two times

runners.name: In My Teens
runners.spotlight: 98-rated good-ground Flat winner; unseated at the first in a Fairyhouse Grade 3 in November and tailed off in a Grade 2 contest at Leopardstown over Christmas (2m, yielding); didn't jump fluently when 16l behind the Mark Walsh-ridden Proactif at Fairyhouse in January; goes handicapping now but Walsh rides Saveforarainyday.
runners.comment: 98-rated Flat winner; h'cap debutant after moderate Graded efforts over hurdles this term

runners.name: Woodstream Lad
runners.spotlight: Three hurdles wins, the latest at Downpatrick in July over 2m3f (good); pulled up after making mistakes over fences when last seen at Downpatrick in August; may need this first run back.
runners.comment: Three-time hurdle winner, may need this first run back after 249 days off

runners.name: Sonny May
runners.spotlight: C&D win in November 2024 (soft) was a second hurdling success; didn't set the world alight in subsequent runs including when well beaten at the festival here last season; reverts to hurdles after two poor chase runs this term; needs to bounce back to form.
runners.comment: Reverts to hurdles after two poor chase starts; needs to come back to form

races.race_name: Stables Restaurant Naas INH Flat Race

runners.name: Thinkitdontjinxit
runners.spotlight: Bought for 185,000GBP after winning a Ballycrystal point-to-point by 8l; runner-up in two bumpers at Thurles in February-March, both over 2m on soft and soft-to-heavy ground and shaped like further would suit in the latest one; not discounted up in trip.
runners.comment: Point winner, runner up in both bumpers at Thurles, step up in trip a plus, not discounted

runners.name: Premier Division
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite when a 4l third of 11 at Fairyhouse; solid run when a 5.5l third behind The Mourne Rambler and Cityofblindinlites at Leopardstown; place claims again upped in trip, but not certain to turn the tables on Cityofblindinlites.
runners.comment: Promise both starts, behind Cityofblindinlites latest, not certain to turn the tables

runners.name: Largy Star
runners.spotlight: 2-3 in point-to-points; remote second on Rules' debut in a Naas bumper (18.5f, heavy); got much closer when a 5.75l third in a Fairyhouse maiden hurdle on latest in first-time tongue tie (retained) and that form has been boosted by the runner-up; player.
runners.comment: 2-3 in points, remote second at Naas, closer in Fairyhouse maiden hurdle latest, player

runners.name: Cityofblindinlites
runners.spotlight: Solid form on debut at Leopardstown's Christmas meeting, finding just The Mourne Rambler too good (Premier Division third, the fourth and fifth have both won since); hung to the right that day so this track should suit; leading claims if seeing out this longer trip (mixed messages in pedigree).
runners.comment: Beaten by The Mourne Rambler on debut, leading form claims if stays this longer trip

runners.name: Muckanagh Lough
runners.spotlight: Ordinary point-to-point form but fine effort on second start under Rules when 1.75l second of 9 at Leopardstown's Christmas meeting (2m4f, yielding); beaten 0.5l on debut for this yard in a Limerick bumper (19.5f, sft-hvy) when didn't get the clearest of runs; solid chance.
runners.comment: Big promise last two bumper runs, denied a clear run when beaten 0.5l latest, chance

runners.name: Heldam
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; sent off the 15-8 favourite but well held by Cityofblindinlites on their meeting at Leopardstown in December; failed to justify short odds when an 8.5l third of four at this venue in February (2m, heavy); can't dismiss given who trains him, but others have stronger form.
runners.comment: Point winner, beaten favourite in both bumpers, others have stronger form claims

runners.name: Holly's Thunder
runners.spotlight: Crystal Ocean mare; half-sister to eight winners inc Don Poli (high-class 2m4f-3m1f hurdle/chase), Debece (useful bumper and 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase) and Politesse (useful bumper and 2m-3m hurdle/chase); dam 2m winner; respected source, well bred and worth a market check.
runners.comment: Crystal Ocean mare, half-sister to classy Don Poli, respected source, check the market
runners.owner: R H Fowler & Mrs A Frost & David Hammond

runners.name: Muted Melody
runners.spotlight: Has shown minor promise in three of her four bumper starts, including when a 12.5l fourth of 7 in a Listed heat at Gowran when last seen in October; needs to improve again to make an impact here.
runners.comment: Promise in last three starts in bumpers, will need to improve again to figure here

runners.name: Gonewild
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; promise on debut in a hot maiden hurdle (2m, yielding) at Leopardstown in December that has thrown up plenty of winners; weakened late when beaten 11.5l into third in a 2m3f Cork bumper (yielding) after a 100-day break in first-time tongue tie (retained); may have needed that and could go well.
runners.comment: Promise in hot maiden hurdle on debut, may have needed latest, could go well

runners.name: Denham Rock
runners.spotlight: Homebred Mahler gelding; dam placed 1m 2yo-2m1f Flat (inc AW; RPR 75) and 2m/2m5f hurdle winner, half-sister to 6f winner Marble Bar, out of Listed-placed 7f 2yo winner (stayed at least 1m4f); shrewd connections have won this race before, so market check advised.
runners.comment: Home-bred Mahler gelding, dam 2m-2m5f hurdle winner, check the market

runners.name: Macdonagh Park
runners.spotlight: Placed in one of three starts in point-to-points; cost E110,000 as a 3yo and related to two bumper winners, but major step forward required on Rules' debut.
runners.comment: Placed in one of three points, related to two bumper winners, major step forward needed

runners.name: Tubberfinn Gordon
runners.spotlight: Austrian School gelding; dam unraced half-sister to useful 2m2f-3m hurdle/chase winner Bally Longford, out of unraced sister to smart Irish hunter chaser Stay In Touch; likely best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Austrian School gelding, dam unraced, likely best watched on debut

runners.name: Lyrical Park
runners.spotlight: Modest form in both starts in point-to-points; by a top sire and dam a Listed bumper winner but needs to improve plenty to feature here.
runners.comment: Modest form in both points, well-bred but needs to improve plenty to feature here

runners.name: Colwyn Bay
runners.spotlight: Outran odds of 66-1 to finish a 7.75l third on debut in a Clonmel bumper (2m3f, soft); should improve for that experience but will need to find more to figure here.
runners.comment: Outran odds on debut when third at Clonmel, will need to find more to figure here

runners.name: Delorean Dynamite
runners.spotlight: Very modest form in three bumpers and unseated at the fourth on hurdles debut at Tramore on latest; would be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Modest form in three bumpers, unseated on hurdles debut latest, unlikely

runners.name: Rattledelatch
runners.spotlight: 50-1 and pulled up before the last on debut in a Gowran maiden hurdle in November; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: 50-1 and pulled up on debut in a Gowran maiden hurdle, seems safe to rule out
runners.owner: John Joseph Flynn & Conor Lannen & Mrs Miriam Queally

runners.name: Jukebox Charlie
runners.spotlight: Attracted a bit of market support on debut (40-1 into 14-1) at Down Royal in December but completely tailed off; probably capable of better, but hard to fancy on that effort on first start for new yard.
runners.comment: Backed at big odds on debut at Down Royal but tailed off, new yard, hard to fancy

runners.name: Dark Eagle
runners.spotlight: Related to a couple of winners but was never involved on debut at Down Royal in December when a 40-1 shot; tongue-tie now goes on.
runners.comment: Never involved when 80-1 on debut at Down Royal, tongue tie goes on now

runners.name: Ned Hill
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in two bumper runs for Fergal O'Brien, the latest at this meeting last year when beaten 82l; has joined a top yard but needs to leave those previous efforts well behind.
runners.comment: Ordinary form for Fergal O'Brien, has joined top yard, needs to leave previous runs behind

runners.name: Ajp Falco
runners.spotlight: 22-1 and never landed a blow on debut at Fairyhouse in November; not easy to make a case for on that evidence.
runners.comment: Never landed a blow on debut at Fairyhouse, hard to fancy on that evidence

runners.name: Killultagh Fox
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 18-1 on debut at this venue in 2023 and ran a fine race to finish a 3.75l third (Fleur In The Park second) for Ray Cody; now in the care of Willie Mullins and while a 933-day absence is off-putting, any market moves for this half-brother to Killultagh Vic should be noted.
runners.comment: Fine debut run here in 2023, joined Willie Mullins, market best guide after huge layoff
runners.owner: Mrs Rose Boyd & Mrs Marie J Armstrong

races.race_name: Quaff Box Handicap

runners.name: Ballon Rouge
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way on her Wolverhampton handicap debut early last month but didn't look to see out 1m4f having gone to the front off the home turn; 4lb lower and she ought to fare better.
runners.comment: Didn't look to see out 1m4f having gone to the front latest; 4lb lower; one to consider

runners.name: Central Command
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to three winners, including on Tapeta; gelded since well held in three 7f runs at Kempton late last year (big prices each time); goes in first-time headgear and this is more realistic.
runners.comment: Gelded since well held in three 7f runs at Kempton last year; goes in first-time headgear

runners.name: Project Kinsman
runners.spotlight: Better on her C&D handicap debut here 18 days ago, upped from 7f, and is 1lb lower tonight; winner and third that night have let the side down since but that gave something to build on.
runners.comment: Better on her C&D handicap debut here 18 days ago; that gave something to build on

runners.name: Sweet Love
runners.spotlight: Exposed, regressive maiden who again finished weakly on her first crack at 1m4f two months ago; handicapper giving her every chance but she's vulnerable to improvers and not obviously fancied.
runners.comment: Exposed, regressive maiden; handicapper giving her every chance but she's vulnerable again

runners.name: Fallacious Promise
runners.spotlight: First signs of life when coming home strongly, upped to 1m, for second in a Kempton 0-55 in February (race worked out fairly among the principals); stamina not assured on pedigree, upped 3lb, but his claims are obvious otherwise for a yard enjoying a good spring.
runners.comment: First signs of life when coming home strongly, upped to 1m, last time; leading claims

runners.name: Steel Fixer
runners.spotlight: Quite well related, being a half-brother to three AW winners; overraced and hit traffic in a messy Wolverhampton handicap (1m4f) on his AW debut ten days ago, but that was at least a step up on his turf efforts.
runners.comment: Overraced and hit traffic latest but that was at least a step up on his turf efforts

runners.name: Skirt Around
runners.spotlight: Not without hope in three 7f runs in the space of a few weeks last September, including here, since when she's undergone wind surgery; starts handicap life at a sensible level and the market should tell a tale for a yard also responsible for Project Kinsman.
runners.comment: Starts handicap life at a sensible level; market useful (yard also run Project Kinsman)

runners.name: Regal Knight
runners.spotlight: Huge prices and well beaten in three runs last autumn; this more realistic back from seven months off, now handicapping off a lowly opening mark, but would want to see some support before considering him.
runners.comment: This more realistic but would want to see some support before considering him
runners.owner: D Bishop P Robinson S Bland T Coyle

races.race_name: Get Your Free Bets @ freebets.com Handicap

runners.name: Say What You See
runners.spotlight: Two wins and a second from three runs on Tapeta, the second coming when clear with a progressive winner over C&D last August; turned over twice as favourite on turf this spring but holds decent claims once more.
runners.comment: Two wins and a solid second from three runs on Tapeta; holds decent claims once more

runners.name: Mao Shang Wong
runners.spotlight: Not seen it out over 1m4f a couple of times since two wins in March, one over C&D; it might just be that his current mark is beyond him but there won't be any excuses tonight.
runners.comment: Not seen it out over 1m4f since two wins in March, one over C&D; should fare better

runners.name: Star Cast
runners.spotlight: Two C&D wins already this year, latterly off 4lb lower, and continues in form, the drop to 1m working against her when she came home nicely at Doncaster five weeks ago; goes on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins already this year; drop to 1m worked against her last time; shortlisted

runners.name: Jack Sparowe
runners.spotlight: Not as good now and prone to the occasional off-day; still to win over C&D but his record suggests he needs considering off this mark, especially given his two previous runs in cheekpieces both resulted in near-misses.
runners.comment: Two previous runs in cheekpieces were solid and likes it here; a case could be made

runners.name: Sure And Stedfast
runners.spotlight: Buzzy gelding who won twice on turf last year; only run once before on the AW but this mark should be within range if he keeps calm in a first-time hood back from six months off.
runners.comment: Buzzy gelding; this mark should be within range if he keeps calm in a first-time hood

runners.name: Jujubella
runners.spotlight: Progressive for cheekpieces going on early last year, with three course wins, latterly off a 4lb lower mark; again goes without the headgear back from six months off and there's nothing to suggest she's best caught fresh.
runners.comment: Progressive for cheekpieces going on early last year, with three course wins; headgear off

runners.name: Sound Janet
runners.spotlight: Excuses when last seen but was otherwise expensive to follow for Roger Varian before picked up for 25,000gns in December; ran well first time out as a 2yo on Tapeta, though, and this mark should be within range if ready to go after six months off.
runners.comment: Expensive to follow for Roger Varian before sold for 25,000gns; market useful

runners.name: Haveyoumissedme
runners.spotlight: Stays a deal further but has always liked it here and has run well a couple of times over C&D this year; a good pace would help but finished behind Say What You See on turf last time and others hold stronger claims.
runners.comment: Always liked it here and has run well a couple of times over C&D this year; each-way shout

runners.name: Sea The Light
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f maiden on this card last year on his racecourse debut; struggled subsequently but this is a bit easier down another 3lb in first-time blinkers (wore cheekpieces when last seen); yard also run Jack Sparowe.
runners.comment: Won a 7f maiden on this card last year on his racecourse debut; this a bit easier down 3lb

runners.name: King Of Fury
runners.spotlight: Quite late in the day to be going beyond an extended 1m for the first time; often makes the running and can race freely, so will need to settle early from a wide stall back from ten weeks off; not in much form when last seen either way.
runners.comment: Late in the day to be going beyond an extended 1m for the first time; needs to settle
runners.owner: Mrs K S Pratt & M Dods

runners.name: Natzor
runners.spotlight: Wins for David O'Meara both came over C&D, including a division of this race two years ago; not beaten far at 100-1 here two months ago but looked flattered in a messy affair and he's generally struggled for this yard.
runners.comment: Wins for David O'Meara both came over C&D; generally struggled for this yard

runners.name: Free Speech
runners.spotlight: Patchy during a limited campaign last year for Ed Dunlop; wins as a 3yo came on soft turf and this trip looks a bare minimum for her new yard on comeback.
runners.comment: Wins as a 3yo came on soft turf; this trip a bare minimum for her new yard on comeback

races.race_name: Roflow Dust & Fume Lev Systems Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: New York Minute
runners.spotlight: Headgear helped last autumn, with him landing a hat-trick, including two over 2m round Southwell, latterly off a 4lb lower mark; solid effort there last Thursday, back from a few months off (1m3f), but he'll want a similarly strong pace this evening.
runners.comment: This trip a bare minimum and will want a good test this evening, as at Southwell last week

runners.name: Machete
runners.spotlight: Not as good now but remains generally consistent and is back on the mark off which he won here (1m2f) in the winter; needs to bounce back from a lesser effort over C&D when last seen six weeks ago.
runners.comment: Generally consistent and is back on the mark off which he won here (1m2f) in the winter

runners.name: Ludo's Landing
runners.spotlight: Won for blinkers first being fitted and did so again for them going back on over C&D two months ago; lacked consistency in recent times and there's not much wiggle room off this mark but he's capable if on a going day.
runners.comment: Lacked consistency in recent times; not much wiggle room off this mark but he's capable

runners.name: The Cursor
runners.spotlight: Powerful yard, who won this two years ago, had a few disappoint at short prices of late; this lightly raced 400,000gns yearling holds obvious claims, having been gelded since winning his novice last summer (highly tried a couple of times prior) though.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; holds obvious claims, having been gelded since winning his novice last July

runners.name: Melinda
runners.spotlight: Limited in four runs on the Flat as a 3yo before winning a juvenile hurdle when last seen 18 months ago; this looks quite an ask from 2lb out of the handicap under Lexie Clarkson, who's taking just a second ride under rules.
runners.comment: This is quite an ask from 2lb out of the handicap under the inexperienced Lexie Clarkson

races.race_name: Roflow Specialist Ventilation & Dehumidification Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Eklleem
runners.spotlight: 200,000gns yearling; mixed messages as to what he achieved when well-held second in a 7f Newmarket novice in October but that's still the best form on offer and there should be more to come as a 3yo now gelded.
runners.comment: Standard setter and there should be more to come as a 3yo now gelded

runners.name: Eljowhary
runners.spotlight: 140,000gns yearling and a half-brother to the useful White Crown Star; dam unraced half-sister to winners Eleonora Duse (1m2f Group 2) and Scottish Stage (1m2f Listed; runner-up in Irish Oaks); has been gelded but he's nicely bred and in top hands.
runners.comment: 140,000gns yearling; has been gelded but he's nicely bred and in top hands

runners.name: Viper
runners.spotlight: Market springer on his debut at Doncaster a month ago (7f, good to soft) and he shaped nicely in a race dominated by strong yards; open to improvement.
runners.comment: Shaped quite nicely at Doncaster in a novice dominated by strong yards

runners.name: Record Day
runners.spotlight: Posted modest RPRs when showing some promise in his first two races; looks one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Posted modest RPRs when showing some promise in his first two races

runners.name: Marhayb
runners.spotlight: 160,000gns yearling; third foal; half-sister to winners Fire Flame (7f; RPR 82) and Marianglas (7f AW; 74); dam 6f 2yo winner (100); the market could be informative with the owner doubly represented.
runners.comment: 160,000gns yearling; market could be informative with the owner doubly represented

runners.name: Grasmere Boy
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; dam 7f winner (RPR 93) out of French 1m4f winning half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Roscius; he'd be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Fourth foal; dam 7f winner (RPR 93) who is well related; still likely best watched

runners.name: Khaleejy
runners.spotlight: Cost 75,000euros as a 2yo but never raced for James Tate (withdrawn in late 2024 after getting upset in the stalls; 5-1 at the time); can only be watched making debut as a 5yo.
runners.comment: Cost 75,000euros as a 2yo but never raced for James Tate; makes debut as a 5yo

runners.name: Billyjoegold
runners.spotlight: Beaten double-digit lengths in two C&D novices and one for another day.
runners.comment: Beaten double-digit lengths in two C&D novices and one for another day

runners.name: Thunder Rush
runners.spotlight: His fifth over 1m here in March was a remote one and he went off at 33-1.
runners.comment: His fifth over 1m here in March was a remote one and he went off at 33-1

runners.name: Furrst Lady
runners.spotlight: Refused to race on debut and tailed off when she has completed; had wind surgery.
runners.comment: Refused to race on debut and tailed off when she has completed; had wind surgery

races.race_name: Northumbria Leisure For Gaming Machines Handicap

runners.name: Yy Spirit
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fourth attempt when landing a modest fillies' novice over C&D in December; 66-1 and well beaten on last month's stable/handicap debut; best watched.
runners.comment: C&D win for Ed Bethell in December; well-beaten 66-1 shot on recent stable/handicap debut

runners.name: Dandy Breeze
runners.spotlight: Slowly away when second, beaten a nose, on his debut here in December (5f; 12-1); duly went one better when the odds-on favourite for another 5f novice here in January; gelded afterwards; makes his handicap debut over an extra furlong and off a mark that looks manageable; betting useful.
runners.comment: Two 5f runs here this winter yielded a second and a win; unexposed handicap debutant

runners.name: Starmade
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of his three runs last autumn, getting off the mark in a 5f maiden here in October; plugged on for third in a 5.7f handicap at Bath (good to soft) on his reappearance two weeks ago, beaten nearly 5l at the finish; needs more to defy this mark but returning to AW may help.
runners.comment: Never-dangerous third at Bath (5.7f) on seasonal/handicap debut; 1-1 on AW; needs more

runners.name: Numero Vingt
runners.spotlight: Won two 6f events on Polytrack in February; unplaced in both handicaps though and his latest 2lb drop isn't enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Two Polytrack wins in February but on a tough mark judged on subsequent handicap form

runners.name: Loquella
runners.spotlight: C&D win on debut and then ran well under a penalty at Wolverhampton; dropped away to finish sixth of the seven runners in a 7f handicap at Southwell in March; returning to 6f can help but not obviously well treated.
runners.comment: C&D winner on debut; unexposed but she needs to leave her handicap debut run behind her

runners.name: Stoic Poet
runners.spotlight: No immediate improvement for the switch to handicaps when eighth of nine over 7f at Haydock 216 days ago; his novice and maiden runs had threatened better though and he still has time to fulfil that promise; betting useful in a winnable race.
runners.comment: Down the field over 7f on nursery debut but promise beforehand and still unexposed

runners.name: There's A Chance
runners.spotlight: Made a successful handicap debut at Hamilton (6f, good) last July off a 6lb lower mark and that form worked out nicely; ran in sales races on her final two starts in 2025, eighth of 21 at Naas and seventh of 14 at Goodwood (7f, good to soft); was challenging 1f out in the latter event before her stamina gave way; untried on AW and absent for 219 days but there could be more to come this year.
runners.comment: Won only nursery run as a 2yo; better than distance beaten suggests when last seen; chance

runners.name: Logi Bear
runners.spotlight: Faced some stiff tasks as a 2yo after winning a Newmarket novice last May (6f, good); not on a bad mark on his best efforts now starting out for John Butler (sold out of Richard Hannon's stable for 20,000gns last October); has been gelded; betting to guide.
runners.comment: Highly tried after novice win last May; sold 20,000gns in October; gelded; market useful

races.race_name: Hays Travel: Nobody Offers You More Handicap

runners.name: Blufferonthebus
runners.spotlight: The last of three wins in 2025 came here off only 1lb lower, albeit in a Class 6; offered little since then.
runners.comment: It was a Class 6 she won here in November and has offered little since

runners.name: The Green Man
runners.spotlight: Close up behind Beale Street here in March (6f) and produced a similar effort from off the pace to be runner-up over C&D 11 days ago; needs a good pace to chase (stays 1m) and no guarantee that he'll get one here.
runners.comment: Needs a good pace to chase and major claims in current form should he get one
runners.owner: The Dalby Family And P Kirby

runners.name: Ey Up Its Jazz
runners.spotlight: Realistically weighted on his best efforts last year and his second run in March was better than the first; however, his AW exploits are unconvincing.
runners.comment: Realistically weighted on his best efforts last year but his AW exploits are unconvincing

runners.name: William Dewhirst
runners.spotlight: Prolific here during the 2024/25 AW season but he's beaten one rival in two starts after an absence; couldn't support with any confidence.
runners.comment: Lots of good form here but he's beaten one rival in two starts after an absence

runners.name: Recency Bias
runners.spotlight: Won his novice here by 5l and looked on a workable mark for his handicap debut at Wolverhampton but he never travelled after starting slowly; wouldn't judge him on that performance and could be given another chance.
runners.comment: Never travelled on handicap debut but he impressed in his novice here the run before

runners.name: Pressure's On
runners.spotlight: Didn't appear to be crying out for the return to 1m at Doncaster and weakened late on; this is his trip, over which he was twice runner-up at Wolverhampton in March; not handicapped out of it, either.
runners.comment: Returns to his optimum trip of 7f and he's not handicapped out of it, either

runners.name: Beale Street
runners.spotlight: All four wins have been here but over 6f; he appears to stay 7f but had a shocker over C&D last time; that was too bad to be true in fairness.
runners.comment: Good record here over 6f and stays 7f; latest run was too bad to be true

runners.name: Gressington
runners.spotlight: Won a small-field C&D handicap over Christmas but two nothing runs in January and difficult to know what to expect from him after a break.
runners.comment: Won a small-field C&D handicap over Christmas but two nothing runs in January
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

races.race_name: Ride For Tommie Jakes Racing Club Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Caragio
runners.spotlight: Began his 3yo campaign with a BHA mark of 93; a lot of water has flowed under the bridge since but he has bounced back to form for Adam Kirby, finishing second at Chelmsford in March (7f) before an easy win at Yarmouth ten days ago (6f, good to firm); 3lb well in under a penalty; obvious chance.
runners.comment: Easy win on turf ten days ago and well in under a penalty; obvious chance

runners.name: Lion's House
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; popped up in a 5f handicap here in February but more to prove after lesser efforts here (bled from the nose) and on turf on his last two starts.
runners.comment: Conditions fine and on a fair mark but consistency wouldn't be his strong suit

runners.name: Annie Edson Taylor
runners.spotlight: Heavy ground excuses her low-key end to 2025 and she ran a series of good races, including a win at Hamilton when first tried in blinkers; whether first time out is the time to catch her remains to be seen but interesting that a tongue-tie goes on for her return.
runners.comment: In good form on good or softer last summer; tongue tied for her return; AW a query

runners.name: Woolridge
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden; well short of his Irish best in two runs for new stable in March (5f); enough to prove for now.
runners.comment: 0-10 and he has been well short of his Irish best in two runs for his new stable

runners.name: Raft Up
runners.spotlight: Good efforts in all three 6f runs this year, placed behind the prolific Yorkshire Glory twice and readily seeing off five rivals at Southwell eight days ago; 1lb well in under a penalty; should go well.
runners.comment: Three good 6f runs this year, winning at Southwell last week; 1lb well in under a penalty

runners.name: Asadjumeirah
runners.spotlight: Five-time C&D winner; unable to justify favouritism in a 0-55 here 11 days ago and he has more on his plate at this level.
runners.comment: Conditions to suit but he was the beaten favourite in a lesser race here 11 days ago

runners.name: Speeding Bullet
runners.spotlight: Reinvented as a sprinter by Paul Midgley last season, winning a 6f handicap at Pontefract in July and turning in three solid efforts over 6f and 5f afterwards; returns from 238 days off but he still has handicapping scope; market confidence would look significant.
runners.comment: Did well dropped to sprint trips last summer; unexposed in this discipline; check betting
runners.owner: Slaters Arms Racing And Ged Shields

runners.name: Invincible Ruby
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f handicap at Catterick off this mark last July but it has been downhill ever since; plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Out of sorts since winning at Catterick (7f) last July; plenty to prove

runners.name: Kings Merchant
runners.spotlight: On a dangerous mark now and his three runs this year have offered hope that his losing run could be snapped imminently; effective on AW and likely to be involved.
runners.comment: Been a while since his last win but down in the weights and threatening something bigger

runners.name: Bellagio Man
runners.spotlight: Recorded back-to-back C&D wins last October (the second off 3lb lower) but the wheels came off in his hat-trick bid in November; back from 161 days off with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Back-to-back C&D wins in October but ran poorly in his hat-trick bid when last seen; risky

runners.name: Lord Abama
runners.spotlight: Two-time C&D winner; unplaced but ran with credit in two Thirsk handicaps last month; type to pop up soon off a reduced mark.
runners.comment: Two-time C&D winner; on a handy mark and he's the type to pop up soon
runners.owner: Mrs K S Pratt & M Dods

runners.name: Goal Line
runners.spotlight: Finished a creditable third of seven (6f, good to soft; RPR 66) at Ayr on his 2yo debut; well beaten twice afterwards (7f and 1m); makes handicap and stable debut after a 506-day absence; has been gelded; betting to guide.
runners.comment: One good run and two lesser ones for Adrian Keatley in 2024; absent since; check betting

races.race_name: Connolly's Red Mills Open Hunters' Chase

runners.name: Great Valley
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten in six since February 2025, encompassing 2m4f-3m contests around various track and going types; effortless transition to hunter chases at Ludlow latest (2m4f, good), and although patiently ridden there his proven ability at making all might yet come in handy down in trip.
runners.comment: Easy Ludlow hunter chase win latest (2m4f, good); tactically versatile, and ground fine.

runners.name: On Lovers Walk
runners.spotlight: First wins for three years when striking last month in a Paxford restricted (2m4f) and Fakenham maiden hunter chase (3m, led last stride), both on good; prospects would improve if curbing the habit of jumping right evident on both of those occasions.
runners.comment: 2m4f point and 3m hunter chase wins lately; ideally needs to lose habit of jumping right.

runners.name: Franigane
runners.spotlight: 33-1 into 18-1, flattered slightly by near-1l margin behind an idling Barton Snow in this race last year (good), but that was still a mighty run after 418 days out; absent again since, but his comparatively moderate RPRs around sharpish tracks probably concerns more.
runners.comment: Found only Barton Snow too good in this last year; not as convincing on sharper tracks.

races.race_name: Pertemps Network Intermediate Point-To-Point Hunters' Chase

runners.name: The Dancing Tree
runners.spotlight: Ex-Gavin Cromwell; likely needed first run since April 2025 when beaten 28l on British/yard debut; middle leg of subsequent five-timer was a 6l defeat of Practice Run in a Howick intermediate (soft); untried over rules fences, but versatile as regards track and going types.
runners.comment: Five-timer includes an intermediate defeat of Practice Run; track and ground fine.

runners.name: Mister Splash
runners.spotlight: Stamina to spare as a dual 3m4f winner, including at High Easter two runs ago, and effective on sound surfaces; can't afford a repeat of the jumps to the right of last month's Fakenham near-miss (chase debut).
runners.comment: Jumped markedly right on last month's hunter chase debut near-miss; sound surfaces suit.

runners.name: Ultimate Survivor
runners.spotlight: A length second but in turn well clear of Tellmesomethingood at Larkhill in December (good), and went one better there next time; didn't settle especially well on his first tilt at an intermediate latest, however, and brings zero rules experience with him.
runners.comment: Holds Tellmesomethingood on December clash; has no rules experience to call upon.

runners.name: Fame And Concrete
runners.spotlight: Both restricted wins in 2025 were recorded on good, which he's not encountered so far this campaign; interesting on that account, though no previous rules chasing experience and posted both his respective worst hurdle and bumper RPRs at Warwick.
runners.comment: Ground closer to good would be appreciated; hasn't shone at Warwick twice previously.

runners.name: Practice Run
runners.spotlight: No experience of rules fences, and has 6l to make up on The Dancing Tree judged on their Howick clash in March (soft); still on the up, however, making all in an intermediate at that same venue latest (good).
runners.comment: 6l to find with The Dancing Tree on their March clash; won an intermediate when last seen.

runners.name: Tellmesomethingood
runners.spotlight: Dual 2025 point winner around stayers' tracks, and present season's best effort similarly came around Lockinge's galloping line last time, so may not appreciate Warwick as much as some; sound surface fine.
runners.comment: Sound surface will be fine, but might well have had more to offer around Cheltenham.

races.race_name: GB Pointing Open Hunters' Chase

runners.name: Mister Coffey
runners.spotlight: It was disappointing how far below form he ran when fourth switched to a hunter chase at Newbury latest (3m, good to soft), for all that it was a decent race of its type; even so, a repeat of the form of last November's Cheltenham second would still easily suffice in this thinner-looking race.
runners.comment: 0-20 over fences and disappointing on hunter chase debut; this is easier again, however.

runners.name: High Game Royal
runners.spotlight: Outpaced on all four starts this year, latterly in hunter chases at Stratford (2m5f) and Exeter (3m), on which evidence the marathon on this card might have been a more comfortable fit; others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: Outpaced again when 17l third at Exeter latest (3m); worth trying over further still.

runners.name: What A Glance
runners.spotlight: Easy winner of this race in 2024 (soft) and the Stratford champion event (good) straight after, and nearest miss since when held by a neck at Brocklesby in February; there's always the chance he'll thrown in a mistake, but case is still good on this easiest-looking hunter chase assignment for some time.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024; this is easiest hunter chase task for a while; can make the odd mistake.

runners.name: Laurel Girl
runners.spotlight: Over a fence behind last month when registering a first completion this campaign at the seventh attempt; impossible to fancy.
runners.comment: Over a fence behind when registering first completion in seven last time; not the winner.

runners.name: Alaphilippe
runners.spotlight: Will have appreciated a simple task in his members race last time after a hard-fought Didmarton dead-heat; in better heart than many, but it's a worry that all bar one of his four chase starts have featured their share of jumping errors, including this race last year (pulled up).
runners.comment: Two contrasting wins latterly (dead-heat, easy members win); can make mistakes in chases.

races.race_name: Jonathan Neesom Memorial Open Hunters' Chase

runners.name: Crawter
runners.spotlight: Landed the intermediate on this card last term (good) despite his customary habit of jumping right; listless display at Exeter on his most recent hunter chase outing and had to work hard to land the odds in a 3m6f Larkhill open since (good to firm).
runners.comment: Intermediate winner on this card last year; hard ridden to land 3m6f open point latest.

runners.name: Imperial Esprit
runners.spotlight: Exposed, but also placed in hunter chases on last three visits to Stratford (all sub-3m) and notably a winner during May in each of the previous three years; respected, for all that there's not huge substance to some of this spring's successes (two conditions events and a walkover).
runners.comment: May winner from 2023 to 2025 inclusive and usually places in hunter chases; not ruled out.

runners.name: Corporal Jackjones
runners.spotlight: More than 5l too good in a Stratford novice hunter in March (2m5f, soft) despite whacking the last, and no shame since when second at Lower Machen giving the prolific Fumet d'Oudairies 3lb; could argue he's marginally more compelling at sub-3m trips, but among the likeliest even so.
runners.comment: Fine effort in defeat since Stratford hunter chase win (2m5f, soft); among the likeliest.

runners.name: Kilfilum Woods
runners.spotlight: Found only the prolific Grace A Vous Enki too good in the Lady Dudley Cup classic at Chaddesley Corbett last time (3m2f), having won two opens over shorter immediately before (all on good); first-fence casualty when last seen in a hunter chase, but generally a safe conveyance nowadays.
runners.comment: In fine heart; 2m4f/3m open point wins before last month's Lady Dudley Cup second (good).

races.race_name: Natural Green Creative Spaces Mares' Open Hunters' Chase

runners.name: Police Academy
runners.spotlight: Unlucky to lose last June's Uttoxeter chase win (3m, good) on a medical technicality; wheels came off in the autumn but she has revived for current yard, keeping on well for Higham and High Easter open successes on good to firm; could prove the answer.
runners.comment: Wheels came off under rules last autumn, but firmly back on track in open points; player.

runners.name: Hollywood Harmon
runners.spotlight: 125-1 and never a factor at Aintree last time; won't find it as hard to adopt her preferred position at or near the early speed here, but barely stays 3m (sole win at that trip came at Askham Bryan's very sharp circuit) and has further still to go here.
runners.comment: Swimming in easier waters after Aintree last time; more compelling at sub-3m trips.

runners.name: Missed Tee
runners.spotlight: Third and sixth in the last two renewals of the 2m race on this card, and still appeals more over shorter despite a win over the full 3m trip at Chaddesley Corbett last May; 0-14 over rules fences.
runners.comment: 3m point winner last May but a bit more persuasive over shorter; 0-14 over rules fences.

runners.name: For Rita
runners.spotlight: Most realistic assignment of the year when taking a Kingston Blount hands-and-heels race latest (good), in the process posting a win for the sixth straight pointing season; acts on all going, but the form of her two previous hunter chase efforts (almost three years apart) doesn't pass muster.
runners.comment: At least one point win every season since 2021; previous hunter chase form falls short.

runners.name: Walk Of No Shame
runners.spotlight: Close third of three in a Dunsmore open (good to soft) on her most recent pointing start, but her quirkier side was also evident this year having run out under two different riders; risks attached therefore, and she offered nothing on a sole previous chasing start (3m, 0-120) in autumn 2024.
runners.comment: Open third last time, but temperament as evident as ability in points with two run-outs.

runners.name: Loud And Proud
runners.spotlight: Easily won a 3m2f Doncaster mares' handicap in March 2025 for Harry Derham (good) and denied only close home in points at Revesby Park (3m) and Bangor-on-Dee (2m4f) this year; have to hope last-time failure doesn't presage a return to last autumn's rules struggles, however.
runners.comment: 3m2f Doncaster mares' chase winner in March 2025; point flop last time, however.

runners.name: All Loved Up
runners.spotlight: Enjoying her most productive season to date, last month's Kimble open point win (good to firm) making it three wins from four; stamina to spare (won the 3m6f Lord Ashton of Hyde classic) and no shock if she goes closer than last year's 24l fourth in this contest.
runners.comment: 3-4 in points this term, including a 3m6f classic; can improve on last year's fourth.

races.race_name: Foran Equine Open Hunters' Chase

runners.name: Jeffery's Cross
runners.spotlight: Beaten under 1l in this race last year (Myth Buster pulled up); won't mind the move from Cheltenham given his strong body of work around sharp tracks (including 2-2 in hurdles races here, albeit losing one for interference), and nice break since hard-fought Newbury win; ground fine.
runners.comment: Close second in this last year; effective around here as a hurdler years ago; ground fine.

runners.name: Slipway
runners.spotlight: Former winner of Highland and Southern Nationals (good to soft), so surprising he didn't see out 3m here on January's hunter chase debut; back on track in a Siddington novice riders' point latest, additionally a first career success on good ground; safe ride (never falls) and not dismissed.
runners.comment: Highland and Southern National winner; point win latest was his first on good ground.

runners.name: My Gift To You
runners.spotlight: Highly impressive 26 finishes in the first four (eight of them wins) from 30 rules/pointing runs for the Owens, with stamina proven up to 3m6f; all-the-way hurdles winner over 3m2f here just last autumn (good), and likelier than some to run his race.
runners.comment: 3m2f hurdles winner here last autumn; stamina proven up to 3m6f; should run his race.

runners.name: Peaches And Cream
runners.spotlight: Punchestown 3m7f handicap chase win in April 2022 (good) remains his most recent under rules, but the first of two point starts netted him a last-gasp Bangor-on-Dee success in March; strike-rate of only 1-11 over 3m3f or more, but this would be easier than almost any of those.
runners.comment: 1-11 in races over 3m3f or more, most of them harder than this; point winner in March.

races.race_name: Carr & Day & Martin Open Hunters' Chase

runners.name: Unexpected Party
runners.spotlight: Bled from the nose when a fading fourth in the Aintree Foxhunters latest (2m5f, good to soft), though still had Fan De Blues comfortably behind for the second time this year; more prolific on easier surfaces but Taunton win in January reaffirmed he's effective on good as well.
runners.comment: Fourth at Aintree, beating Fan De Blues for the second time this year; this is easier.

runners.name: Fan De Blues
runners.spotlight: Wind surgery has largely got him back on track this year, and there's surely a sub-3m hunter chase in him; 9l behind Unexpected Party at Aintree latest, having been beaten half that distance by him at Taunton in January (2m6f, good), and likely needs that old rival to underperform.
runners.comment: Twice beaten by Unexpected Party, including at Aintree, and may chase him home again.

runners.name: Empire De Maulde
runners.spotlight: Victory in a Parham open match last weekend took his score in points to 5-8; diminishing returns in hunter chases, and well held behind Arthur's Sixpence at Newbury in March (3m, good to soft), albeit on going which wouldn't have suited as well as tonight's projected good.
runners.comment: Won match at Parham last weekend; diminishing returns in hunter chases; good ground suits.

runners.name: Arthur's Sixpence
runners.spotlight: Fine effort at 150-1 when under 3l third at Newbury in March; all wins in the last four years, including matches in two opens in 2025, gained going left-handed on sound/quick surfaces, and perhaps best forgiven an uncharacteristic exit at Lockinge latest as he usually finds a leg.
runners.comment: 150-1 third at Newbury in March; uncharacteristic fall since; track and ground will suit.

runners.name: Hystery Bere
runners.spotlight: 2m novice handicap winner here three years ago to the day (good; RPR 129), and several decent placed efforts after; able schoolmaster for Joe Neal in novice rider points lately, getting there in time at Kimble (3m, good to firm) but not quite last time; others a bit more persuasive.
runners.comment: 2m novice handicap chase winner here three years ago; recent win under this novice rider.

races.race_name: Edgewood Stakes Presented By Accenture (Grade 2) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Taken By The Wind
runners.owner: Magdalena Racing, Terry Bradshaw Et Al

races.race_name: Longines Kentucky Oaks (Grade 1) (Fillies) (Dirt)

runners.name: Prom Queen
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner at Gulfstream Park of maiden in February (by 8l) and Grade 2 Gulfstream Park Oaks (8.5f; beat My Miss Mo by about 3l in steadily run race); travelled strongly, made eye-catching move on turn and not at all hard ridden to assert in latter race, scoring with lots in hand while still a bit green; should be suited by 1m1f; much stiffer test and must adapt to big field, but open to considerable improvement and an exciting prospect.
runners.comment: Big move on turn to win 8.5f Grade 2 at Gulfstream on only third start; exciting prospect

runners.name: Always A Runner
runners.spotlight: Most promising winner of both starts in a maiden at Tampa Bay in February (by just over 6l) and Grade 3 Gazelle Stakes (1m1f) at Aqueduct in April; always going well but had a bit to do on latter occasion, going on really strongly to beat Pashmina by just over 1l, well on top at finish; stays well (will likely have to come from some way back) and is very good prospect.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in two and stayed on strongly to win Grade 3 at Aqueduct (1m1f) by 1l; promising
runners.owner: Douglas Scharbauer & Three Chimneys Farm LLC

runners.name: Counting Stars
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner of four races, notably Grade 2 Fantasy Stakes (well placed, beat Search Party by 5l) at Oaklawn Park last time out; possibly unlucky when just under 1l second to Explora in Grade 3 Honeybee there on penultimate start, plenty to do off moderate fractions; strong claims on first start at 1m1f.
runners.comment: Easy Grade 2 winner latest; looked unlucky when second to Explora time before; good chance

runners.name: Percy's Bar
runners.spotlight: Demoted to second in Grade 1 last year, when also third in Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies despite being hampered; quickened through inside gap, got first run, when beating Zany by about 2l in Grade 1 Ashland at Keeneland (8.5f) on reappearance; others may improve more at 1m1f but she possesses impressive tactical speed.
runners.comment: Breeders' Cup third; got first run, beat Zany 2l in 8.5f Grade 1 at Keeneland; good chance

runners.name: Explora
runners.spotlight: High-class juvenile, easy winner of Grade 2 Oak Leaf at Santa Anita and good second in Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies at Del Mar; winner of two of three starts this year, on latest start beating Counting Stars by just under 1l in Grade 3 Honeybee at Oaklawn; should stay 1m1f and is a tractable, focused runner, but has a two-month layoff (missed intended prep) to overcome.
runners.comment: Grade 2 win in 2025 and beat Counting Stars in Grade 3 at Oaklawn; player but 2 months off
runners.owner: Michael E Pegram, Karl Watson & Paul Weitman

runners.name: Meaning
runners.spotlight: Winner of three of four starts, notably Grade 2 Santa Anita Oaks by 2l from Brooklyn Blonde, tracking pace and asserting steadily in straight; only defeat when 4l fourth to Super Corredora in Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies at Del Mar, where behind Explora and Percy's Bar; solid place claims.
runners.comment: Behind Explora and Percy's Bar when fourth at Breeders' Cup but Grade 2 win latest; claims

runners.name: Pashmina
runners.spotlight: In frame in black-type races three times after winning a maiden at Fair Grounds by 10l; very good second, beaten only around 1l and well clear or rest, behind Always A Runner in Grade 3 Gazelle at Aqueduct (1m1f) last time, when she looked to be getting away from eventual winner but hopped tracks in straight; smart filly, bit more exposed than some, but an interesting outsider.
runners.comment: In frame in black-type races and Grade 3 (improved form); smart but bit exposed

runners.name: Resist
runners.spotlight: Reserve who now gets in; progressive form at lower level; best effort when about 2l third (Lovely Grey second) to Lorelei Lee in Listed race at Turfway Park last time; very stiff task.
runners.comment: Very stiff task based on third in Listed race at Turfway Park on latest start

runners.name: Zany
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner of both starts last year in maiden at Gulfstream Park and Grade 2 Demoiselle at Aqueduct in December; landed the odds in Listed race at Tampa in February; unsuited by slow pace, caught wide and stayed on well late when about 2l second to Percy's Bar in Grade 1 Ashland at Keeneland last month (again edged left in straight); will relish first run beyond 8.5f; serious claims.
runners.comment: Grade 2 winner in 2025 caught eye when second to Percy's Bar at Keeneland; serious claims

runners.name: Search Party
