runners.spotlight: Useful form at Oaklawn to win maiden (by 6l) last December and Listed race (by neck) in February; in frame there since, unable to get on terms, when fourth to Explora in Grade 3 Honeybee and second to Counting Stars in Grade 2 Fantasy; will be suited by further than 8.5f but something to find.
runners.comment: Listed winner but never on terms when in frame in Graded company at Oaklawn; needs more

runners.name: Brooklyn Blonde
runners.spotlight: First race outside maiden company (winner at Santa Anita in March) when 2l second to Meaning in 8.5f Grade 2 Santa Anita Oaks last month, making an effort to challenge in straight but brushed aside; should stay 1m1f but needs to find a good bit more.
runners.comment: Good 2l second to Meaning in 8.5f Grade 2 at Santa Anita, brushed aside; needs to improve

runners.name: Lovely Grey
runners.spotlight: Reserve who now gets in; beaten on all five starts outside maidens and yet to tackle a Graded race; very stiff task.
runners.comment: Beaten all five starts outside maidens and yet to run in a Graded race; very stiff task

runners.name: Nycon
runners.spotlight: Reserve who now gets in; runner-up in black-type race and Listed event this year but well held when fourth behind Always A Runner in Grade 3 Gazelle at Aqueduct; lots to find.
runners.comment: Well held when 10l fourth to Always A Runner in Grade 3 Gazelle at Aqueduct; lots to find

runners.name: Dazzling Dame
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has won four races, notably black-type race at Colonial Downs in March; well held on her only go in better company when beaten 10l in Grade 3 at Churchill Downs last September; would be a shock winner against this quality of opponent.
runners.comment: Four lesser wins, notably black-type race; well held in a Grade 3 race; outsider
runners.owner: Respect The Valleys LLC, Medallion Racing & Madaket Stables

races.race_name: Unbridled Sidney Stakes (Grade 2) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Haulin Ice
runners.spotlight: Career record shows her ability to win (wins-runs record of 12-21) and out to complete a hat-trick; narrow success in an allowance listed at Oaklawn Park 35 days ago; should go well despite being up in class.
runners.comment: This represents a step up in class but wouldn't rule out a big run.
runners.owner: C2 Racing Stable LLC, WSS Racing & Agave Racing Stable

runners.name: Queen Maxima
runners.spotlight: Favourite but out of the places when third in a Grade 3 at Santa Anita four weeks ago; has chalked up four wins from her last seven; C&D has proven suitable before; down in distance; class rise to contend with.
runners.comment: Out of the places 27 days ago; on target four times in her last seven outings.
runners.owner: Dutch Girl Holdings LLC & Irving Ventures LLC

runners.name: Moon Spun
runners.spotlight: Disappointing to see her finish eighth 48 days ago given market expectations at Gulfstream Park in a black type; resume shows that she is a proven winner (seeking a sixth career win); steps up to allowance company.
runners.comment: Below expectations 48 days ago; has managed five wins from ten career runs.

runners.name: Time To Dazzle
runners.spotlight: Didn't run too badly when out of the frame at Keeneland (beaten just over 3l) last month (not far behind Creed's Gold) in a Grade 2; has managed four wins from 15 career runs.
runners.comment: Not beaten that far in fourth 19 days ago; a proven winner with a career wins-runs record of 4-15.

runners.name: Shisospicy
runners.spotlight: Won the Breeders' Cup Turf Sprint at Del Mar, showing early speed to lead from a wide draw in October; has won at this course; returning from a layoff here; an easier task than her previous race; hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Can be on the premises; on top in Breeders' Cup Turf Sprint six months ago.

runners.name: Buttercream Babe
runners.spotlight: Scored by just over 1l in an allowance race four weeks ago at Keeneland when dropped back to this trip on debut for this trainer; searching for her fourth career win; moves up into an allowance race.
runners.comment: On target last month; searching for her fourth career win.

runners.name: Creed's Gold
runners.spotlight: A Grade 3 winner in July and admirable career strike-rate (five from 13 to date); sound effort when 28-1 second at Keeneland last month in a Grade 2; should get involved.
runners.comment: No surprise to see her go well; sound effort last month.

runners.name: Shoot It True
runners.spotlight: Minor honours in an allowance race last month (4-9) behind Buttercream Babe (Keeneland); consistent performer; has chalked up three wins from her last six; moves up into an allowance race.
runners.comment: Beaten into second when 4-9 last month; on target three times in her last six outings.
runners.owner: Ice Wine Stable & Smart Choice Stable

runners.name: Mae Town
runners.spotlight: Has won three of her five career starts and made it a hat-trick last time; narrow success in a stakes at Ellis Park in July; returns from a break today; steps up to allowance company.
runners.comment: Tight-margin winner in July; can boast winning form over this C&D.

races.race_name: Eight Belles Stakes Presented By Sysco (Grade 2) (Fillies) (Dirt)

runners.name: Paradise
runners.spotlight: Has made an impressive start to her career with two wins from four starts; things didn't pan out for her when favourite (10l third) over 1m1f at Aqueduct (allowance Grade 3) four weeks ago; down in trip here.
runners.comment: Bit disappointing four weeks ago; two from four in her career so far.

runners.name: Luv Your Neighbor
runners.spotlight: Has been fairly consistent of late; not beaten too far when third at Fair Grounds in a Grade 2 41 days ago; down in distance.
runners.comment: Not disgraced in third 41 days ago; has largely been consistent of late.

runners.name: Sippin Pretty
runners.spotlight: Bids for hat-trick and two from three in her career so far; was well on top at the line at Keeneland last month in an allowance optional claimer; covers an extra 1f here.
runners.comment: Won by 6l last month; winner of two of her three runs to date.
runners.owner: Six Column Stables LLC Et Al

runners.name: On Time Girl
runners.spotlight: Third when fancied (4-5) over 1m at Gulfstream Park (Grade 2) two months ago; unbeaten at this course after two runs and four from six in her career so far; definite chance based on the pick of her form.
runners.comment: Has won in classy company before and wouldn't rule out a big run.

runners.name: Front Runnin
runners.spotlight: Has achieved four wins from 11 career runs; finished fifth, beaten a fair way over 6f at Evangeline Downs (black type) last month on debut for this trainer; unchartered territory as she moves up in trip.
runners.comment: Held when fifth 28 days ago; has managed four wins from 11 career runs.
runners.owner: Gerald Bruno Jr, Carl J Deville Et Al

runners.name: French Friction
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in the Ashland Stakes upped to 1m1/2f (Keeneland) last month; has made a pleasing start to her career with two wins from three starts and has a nice pedigree (by City Of Light); this might be easier; she shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: She can make a bold bid; has made a pleasing start to her career with two wins from three starts.

runners.name: Solemn Vow
runners.spotlight: Behind Sippin Pretty over 6f at Keeneland (allowance optional claimer) when 6l runner-up last month; tackling an extra 1f now.
runners.comment: Not beaten too far four weeks ago; covers an extra 1f here.

runners.name: Goodall
runners.spotlight: Won a black type by 4 1/2l with River Wind and Front Runnin behind (Oaklawn Park) 48 days ago; this daughter of Yaupon looks the part on breeding and going for a hat-trick; more on her plate but has a shout.
runners.comment: More on her plate this time but expected to be in the thick of things.

runners.name: River Wind
runners.spotlight: Behind Goodall when runner-up (sent off 8-11) at Oaklawn Park 48 days ago in a black type; covers an extra 1f here.
runners.comment: Favourite when runner-up 48 days ago; covers an extra 1f here.
runners.owner: Turman Racing Stable LLC, Resilient Six Stables LLC & TJR Th

races.race_name: Alysheba Stakes Presented By U.S. Army (Grade 2) (Dirt)

runners.name: Skippylongstocking
runners.spotlight: Won by just over 5l over 1m1f at Oaklawn Park (handicap Grade 3) 41 days ago; a good record at this level and bidding for a four-timer; this represents a step up in class but wouldn't rule out a big run.
runners.comment: This represents a step up in class but should get involved.

runners.name: East Avenue
runners.spotlight: Close up runner-up five weeks ago behind Nu What's New at Oaklawn Park in an allowance Grade 3; a winner over C&D.
runners.comment: Not beaten far 34 days ago; has winning form over this course and distance on the CV.

runners.name: Navajo Warrior
runners.spotlight: Scored by a slim margin in an allowance optional claimer at Gulfstream Park 41 days ago; brings winning C&D form to the table; managed five wins in his last seven outings.
runners.comment: Won by slim margin six weeks ago; on target five times in his last seven outings.
runners.owner: Bag Racing Stables, Miller Racing Llc Et Al

runners.name: Corporate Power
runners.spotlight: Missed out by half a length at Fair Grounds six weeks ago in a Grade 2; managed two wins in his last six outings.
runners.comment: Just denied 41 days ago; managed two wins in his last six outings.

runners.name: Tappan Street
runners.spotlight: Out of the money and beaten 23l in the Pegasus World Cup at Gulfstream Park, finishing well behind reopposing Skippylongstocking in January; winner of three of his five career runs; this might be easier; form of the trainer is a plus; he should go close.
runners.comment: Has to be considered; three from five in his career so far
runners.owner: WinStar Farm LLC CHC Inc, Cold Press Racing & Qatar Racing

runners.name: Grande
runners.spotlight: Has won four of his five career runs and on a hat-trick; got home in front by three-quarters of a length (Gulfstream Park) 34 days ago in a Grade 3.
runners.comment: Tight-margin winner five weeks ago; going for a hat-trick.

runners.name: Nu What's New
runners.spotlight: Narrow success in an allowance Grade 3 (Oaklawn Park) five weeks ago; managed three wins in his last four outings
runners.comment: Won by slim margin five weeks ago; has chalked up three wins from his last four.

runners.name: Disruptor
runners.spotlight: Out to complete a hat-trick and three from five in his career so far; scored by a slim margin in an allowance Grade 3 (Tampa Bay Downs) two months ago.
runners.comment: Tight-margin winner two months ago; winner of three of his five runs to date.
runners.owner: Repole Stable, Spendthrift Farm LLC Et Al

runners.name: Baeza
runners.spotlight: Finished sixth in the Breeders' Cup Classic at Del Mar in October; ran well in a Grade 2 in July and claimed the Grade 1 Pennsylvania Derby in September; back from an absence here; might find this easier; excuses last time (brushed) and has to be high on list.
runners.comment: Excuses last time (brushed) and ought to be on the premises.
runners.owner: C R K Stable LLC & Grandview Equine

races.race_name: Modesty Stakes Presented By Veritas Prime (Grade 3) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Pin Up Betty
runners.spotlight: Consistent lately and good C&D record; not disgraced in the allowance Grade 1 Jenny Wiley Stakes at Keeneland three weeks ago; down in class; on the shortlist based on the pick of her form.
runners.comment: Has won in classy company before and has to be respected.

runners.name: Gezora
runners.spotlight: Achieved a narrow win in the Breeders' Cup F&M Turf at Del Mar six months ago; on target four times in nine runs when trained in France by Henri-Francois Graffard, earning decent prize-money; back from an absence here; drops in class; one to note.
runners.comment: Likely to be a threat; won Breeders' Cup F&M Turf six months ago.

runners.name: Kathynmarissa
runners.spotlight: Brings winning C&D form to the table; managed three wins in her last seven outings; won with something to spare in an allowance optional claimer at Saratoga on debut for new trainer in August; returns from a layoff here; chance despite having more on her plate.
runners.comment: This represents a step up in class but looks like a player.

runners.name: Whiskey Decision
runners.spotlight: Good effort when close up at Tampa Bay Downs two months ago in an allowance Grade 2; on target twice in her last five outings; producing consistent form; down in class.
runners.comment: Just touched off two months ago; on target twice in her last five outings.

runners.name: Proctor Street
runners.spotlight: Managed three wins in her last six outings; a winner over C&D; not far behind Whiskey Decision when third at Tampa Bay Downs two months ago in an allowance Grade 2; an easier task than her previous race; reunited with Tyler Gaffalione, the pair have built a good partnership; needs to be respected if replicating her best form and this step up in class unlikely to hinder her.
runners.comment: Should not be far away despite having more on her plate.

runners.name: Ramsey Pond
runners.spotlight: Beaten 2l 34 days ago when sent off 25-1 (Gulfstream Park) in an allowance Grade 3; has been in the winners' enclosure twice in her career; down in distance.
runners.comment: Creditable effort 34 days ago; searching for her third career win.

runners.name: Stylish Sue
runners.spotlight: Has winning track form; managed three wins in her last seven outings; ran out a ready winner at Turfway Park over 1m 1/2f (allowance listed) six weeks ago.
runners.comment: Won by just over 4l 41 days ago; track has proven suitable before.

runners.name: Dona Clota
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced with this just her fifth appearance; almost victorious in a Grade 3 in February; behind Ramsey Pond when last of seven five weeks ago (Gulfstream Park) in an allowance Grade 3; now tackles shorter distance; also engaged in race 6 at Belmont At The Big A.
runners.comment: Last of seven five weeks ago; lightly raced (running for just the fifth time).

runners.name: Queens Command
runners.spotlight: Is three from 20 in her career; knocking at the door recently; respectable effort to finish fourth, beaten 2l in an allowance listed (Gulfstream Park) 34 days ago.
runners.comment: Creditable effort 34 days ago; often been there or thereabouts lately.

runners.name: Adrasteia
runners.spotlight: Finished a decent third of 11 four months ago (Turfway Park) in a black type; running consistently; a winner over C&D; layoff to overcome; drops in trip.
runners.comment: Gave good account in December; string of consistent efforts of late.

races.race_name: Fasig-Tipton La Troienne Stakes (Grade 1) (Fillies & Mares) (Dirt)

runners.name: Fully Subscribed
runners.spotlight: Progressed well with experience in 2025, about 4l winner at Aqueduct of Grade 2 Mother Goose in November and Grade 3 Comely in December; clocked a notably fast time in latter, despite running bit wide; highly promising but all three wins were at Aqueduct and she comes off five-month layoff.
runners.comment: All three wins at Aqueduct, including Grade 2 and Grade 3; promising but five-month layoff

runners.name: Dry Powder
runners.spotlight: Beaten a neck by Clicquot in last year's Grade 1 Cotillion at Parx (8.5f); impressive winner of allowance race at Gulfstream latest, stumbling at the start but recovering to lead before turn for home and drawing 6l clear; solid claims on best form.
runners.comment: Close in a Grade 1 in 2025 and impressive in Gulfstream allowance latest; solid claims

runners.name: Nerazurri
runners.spotlight: Has generally plied her trade at a lower level, winning a pair of Oaklawn black-type races this year; never able to challenge in Grade 1 Apple Blossom there latest start, beaten 14l; place chance at best but easy to oppose.
runners.comment: Won a pair of Oaklawn black-type races but well held in Grade 1 there latest; more needed

runners.name: Scalable
runners.spotlight: Fluent winner at Aqueduct of black-type race and Listed event on last two starts; has generally struggled in better company than that since winning Grade 3 narrowly in 2024; in good form and won only start here but needs to improve.
runners.comment: Fluent winner of two lesser races of late but has generally struggled in better company

runners.name: Shred The Gnar
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and progressive winner of three races in 2025, notably Grade 3 Chilukki here in November; best when forcing strong pace but unable to dominate in Grade 3 Royal Delta at Gulfstream on return and well beaten; stays 8.5f; return to forcing tactics back here could see her go close (reportedly training well).
runners.comment: Made all for three 2025 wins, including Grade 3 here; chance, if able to dominate again
runners.owner: Flying Dutchmen Breeding And Racing LLC

runners.name: Take Charge Omaha
runners.spotlight: Four wins, but none outside maiden or allowance company; favourite, came from 5l off pace early to win by 2l at Keeneland latest start; in decent form, but lots more needed.
runners.comment: Four wins from off pace in maiden and allowance company; much more needed here

runners.name: Snowyte
runners.spotlight: Very well regarded as juvenile in 2024, when second in Grade 1 at Aqueduct; has shown flashes of same ability in lesser company since, making all in a maiden at Monmouth last August and an allowance at Gulfstream (by 11l in fast time) in February; beaten nearly 2l by Scalable at Aqueduct on latest start; talented but inconsistent.
runners.comment: Grade 1 second as 2yo in 2024; flashes since, but beaten nearly 2l by Scalable at Aqueduct

runners.name: Chasten
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced, well-bred filly; progressive in lesser company since winning maiden here in November 2024; workmanlike half-length winner of black-type event at Colonial Downs on latest start, but early pace was not strong enough for her to impress; strong finisher who is suited by this trip and could go well, granted good gallop; shapes as though having more ability than her form suggests; interesting.
runners.comment: Deep closer; won half her six (lesser) races, including here; could do a lot better

runners.name: Bless The Broken
runners.spotlight: In frame on all three Grade 1 starts, including when third in 2025 Kentucky Oaks over 1m1f here (Drexel Hill second); creditable 6l second in Beholder Mile at Santa Anita in March on latest outing, readily left behind by high-class Splendora; more needed, but she's having only her third start for Brad Cox and could improve.
runners.comment: In frame all three Grade 1 starts; more needed but having only third start for Brad Cox
runners.owner: Mountmellick Farm, Qatar Racing Llc And Hunter Valley Farm

runners.name: Drexel Hill
runners.spotlight: Best form when 32-1 runner-up here in 2025 Grade 1 Kentucky Oaks; won black-type event at Tampa Bay in January, then beaten 12l when fourth to subsequent Grade 1 Apple Blossom winner Claret Beret in Grade 3 Royal Delta at Gulfstream; inconsistent, but very useful on her day.
runners.comment: Has not matched second in 2025 Kentucky Oaks; 12l fourth in Grade 3 at Gulfstream latest

runners.name: Miss Justify
runners.spotlight: Five career wins from nine starts and unbeaten in two here in 2024 (allowance race and Listed event); raced just twice last season, winning an allowance before moderate fourth in Grade 2 at Saratoga; formerly trained by Todd Pletcher and stiff task for new barn today.
runners.comment: Won five, including two lesser races here in 2024; first start for 11 months and new barn

races.race_name: Premio Parioli (Italian 2000 Guineas) (Group 3) (Colts) (Turf)
Premio Regina Elena (Italian 1000 Guineas) (Group 3) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Regina Di Spade
runners.owner: Scuderia Giacobbe Ferruccio E C Sas

races.race_name: Prix du Muguet (Group 2) (Turf)

runners.name: Silius
runners.spotlight: Knows how to win, like his stablemate, and also like No Lunch has so far come up short in Pattern company, although his third in a recent C&D Group 3 on soft ground shows he's capable of landing one; genuinely good going is potentially an issue considering his winning form and Maxime Guyon appears to prefer the chances of No Lunch but Christophe Soumillon is a notable booking as his replacement.
runners.comment: Seems second string on jockey bookings but too classy at his best to ignore

runners.name: No Lunch
runners.spotlight: Won a lot of races, mostly on the AW, and comes into this in great heart but the concern is that he's not won above Listed level during his career, and was sixth in this 12 months ago; not discounted, though, considering his confidence is on a high and Maxime Guyon appears to have chosen him over Silius.
runners.comment: Not won above Listed level yet but confidence is on a high and Maxime Guyon's chosen mount

runners.name: Next Mine
runners.spotlight: Possesses a consistent profile and some smart form, most notably a win at this level at San Siro (1m, heavy) last November; posted an encouraging return at Dusseldorf 19 days ago and appears to handle good ground, so should put up a bolding showing; Alexis Pouchin rides for first time.
runners.comment: Only one of these to have won at this level in the past, so obvious claims

runners.name: Seagulls Eleven
runners.spotlight: Smart performer as a 2yo over 7f but a bit in-and-out last season, although his second to Opera Ballo in July looks really good form now; went on to land a Group 3 after that (1m, soft) and then ran creditably in a Group 1, but not been seen since finishing down the field in the massively valuable Golden Eagle in Australia during November; needs to bounce back but has the class to do so.
runners.comment: Absent since modest effort in Australia how G1 placed as 2yo, so has the class to feature
runners.owner: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four

runners.name: Trust On
runners.spotlight: Another who progressed with racing and beat Silius over C&D on heavy ground here in November, but was then beaten by that rival in March before a career-best 33 days ago, albeit behind Silius again, in a C&D Group 3; respected if he improved a bit more following that effort but this ground is an unknown for him.
runners.comment: Ran well last time, albeit behind Silius; needs more, though, and ground an unknown

runners.name: Tumbler
runners.spotlight: Fifth in this last year; won't lack for race-fitness having been on the go in the Middle East since late January at up to 1m1f, performing with plenty of credit in a couple of Group 2s and most recently a Group 1 behind Ombudsman; capable of being involved but hasn't won above handicap level yet; seems to handle most ground for all that his wins have come on softer.
runners.comment: won't lack for race-fitness having been on the go in the Middle East since late January
runners.owner: Scea Haras Des Forets, Regis Clarot Et Al

runners.name: Saidi El Fal
runners.spotlight: Mostly progressive performer throughout his career but hasn't been seen since a modest effort over C&D on very soft in a Group 3 during October; has run well in the past first time after a break and would hold place claims if anywhere the level he achieved when landing a hat-trick last campaign, including one success on good ground.
runners.comment: Needs improvement but can go well fresh; place chances if at his best

races.race_name: Prix Allez France (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Azaniya
runners.spotlight: Looks to be versatile with regards to underfoot conditions and ended 2025 on the up, winning back-to-back Listed events in France (one under Maxime Guyon), the last of which was here by over 4l on heavy ground; can race up with the pace or lead, so has the potential to dominate sectionals; stable in great form.
runners.comment: Stable in good form and should race on the front-end; interesting

runners.name: Cankoura
runners.spotlight: Developed into a high-class performer last year, placing third in the Group 1 Prix de Diane (1m2f 110yds, good to soft) before winning the Group 3 Prix de Psyche (1m2f, good) in August, when Zia Agnese was third; far from exposed on her 4yo return and trainer/owner already enjoying a fine season; holds a Group 1 entry.
runners.comment: Far from exposed on her 4yo return and trainer/owner already enjoying a fine season

runners.name: Sunly
runners.spotlight: Gave plenty of indications last year that she could be destined for top honours, starting her career with three wins before a narrow second in a 1m4f Group 2 at Longchamp; fascinating that she has been kept in training and reunited with Christophe Soumillon (who is 3-3 on her), so rates one of the key players if this trip doesn't prove to be too sharp at Pattern-race level; another with a Group 1 entry.
runners.comment: Highly promising last year and of firm interest if this trip doesn't prove too sharp

runners.name: Light The Ghost
runners.spotlight: Brought along steadily and rewarded connections patience with a Group 3 success here when last seen in October over a similar trip on very soft (Zia Agnese third that day); this looks a good starting point for the year and in the mix if handling the faster surface.
runners.comment: Won a Group 3 over a similar trip last year and in the mix if handles faster ground
runners.owner: Crispin De Moubray & M L P Consultancy

runners.name: Indalimos
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and improved throughout her 3yo campaign, which saw her land a 1m4f 110yd Group 3 at Deauville from a subsequent Group 1 winner; may not have stayed or liked the ground when sent to the top level after but made a nice start to this season over a similar trip at Longchamp, when Tajlina was a place behind; sound claims assuming normal progress.
runners.comment: Made a nice start to this season and sound claims assuming normal progress

runners.name: Tajlina
runners.spotlight: Notably a half-sister to dual Pattern-race winner and Group 1-placed Map Of Stars; ran well a couple of times last year at this level over similar trips and shaped with promise recently after a 352-day absence, so it'll be no surprise if she steps forward plenty from that; Group 1 entered and stable has two other leading contenders.
runners.comment: Not had much racing but has a classy pedigree; should benefit from recent return run

runners.name: South Bay
runners.spotlight: Won two of her five races, the most recent of which was a Listed event on the AW; never featured on her return to grass last time, albeit in a very good Group 3 here, but has plenty of scope for even better.
runners.comment: Has a good level of ability but needs to improve from most recent effort

runners.name: Zia Agnese
runners.spotlight: Smart filly, who has met a few of these in the past and is capable of a big effort on here day, as she showed when beating the odds-on Tajlina here last April, with Indalimos back in third; off since finishing third behind Light The Ghost in October, but no big surprise if she outruns her odds if showing up at her best.
runners.comment: Has met a few of these in the past and can outrun her odds at times

runners.name: Riyabovka
runners.spotlight: Smart mare at her peak and comes into this off a career-best in a Group 2 at Meydan in February when second over 1m1f on good ground; much more exposed than her rivals, though, so will want to repeat last time's level to have a chance of beating some of these.
runners.comment: New career best last time but more exposed than these

races.race_name: WETTSTAR.de - Bavarian Classic (Group 2) (Turf)
JCB Newmarket Stakes (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings)

runners.name: Poseidon's Warrior
runners.spotlight: Easy win at Goodwood (1m1f, good) last October on second start and he reappeared with a 1l Listed second here (1m1f, good) 16 days ago, staying on well; the first-time hood could help him settle better and he holds leading claims for top trainer who has won three of the last four runnings.
runners.comment: Stayed on well for 1m1f Listed second here on reappearance and holds leading claims

runners.name: My Love Is King
runners.spotlight: Won last November on sole 2yo start (7f, AW) and reappeared with a near-miss in a hot novice contest at Newbury (1m2f, good) a fortnight ago; this Dante/Derby entry is from a classy family and should continue to progress for his top stable; could have a part to play.
runners.comment: Went close in hot novice at Newbury on recent reappearance and could have a part to play

runners.name: Archers Bay
runners.spotlight: Ex-Ballydoyle colt who got off the mark in first-time cheekpieces (retained since) on his stable debut in a Wolverhampton novice (9.4f, AW) in December and followed up over this C&D (good) 16 days ago on his first handicap start; could continue to progress but does need another step forward today.
runners.comment: Made it 2-2 for Marco Botti with C&D handicap win; needs another step forward today

runners.name: Lyneham
runners.spotlight: Went close on debut at Doncaster (1m, soft) last September then kept on well to win a 1m maiden here (good) in October; this is a much stiffer test of his credentials but his dam is from a classy family and he's in excellent hands; entered in the Dante and Derby and could be set for big 3yo campaign.
runners.comment: 1m course maiden winner; in excellent hands and could be set for big 3yo campaign

runners.name: Ancient Egypt
runners.spotlight: Won fast-ground novice races at Beverley (7.4f) and Goodwood (1m) last summer on his first two starts; he's better than he showed when 14l seventh of eight in the Group 2 Royal Lodge here in September (1m, good to firm; 16-1) and has Dante/Derby entries but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: He's better than he showed in the Royal Lodge here but others have more pressing claims

races.race_name: Longines Sagaro Stakes (Group 3) (A Gold Cup Trial)

runners.name: Sweet William
runners.spotlight: Headgear and Rab Havlin all starts; not straightforward but he nearly always gives his running, including at this track; he did not have the top stayers to worry about last September when winning his second Doncaster Cup and the same seems to apply today, so he's a leading player.
runners.comment: Nearly always gives his running, Ascot included; does not have top stayers to worry about

runners.name: Caballo De Mar
runners.spotlight: Much improved last year, best effort to land the Group 1 Cadran at Longchamp (2m4f, soft) in October; close second in the Dubai Gold Cup at Meydan (2m, good) was a fine start to this campaign and his run on good to firm at Royal Ascot last June, although subsequently eclipsed, looked excellent at the time; needs respect but concedes weight all round.
runners.comment: Group 1 Cadran winner and close second in Dubai Gold Cup, but he concedes weight all round

runners.name: Tabletalk
runners.spotlight: No win since Melrose Handicap at York (1m6f, good to firm) in 2024 but he ran very well in 1m4f Group races on first three starts last season and returned to that form when a close second in Saudi Arabia (1m7f) 11 weeks ago; that puts him back in the picture even though he's unraced over this far.
runners.comment: No win since 2024; returned to form when close second in Saudi Arabia (1m7f) 11 weeks ago

runners.name: Tarriance
runners.spotlight: Won the Melrose Handicap at York (1m6f, good to firm) last August, before the St Leger proved too tough; gelded soon after and ran well when third in Saudi Arabia (1m7f) in February; runner-up Tabletalk conceded him 11lb that day and it's only 1lb today, but this 4yo should retain potential for 2m+.
runners.comment: Held by Tabletalk on Saudi Arabia form latest but this 4yo should retain potential for 2m+

runners.name: Ascending
runners.spotlight: Landed the Cork Derby (1m4f) last May and a Royal Ascot handicap (2m4f) last June, before his excellent second in the Ebor Handicap at York (1m6f) in August; not disgraced on final start but needs a serious advance to mix it with the best of these in today's Group 3.
runners.comment: Fine season in 2025 handicaps; serious advance needed against best rivals in this Group 3

runners.name: Miss Alpilles
runners.spotlight: Won 1m6f handicap at Goodwood (good to firm) last August and a first crack at 2m suited her even better when she led over 1f out to score at 20-1 in a Listed race at Newmarket (good) in September; today's Group 3 should demand plenty more but she could still have untapped potential at 2m+.
runners.comment: First crack at 2m was 20-1 win in a Listed race at Newmarket in September; potential still

runners.name: Deira Mile
runners.spotlight: A novice event remains his only win from ten races but he was fourth in the 2024 Derby and St Leger; well held in Saudi Arabia and Dubai on his only two starts last year, left Owen Burrows for 16,000gns last October and gelded last month; some caution has to be advised.
runners.comment: 4th in the 2024 Derby and St Leger; left O Burrows for 16,000gns last October and gelded

runners.name: Dubai Future
runners.spotlight: Has delivered smart efforts almost every season since 2020 (missed 2024) and last year's win in the 2m Dubai Gold Cup at Meydan was one of his best; that campaign went the wrong way and he did not perk up when returning for Dubai Gold Cup (20-1) five weeks ago, but holds a cracking chance if returning to his best.
runners.comment: 2025 went wrong way; below form in Dubai Gold Cup five weeks ago but won it last year

races.race_name: Betfred "The Classic Bookmaker" Handicap

runners.name: St Anton
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on final two of his three 2yo starts (7f/1m, good to soft/good) and improved form on recent seasonal/handicap debut when close third over C&D (good); well regarded and open to further improvement; one to consider.
runners.comment: Close third in C&D handicap on reappearance and he's open to further improvement

runners.name: Lake Como
runners.spotlight: Won Newcastle novice (7f, AW) in December on fourth start and followed up in four-runner nursery at Southwell (1m, AW); returned from break with 50-1 third in warm conditions race at Kempton (1m, AW) in March, when conceding weight all round, and on that evidence he looks well treated off an unchanged mark; down the field on sole turf start but that was his debut last June.
runners.comment: Third in warm conditions race at Kempton and looks well treated off an unchanged mark

runners.name: Comic Hero
runners.spotlight: Went close in two fast-ground novice races last summer and returned with a novice win at Kempton (7f, AW) in February; 15-8 favourite and denied a clear run when 2l fifth of six on handicap debut at Musselburgh (1m, good to soft) four weeks ago; retains potential and is not ruled out but he lost places close home once in the clear at Musselburgh and has to raise his game.
runners.comment: Retains potential but lost places once in the clear at Musselburgh & has to raise his game

runners.name: Zennor Storm
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Kempton (7f, AW) last October and followed up there (1m, AW) in December; his opening mark is no gift but it remains to be seen where his limitations lie and he's respected for his top yard on turf debut.
runners.comment: 2-2, both AW; his opening mark is no gift but remains to be seen where his limitations lie

runners.name: Royal Bodyguard
runners.spotlight: Ran seven times last year and sole win came in four-runner nursery at Kempton (1m, AW); also effective on turf and gelded since last seen; however, unless he finds improvement he's vulnerable to less-exposed types.
runners.comment: One win (1m, AW) from his seven 2yo starts; looks vulnerable to less-exposed types

runners.name: Vincenzo Peruggia
runners.spotlight: Won at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm) last July on second start then well beaten in Group 2 at Goodwood (7f, good) later that month; reappeared with close second of six in handicap at Musselburgh (1m, good to soft) four weeks ago but it's likely he needs a much bigger run in this hot contest.
runners.comment: Reappeared with good second at Musselburgh but likely needs bigger run in this hot contest

runners.name: Fort Rock
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f on Kempton AW last autumn on his first two starts; those were narrow victories and improvement is needed on this handicap debut if he's to remain unbeaten, but he could have plenty more to offer for his top trainer, especially over this extra furlong.
runners.comment: 2-2, both narrow wins on AW, but could have plenty more to offer for his top trainer

runners.name: Objector
runners.spotlight: 3l win on debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in December and had 11l to spare when 1-8 there in January (8.6f); faces much tougher calibre of opponent on this first handicap start but he's brother to a Group 3 winner and his trainer is eyeing up a Royal Ascot handicap; could give a good account.
runners.comment: 2-2, easily in AW novices races; trainer eyeing up Royal Ascot handicap; could go well

runners.name: Elan d'Or
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at the eighth attempt in Chelmsford nursery (7f, AW) last November on his second AW start, and he again had plenty in hand when following up at Kempton (1m, AW); needs to transfer that improvement back to turf but he did show promise on grass last season; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Things very much clicked on AW at end of 2025; needs to transfer improvement back to turf

races.race_name: Commonwealth Cup Trial Stakes (Registered As The Pavilion Stakes) (Group 3)

runners.name: Wise Approach
runners.spotlight: On the up when last seen, rounding off his 2yo campaign with a comfortable come-from-behind win in the Middle Park in September (6f, good to firm); may confirm his superiority over Brussels and Coppull despite the weights turnaround and he is not fully exposed just yet.
runners.comment: Picked up well to win the Middle Park when last seen; penalised but not fully exposed

runners.name: Brussels
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f maiden at the Curragh on debut before a string of good efforts in defeat in good races, notably second-place finishes in the Middle Park (0.75l behind Wise Approach and 5lb better off) and the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint; should be in the shake-up.
runners.comment: Twice 2nd in Group 1 company as a 2yo; should be in the shake-up under suitable conditions

runners.name: Ardisia
runners.spotlight: Progressive as a 2yo, winning five times and also second of 11 in the 2yo race over C&D on Champions Day; should appreciate the return to a stiff 6f having weakened over 7f in the Greenham two weeks ago; one to consider.
runners.comment: Five wins as a 2yo; more exposed than his rivals but still likely to give a good account

runners.name: Division
runners.spotlight: Promise on good to firm on debut; won his next three starts, two novice events and a York Listed race, before pulling his chance away over C&D on Champions Day; improvement required now upped to a Group 3 but he's in excellent hands and still has some potential.
runners.comment: Pulled his chance away over C&D last October; had won 3 times beforehand, including Listed

runners.name: Ghost Mode
runners.spotlight: Five runs as a 2yo; made debut over 6f but campaigned at 7f afterwards, running away with a maiden at Chester (good to soft) before a good second in a York sales race last October; absent for five months prior to a facile success in a 6f handicap at Southwell in March, racing freely and hanging left in the straight but in a different class to some fair handicappers; in much deeper today and looks to face a stiff task at the weights but the Southwell performance smacked of a hugely talented, if slightly wayward, colt and this stiff 6f should suit; unraced on faster than good.
runners.comment: Left 2yo efforts behind with striking, if erratic, Southwell win in March; unexposed at 6f

runners.name: Coppull
runners.spotlight: Third in the Coventry Stakes over C&D before winning the Group 2 Richmond Stakes at Glorious Goodwood (6f, good); rounded off his juvenile campaign with a close third in the Group 1 Middle Park Stakes (6f, good to firm), finishing on the heels of the reopposing Wise Approach and Brussels; stable won this race with Jasour, also a Group 2 winner as a 2yo, in 2024; one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Group 2 winner as a 2yo; good third behind two of these in the Middle Park when last seen

runners.name: Crown Relic
runners.spotlight: All races at 7f, winning a maiden and a novice at Wolverhampton in good style before his creditable fifth in a Deauville Group 3 last month (good to soft); drops in trip with a tongue-tie added for a trainer who this in 2020 and 2023.
runners.comment: Two 7f AW wins before a good 5th in a French Group 3; down in trip with tongue-tie added

runners.name: Midnight Tango
runners.spotlight: Ran well in Listed/Group 3 company last summer following her maiden win at Hamilton and her two lesser efforts at the end of the campaign can both be excused; this free-going filly shouldn't mind the return to sprinting but she will need to raise her game a notch.
runners.comment: Useful 2yo whose final two runs last year can both be excused; others bring stronger form

races.race_name: Betfred Jockey Club Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: Eydon
runners.spotlight: Had 1m4f wins last season in a Group 3 at Newbury (good to firm) in May and Group 2 in Italy (good; made all for career-best form) in October; tailed off in Hong Kong on final start but has a shout if back to his best.
runners.comment: Tailed off in Hong Kong on final start last term but has a shout if back to his best

runners.name: Sunway
runners.spotlight: Peak efforts were as a 3yo but reappearance at Meydan this January saw his first win since 2023; nearly won another 1m6f Group 3 there next time, before a headstrong display (set up big lead) saw him tail off in 2m Dubai Gold Cup five weeks ago; the latter was in blinkers (first time since September) which are unsurprisingly left off today; thereabouts if bouncing back.
runners.comment: Headstrong with blinkers over 2m latest start, after two solid 1m6f Group 3 runs at Meydan
runners.owner: Guy Pariente Holding & Qatar Racing Ltd

runners.name: Santorini Star
runners.spotlight: Upped to 2m for a handicap win at York Ebor meeting (good to firm) last August; tried front-running to positive effect against her own sex over about 1m6f on her subsequent two starts when winning the Group 2 Park Hill at Doncaster (good to soft) and second in Group 1 at Longchamp (very soft); has a form chance on this return but the stayer's mantle will have to be shed.
runners.comment: Progressive 2025; form chance on this return but the stayer's mantle will have to be shed

runners.name: Paradias
runners.spotlight: In the form of his life this winter with a win at Lingfield (1m4f) in February and close calls in his other three outings; those were on AW, which does seem to suit him particularly well, and also in handicaps, which leaves him with something to find in his first Group venture.
runners.comment: In the form of his life this winter but that was on AW and in handicaps; first Group race

runners.name: French Master
runners.spotlight: Won two 1m6f handicaps last term, latterly in taking style from off the pace at Royal Ascot (good to firm) when blinkered first time; slowly away on both subsequent starts, looking as if he was stretched by 2m in the Group 1 Goodwood Cup on first occasion (creditable) but tailed-off second favourite in the Ebor last time (visored; raced wide and made most); back to 1m4f, with headgear removed and needs overall improvement but the gelding operation may have helped.
runners.comment: Back down to 1m4f, headgear removed and needs overall improvement; gelding may have helped

runners.name: Lion's Pride
runners.spotlight: 7l Listed win over C&D (good to firm) last September; there are also plenty of lesser shows to be considered and, after he was not discredited in Qatar in February, he managed only fourth of six when favourite for Group 3 at Newbury 13 days ago; in the mix on peak efforts.
runners.comment: Plenty of lesser shows but in the mix on peak efforts, including 7l Listed win over C&D

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Daisy Warwick Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Crepe Suzette
runners.spotlight: Improved by the run last season and ended with an excellent third of 11 in Doncaster's Group 2 Park Hill Stakes in September; yard took this 12 months ago and she's very much the one to beat.
runners.comment: Ended season with excellent third in Doncaster's Park Hill; very much the one to beat

runners.name: Galaxy Star
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced filly who showed fair winning form in bumpers before making a successful Flat debut in 1m3f maiden at Kempton in March; capable of better but this demands considerable improvement.
runners.comment: Made a successful Flat debut in Kempton maiden; considerably more is needed however

runners.name: Incensed
runners.spotlight: Underwent breathing surgery before making an encouraging return from six months off when runner-up in 1m4f Kempton handicap last month; this fairly useful 1m4f winner can take a step forward now.
runners.comment: Encouraging return with runner-up at Kempton; can take a step forward now

runners.name: Ryka
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed an excellent 2025 and she signed off with a 1m4f listed success at Toulouse in October; remains quite low mileage, so can do better still this season.
runners.comment: Signed off for 2025 with listed Toulouse win; remains quite low mileage, so can do better

runners.name: Coedana
runners.spotlight: Proved most progressive last season when a dual 1m4f AW handicap scorer in the autumn; steps up in grade here but she's capable of further improvement.
runners.comment: Proved very progressive last season; steps up in grade but capable of further improvement
runners.owner: Julie And David R Martin And Dan Hall

runners.name: Kissmehoneyhoney
runners.spotlight: Crystal Ocean filly who looked a good prospect when making a winning start in 11-runner maiden at Leicester (1m2f, good to soft) in September; takes a big step up in grade but this longer trip promises to suit her well, so she shouldn't be passed over at all lightly for leading yard.
runners.comment: Looked good prospect with Leicester debut win; shouldn't be passed over at all lightly

runners.name: Tattycoram
runners.spotlight: Largely progressive sort (1m2f winner) last season but she wasn't seen out after posting just a fair fifth of seven in 1m6f Newmarket handicap in August; this demands a clear personal best after eight months off the track.
runners.comment: Largely progressive last season; this demands a clear personal best after eight months off

runners.name: Miss Wong
runners.spotlight: Looked firmly on the up when landing a pair of handicaps (around 1m3f) at Kempton and Yarmouth last autumn but she ended 2025 on a low note when tailed off in the St Simon Stakes at Newbury; this is no easy reappearance.
runners.comment: Tailed off in St Simon Stakes at Newbury on her final run; this is no easy reappearance

runners.name: Crystal Flyer
runners.spotlight: Winless in 2025 for Tom Clover but she largely performed with credit; changed hands for 70,000 guineas subsequently but this C&D scorer faces no easy task on her seasonal/yard debut.
runners.comment: Winless in 2025 for Tom Clover; this C&D scorer faces no easy task on seasonal/yard debut
runners.owner: Castle Racing Co Ltd & Mps Racing Ltd

races.race_name: Betfred "Nifty 50" Handicap

runners.name: Golden Redemption
runners.spotlight: Placed on all four handicap starts last year (7f/6f, good to firm/good), on the final occasion keeping on for 1l third here when the drop back to 6f looked against him; returned with solid third at Kempton (7f, AW) in March; major player for trainer who has won two of the last four runnings.
runners.comment: Admirably consistent in h'caps, including here; conditions should be right up his street

runners.name: Billyjoh
runners.spotlight: His losing sequence goes back 22 races to February 2024; however, he's often run well in the interim and he's been given a bit of help from the handicapper ahead of this reappearance; can be in the mix if fully tuned up.
runners.comment: On a long losing sequence but often runs well; can be in the mix if fully tuned up

runners.name: Elarak
runners.spotlight: Made it 3-6 when winning a C&D handicap (good to firm) last September, making virtually all; gelded subsequently; reappeared with close fourth of eight in conditions race at Thirsk (7f, good) three weeks ago and could show the benefit of that outing today; this 4yo is firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Won C&D h'cap last autumn; this lightly raced 4yo could build on his recent comeback run

runners.name: Benacre
runners.spotlight: Close third from the front at Musselburgh (7f, good) on April 12 and didn't lead when drawn wide and fifth of 11 there (7f, good) last Sunday; could give a good account if usual prominent tactics are applied but there's a suspicion he's vulnerable up in grade.
runners.comment: Close third at Musselburgh and drawn wide there since; might be vulnerable up in grade

runners.name: Silver Ghost
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good) last May on her second start of last season and followed up at Goodwood (7f, good) later that month; she failed to kick on subsequently but perhaps a break will have helped and she's not discounted on her reappearance.
runners.comment: Won two in a row last May (including C&D) but she failed to kick on subsequently

runners.name: Leadman
runners.spotlight: He has a modest strike-rate for one of his ability (2-19) and needs everything to drop right; however, he's well treated on last year's best efforts and may have needed his recent reappearance run at Thirsk; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Modest strike-rate for one of his ability but he's on a handy and is not ruled out

runners.name: Misunderstood
runners.spotlight: Won 1m 2yo Group 3 at Longchamp in 2024; reappeared with good third in Group 3 there last April; form went the wrong way subsequently and he changed hands for 37,000euros last November; gelded since; however, his new yard does extremely well with French recruits and this 4yo has an attractive mark to work with if tapping back into his historic promise; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Lost his form in France but new trainer does very well with French recruits; watch betting

races.race_name: Edgewood Stakes Presented By Accenture (Grade 2) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Taken By The Wind
runners.owner: Magdalena Racing, Terry Bradshaw Et Al

races.race_name: Longines Kentucky Oaks (Grade 1) (Fillies) (Dirt)

runners.name: Prom Queen
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner at Gulfstream Park of maiden in February (by 8l) and Grade 2 Gulfstream Park Oaks (8.5f; beat My Miss Mo by about 3l in steadily run race); travelled strongly, made eye-catching move on turn and not at all hard ridden to assert in latter race, scoring with lots in hand while still a bit green; should be suited by 1m1f; much stiffer test and must adapt to big field, but open to considerable improvement and an exciting prospect.
runners.comment: Big move on turn to win 8.5f Grade 2 at Gulfstream on only third start; exciting prospect

runners.name: Always A Runner
runners.spotlight: Most promising winner of both starts in a maiden at Tampa Bay in February (by just over 6l) and Grade 3 Gazelle Stakes (1m1f) at Aqueduct in April; always going well but had a bit to do on latter occasion, going on really strongly to beat Pashmina by just over 1l, well on top at finish; stays well (will likely have to come from some way back) and is very good prospect.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in two and stayed on strongly to win Grade 3 at Aqueduct (1m1f) by 1l; promising
runners.owner: Douglas Scharbauer & Three Chimneys Farm LLC

runners.name: Counting Stars
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner of four races, notably Grade 2 Fantasy Stakes (well placed, beat Search Party by 5l) at Oaklawn Park last time out; possibly unlucky when just under 1l second to Explora in Grade 3 Honeybee there on penultimate start, plenty to do off moderate fractions; strong claims on first start at 1m1f.
runners.comment: Easy Grade 2 winner latest; looked unlucky when second to Explora time before; good chance

runners.name: Percy's Bar
runners.spotlight: Demoted to second in Grade 1 last year, when also third in Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies despite being hampered; quickened through inside gap, got first run, when beating Zany by about 2l in Grade 1 Ashland at Keeneland (8.5f) on reappearance; others may improve more at 1m1f but she possesses impressive tactical speed.
runners.comment: Breeders' Cup third; got first run, beat Zany 2l in 8.5f Grade 1 at Keeneland; good chance

runners.name: Explora
runners.spotlight: High-class juvenile, easy winner of Grade 2 Oak Leaf at Santa Anita and good second in Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies at Del Mar; winner of two of three starts this year, on latest start beating Counting Stars by just under 1l in Grade 3 Honeybee at Oaklawn; should stay 1m1f and is a tractable, focused runner, but has a two-month layoff (missed intended prep) to overcome.
runners.comment: Grade 2 win in 2025 and beat Counting Stars in Grade 3 at Oaklawn; player but 2 months off
runners.owner: Michael E Pegram, Karl Watson & Paul Weitman

runners.name: Meaning
runners.spotlight: Winner of three of four starts, notably Grade 2 Santa Anita Oaks by 2l from Brooklyn Blonde, tracking pace and asserting steadily in straight; only defeat when 4l fourth to Super Corredora in Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies at Del Mar, where behind Explora and Percy's Bar; solid place claims.
runners.comment: Behind Explora and Percy's Bar when fourth at Breeders' Cup but Grade 2 win latest; claims

runners.name: Pashmina
runners.spotlight: In frame in black-type races three times after winning a maiden at Fair Grounds by 10l; very good second, beaten only around 1l and well clear or rest, behind Always A Runner in Grade 3 Gazelle at Aqueduct (1m1f) last time, when she looked to be getting away from eventual winner but hopped tracks in straight; smart filly, bit more exposed than some, but an interesting outsider.
runners.comment: In frame in black-type races and Grade 3 (improved form); smart but bit exposed

runners.name: Resist
runners.spotlight: Reserve who now gets in; progressive form at lower level; best effort when about 2l third (Lovely Grey second) to Lorelei Lee in Listed race at Turfway Park last time; very stiff task.
runners.comment: Very stiff task based on third in Listed race at Turfway Park on latest start

runners.name: Zany
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner of both starts last year in maiden at Gulfstream Park and Grade 2 Demoiselle at Aqueduct in December; landed the odds in Listed race at Tampa in February; unsuited by slow pace, caught wide and stayed on well late when about 2l second to Percy's Bar in Grade 1 Ashland at Keeneland last month (again edged left in straight); will relish first run beyond 8.5f; serious claims.
runners.comment: Grade 2 winner in 2025 caught eye when second to Percy's Bar at Keeneland; serious claims

runners.name: Search Party
runners.spotlight: Useful form at Oaklawn to win maiden (by 6l) last December and Listed race (by neck) in February; in frame there since, unable to get on terms, when fourth to Explora in Grade 3 Honeybee and second to Counting Stars in Grade 2 Fantasy; will be suited by further than 8.5f but something to find.
runners.comment: Listed winner but never on terms when in frame in Graded company at Oaklawn; needs more

runners.name: Brooklyn Blonde
runners.spotlight: First race outside maiden company (winner at Santa Anita in March) when 2l second to Meaning in 8.5f Grade 2 Santa Anita Oaks last month, making an effort to challenge in straight but brushed aside; should stay 1m1f but needs to find a good bit more.
runners.comment: Good 2l second to Meaning in 8.5f Grade 2 at Santa Anita, brushed aside; needs to improve

runners.name: Lovely Grey
runners.spotlight: Reserve who now gets in; beaten on all five starts outside maidens and yet to tackle a Graded race; very stiff task.
runners.comment: Beaten all five starts outside maidens and yet to run in a Graded race; very stiff task

runners.name: Nycon
runners.spotlight: Reserve who now gets in; runner-up in black-type race and Listed event this year but well held when fourth behind Always A Runner in Grade 3 Gazelle at Aqueduct; lots to find.
runners.comment: Well held when 10l fourth to Always A Runner in Grade 3 Gazelle at Aqueduct; lots to find

runners.name: Dazzling Dame
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has won four races, notably black-type race at Colonial Downs in March; well held on her only go in better company when beaten 10l in Grade 3 at Churchill Downs last September; would be a shock winner against this quality of opponent.
runners.comment: Four lesser wins, notably black-type race; well held in a Grade 3 race; outsider
runners.owner: Respect The Valleys LLC, Medallion Racing & Madaket Stables

races.race_name: JCB Newmarket Stakes (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings)

runners.name: Poseidon's Warrior
runners.spotlight: Easy win at Goodwood (1m1f, good) last October on second start and he reappeared with a 1l Listed second here (1m1f, good) 16 days ago, staying on well; the first-time hood could help him settle better and he holds leading claims for top trainer who has won three of the last four runnings.
runners.comment: Stayed on well for 1m1f Listed second here on reappearance and holds leading claims

runners.name: My Love Is King
runners.spotlight: Won last November on sole 2yo start (7f, AW) and reappeared with a near-miss in a hot novice contest at Newbury (1m2f, good) a fortnight ago; this Dante/Derby entry is from a classy family and should continue to progress for his top stable; could have a part to play.
runners.comment: Went close in hot novice at Newbury on recent reappearance and could have a part to play

runners.name: Archers Bay
runners.spotlight: Ex-Ballydoyle colt who got off the mark in first-time cheekpieces (retained since) on his stable debut in a Wolverhampton novice (9.4f, AW) in December and followed up over this C&D (good) 16 days ago on his first handicap start; could continue to progress but does need another step forward today.
runners.comment: Made it 2-2 for Marco Botti with C&D handicap win; needs another step forward today

runners.name: Lyneham
runners.spotlight: Went close on debut at Doncaster (1m, soft) last September then kept on well to win a 1m maiden here (good) in October; this is a much stiffer test of his credentials but his dam is from a classy family and he's in excellent hands; entered in the Dante and Derby and could be set for big 3yo campaign.
runners.comment: 1m course maiden winner; in excellent hands and could be set for big 3yo campaign

runners.name: Ancient Egypt
runners.spotlight: Won fast-ground novice races at Beverley (7.4f) and Goodwood (1m) last summer on his first two starts; he's better than he showed when 14l seventh of eight in the Group 2 Royal Lodge here in September (1m, good to firm; 16-1) and has Dante/Derby entries but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: He's better than he showed in the Royal Lodge here but others have more pressing claims

races.race_name: Longines Sagaro Stakes (Group 3) (A Gold Cup Trial)

runners.name: Sweet William
runners.spotlight: Headgear and Rab Havlin all starts; not straightforward but he nearly always gives his running, including at this track; he did not have the top stayers to worry about last September when winning his second Doncaster Cup and the same seems to apply today, so he's a leading player.
runners.comment: Nearly always gives his running, Ascot included; does not have top stayers to worry about

runners.name: Caballo De Mar
runners.spotlight: Much improved last year, best effort to land the Group 1 Cadran at Longchamp (2m4f, soft) in October; close second in the Dubai Gold Cup at Meydan (2m, good) was a fine start to this campaign and his run on good to firm at Royal Ascot last June, although subsequently eclipsed, looked excellent at the time; needs respect but concedes weight all round.
runners.comment: Group 1 Cadran winner and close second in Dubai Gold Cup, but he concedes weight all round

runners.name: Tabletalk
runners.spotlight: No win since Melrose Handicap at York (1m6f, good to firm) in 2024 but he ran very well in 1m4f Group races on first three starts last season and returned to that form when a close second in Saudi Arabia (1m7f) 11 weeks ago; that puts him back in the picture even though he's unraced over this far.
runners.comment: No win since 2024; returned to form when close second in Saudi Arabia (1m7f) 11 weeks ago

runners.name: Tarriance
runners.spotlight: Won the Melrose Handicap at York (1m6f, good to firm) last August, before the St Leger proved too tough; gelded soon after and ran well when third in Saudi Arabia (1m7f) in February; runner-up Tabletalk conceded him 11lb that day and it's only 1lb today, but this 4yo should retain potential for 2m+.
runners.comment: Held by Tabletalk on Saudi Arabia form latest but this 4yo should retain potential for 2m+

runners.name: Ascending
runners.spotlight: Landed the Cork Derby (1m4f) last May and a Royal Ascot handicap (2m4f) last June, before his excellent second in the Ebor Handicap at York (1m6f) in August; not disgraced on final start but needs a serious advance to mix it with the best of these in today's Group 3.
runners.comment: Fine season in 2025 handicaps; serious advance needed against best rivals in this Group 3

runners.name: Miss Alpilles
runners.spotlight: Won 1m6f handicap at Goodwood (good to firm) last August and a first crack at 2m suited her even better when she led over 1f out to score at 20-1 in a Listed race at Newmarket (good) in September; today's Group 3 should demand plenty more but she could still have untapped potential at 2m+.
runners.comment: First crack at 2m was 20-1 win in a Listed race at Newmarket in September; potential still

runners.name: Deira Mile
runners.spotlight: A novice event remains his only win from ten races but he was fourth in the 2024 Derby and St Leger; well held in Saudi Arabia and Dubai on his only two starts last year, left Owen Burrows for 16,000gns last October and gelded last month; some caution has to be advised.
runners.comment: 4th in the 2024 Derby and St Leger; left O Burrows for 16,000gns last October and gelded

runners.name: Dubai Future
runners.spotlight: Has delivered smart efforts almost every season since 2020 (missed 2024) and last year's win in the 2m Dubai Gold Cup at Meydan was one of his best; that campaign went the wrong way and he did not perk up when returning for Dubai Gold Cup (20-1) five weeks ago, but holds a cracking chance if returning to his best.
runners.comment: 2025 went wrong way; below form in Dubai Gold Cup five weeks ago but won it last year

races.race_name: Prix du Muguet (Group 2) (Turf)

runners.name: Silius
runners.spotlight: Knows how to win, like his stablemate, and also like No Lunch has so far come up short in Pattern company, although his third in a recent C&D Group 3 on soft ground shows he's capable of landing one; genuinely good going is potentially an issue considering his winning form and Maxime Guyon appears to prefer the chances of No Lunch but Christophe Soumillon is a notable booking as his replacement.
runners.comment: Seems second string on jockey bookings but too classy at his best to ignore

runners.name: No Lunch
runners.spotlight: Won a lot of races, mostly on the AW, and comes into this in great heart but the concern is that he's not won above Listed level during his career, and was sixth in this 12 months ago; not discounted, though, considering his confidence is on a high and Maxime Guyon appears to have chosen him over Silius.
runners.comment: Not won above Listed level yet but confidence is on a high and Maxime Guyon's chosen mount

runners.name: Next Mine
runners.spotlight: Possesses a consistent profile and some smart form, most notably a win at this level at San Siro (1m, heavy) last November; posted an encouraging return at Dusseldorf 19 days ago and appears to handle good ground, so should put up a bolding showing; Alexis Pouchin rides for first time.
runners.comment: Only one of these to have won at this level in the past, so obvious claims

runners.name: Seagulls Eleven
runners.spotlight: Smart performer as a 2yo over 7f but a bit in-and-out last season, although his second to Opera Ballo in July looks really good form now; went on to land a Group 3 after that (1m, soft) and then ran creditably in a Group 1, but not been seen since finishing down the field in the massively valuable Golden Eagle in Australia during November; needs to bounce back but has the class to do so.
runners.comment: Absent since modest effort in Australia how G1 placed as 2yo, so has the class to feature
runners.owner: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four

runners.name: Trust On
runners.spotlight: Another who progressed with racing and beat Silius over C&D on heavy ground here in November, but was then beaten by that rival in March before a career-best 33 days ago, albeit behind Silius again, in a C&D Group 3; respected if he improved a bit more following that effort but this ground is an unknown for him.
runners.comment: Ran well last time, albeit behind Silius; needs more, though, and ground an unknown

runners.name: Tumbler
runners.spotlight: Fifth in this last year; won't lack for race-fitness having been on the go in the Middle East since late January at up to 1m1f, performing with plenty of credit in a couple of Group 2s and most recently a Group 1 behind Ombudsman; capable of being involved but hasn't won above handicap level yet; seems to handle most ground for all that his wins have come on softer.
runners.comment: won't lack for race-fitness having been on the go in the Middle East since late January
runners.owner: Scea Haras Des Forets, Regis Clarot Et Al

runners.name: Saidi El Fal
runners.spotlight: Mostly progressive performer throughout his career but hasn't been seen since a modest effort over C&D on very soft in a Group 3 during October; has run well in the past first time after a break and would hold place claims if anywhere the level he achieved when landing a hat-trick last campaign, including one success on good ground.
runners.comment: Needs improvement but can go well fresh; place chances if at his best

races.race_name: WETTSTAR.de - Bavarian Classic (Group 2) (Turf)
Premio Parioli (Italian 2000 Guineas) (Group 3) (Colts) (Turf)
Commonwealth Cup Trial Stakes (Registered As The Pavilion Stakes) (Group 3)

runners.name: Wise Approach
runners.spotlight: On the up when last seen, rounding off his 2yo campaign with a comfortable come-from-behind win in the Middle Park in September (6f, good to firm); may confirm his superiority over Brussels and Coppull despite the weights turnaround and he is not fully exposed just yet.
runners.comment: Picked up well to win the Middle Park when last seen; penalised but not fully exposed

runners.name: Brussels
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f maiden at the Curragh on debut before a string of good efforts in defeat in good races, notably second-place finishes in the Middle Park (0.75l behind Wise Approach and 5lb better off) and the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint; should be in the shake-up.
runners.comment: Twice 2nd in Group 1 company as a 2yo; should be in the shake-up under suitable conditions

runners.name: Ardisia
runners.spotlight: Progressive as a 2yo, winning five times and also second of 11 in the 2yo race over C&D on Champions Day; should appreciate the return to a stiff 6f having weakened over 7f in the Greenham two weeks ago; one to consider.
runners.comment: Five wins as a 2yo; more exposed than his rivals but still likely to give a good account

runners.name: Division
runners.spotlight: Promise on good to firm on debut; won his next three starts, two novice events and a York Listed race, before pulling his chance away over C&D on Champions Day; improvement required now upped to a Group 3 but he's in excellent hands and still has some potential.
runners.comment: Pulled his chance away over C&D last October; had won 3 times beforehand, including Listed

runners.name: Ghost Mode
runners.spotlight: Five runs as a 2yo; made debut over 6f but campaigned at 7f afterwards, running away with a maiden at Chester (good to soft) before a good second in a York sales race last October; absent for five months prior to a facile success in a 6f handicap at Southwell in March, racing freely and hanging left in the straight but in a different class to some fair handicappers; in much deeper today and looks to face a stiff task at the weights but the Southwell performance smacked of a hugely talented, if slightly wayward, colt and this stiff 6f should suit; unraced on faster than good.
runners.comment: Left 2yo efforts behind with striking, if erratic, Southwell win in March; unexposed at 6f

runners.name: Coppull
runners.spotlight: Third in the Coventry Stakes over C&D before winning the Group 2 Richmond Stakes at Glorious Goodwood (6f, good); rounded off his juvenile campaign with a close third in the Group 1 Middle Park Stakes (6f, good to firm), finishing on the heels of the reopposing Wise Approach and Brussels; stable won this race with Jasour, also a Group 2 winner as a 2yo, in 2024; one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Group 2 winner as a 2yo; good third behind two of these in the Middle Park when last seen

runners.name: Crown Relic
runners.spotlight: All races at 7f, winning a maiden and a novice at Wolverhampton in good style before his creditable fifth in a Deauville Group 3 last month (good to soft); drops in trip with a tongue-tie added for a trainer who this in 2020 and 2023.
runners.comment: Two 7f AW wins before a good 5th in a French Group 3; down in trip with tongue-tie added

runners.name: Midnight Tango
runners.spotlight: Ran well in Listed/Group 3 company last summer following her maiden win at Hamilton and her two lesser efforts at the end of the campaign can both be excused; this free-going filly shouldn't mind the return to sprinting but she will need to raise her game a notch.
runners.comment: Useful 2yo whose final two runs last year can both be excused; others bring stronger form

races.race_name: Prix Allez France (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Azaniya
runners.spotlight: Looks to be versatile with regards to underfoot conditions and ended 2025 on the up, winning back-to-back Listed events in France (one under Maxime Guyon), the last of which was here by over 4l on heavy ground; can race up with the pace or lead, so has the potential to dominate sectionals; stable in great form.
runners.comment: Stable in good form and should race on the front-end; interesting

runners.name: Cankoura
runners.spotlight: Developed into a high-class performer last year, placing third in the Group 1 Prix de Diane (1m2f 110yds, good to soft) before winning the Group 3 Prix de Psyche (1m2f, good) in August, when Zia Agnese was third; far from exposed on her 4yo return and trainer/owner already enjoying a fine season; holds a Group 1 entry.
runners.comment: Far from exposed on her 4yo return and trainer/owner already enjoying a fine season

runners.name: Sunly
runners.spotlight: Gave plenty of indications last year that she could be destined for top honours, starting her career with three wins before a narrow second in a 1m4f Group 2 at Longchamp; fascinating that she has been kept in training and reunited with Christophe Soumillon (who is 3-3 on her), so rates one of the key players if this trip doesn't prove to be too sharp at Pattern-race level; another with a Group 1 entry.
runners.comment: Highly promising last year and of firm interest if this trip doesn't prove too sharp

runners.name: Light The Ghost
runners.spotlight: Brought along steadily and rewarded connections patience with a Group 3 success here when last seen in October over a similar trip on very soft (Zia Agnese third that day); this looks a good starting point for the year and in the mix if handling the faster surface.
runners.comment: Won a Group 3 over a similar trip last year and in the mix if handles faster ground
runners.owner: Crispin De Moubray & M L P Consultancy

runners.name: Indalimos
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and improved throughout her 3yo campaign, which saw her land a 1m4f 110yd Group 3 at Deauville from a subsequent Group 1 winner; may not have stayed or liked the ground when sent to the top level after but made a nice start to this season over a similar trip at Longchamp, when Tajlina was a place behind; sound claims assuming normal progress.
runners.comment: Made a nice start to this season and sound claims assuming normal progress

runners.name: Tajlina
runners.spotlight: Notably a half-sister to dual Pattern-race winner and Group 1-placed Map Of Stars; ran well a couple of times last year at this level over similar trips and shaped with promise recently after a 352-day absence, so it'll be no surprise if she steps forward plenty from that; Group 1 entered and stable has two other leading contenders.
runners.comment: Not had much racing but has a classy pedigree; should benefit from recent return run

runners.name: South Bay
runners.spotlight: Won two of her five races, the most recent of which was a Listed event on the AW; never featured on her return to grass last time, albeit in a very good Group 3 here, but has plenty of scope for even better.
runners.comment: Has a good level of ability but needs to improve from most recent effort

runners.name: Zia Agnese
runners.spotlight: Smart filly, who has met a few of these in the past and is capable of a big effort on here day, as she showed when beating the odds-on Tajlina here last April, with Indalimos back in third; off since finishing third behind Light The Ghost in October, but no big surprise if she outruns her odds if showing up at her best.
runners.comment: Has met a few of these in the past and can outrun her odds at times

runners.name: Riyabovka
runners.spotlight: Smart mare at her peak and comes into this off a career-best in a Group 2 at Meydan in February when second over 1m1f on good ground; much more exposed than her rivals, though, so will want to repeat last time's level to have a chance of beating some of these.
runners.comment: New career best last time but more exposed than these

races.race_name: Betfred Jockey Club Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: Eydon
runners.spotlight: Had 1m4f wins last season in a Group 3 at Newbury (good to firm) in May and Group 2 in Italy (good; made all for career-best form) in October; tailed off in Hong Kong on final start but has a shout if back to his best.
runners.comment: Tailed off in Hong Kong on final start last term but has a shout if back to his best

runners.name: Sunway
runners.spotlight: Peak efforts were as a 3yo but reappearance at Meydan this January saw his first win since 2023; nearly won another 1m6f Group 3 there next time, before a headstrong display (set up big lead) saw him tail off in 2m Dubai Gold Cup five weeks ago; the latter was in blinkers (first time since September) which are unsurprisingly left off today; thereabouts if bouncing back.
runners.comment: Headstrong with blinkers over 2m latest start, after two solid 1m6f Group 3 runs at Meydan
runners.owner: Guy Pariente Holding & Qatar Racing Ltd

runners.name: Santorini Star
runners.spotlight: Upped to 2m for a handicap win at York Ebor meeting (good to firm) last August; tried front-running to positive effect against her own sex over about 1m6f on her subsequent two starts when winning the Group 2 Park Hill at Doncaster (good to soft) and second in Group 1 at Longchamp (very soft); has a form chance on this return but the stayer's mantle will have to be shed.
runners.comment: Progressive 2025; form chance on this return but the stayer's mantle will have to be shed

runners.name: Paradias
runners.spotlight: In the form of his life this winter with a win at Lingfield (1m4f) in February and close calls in his other three outings; those were on AW, which does seem to suit him particularly well, and also in handicaps, which leaves him with something to find in his first Group venture.
runners.comment: In the form of his life this winter but that was on AW and in handicaps; first Group race

runners.name: French Master
runners.spotlight: Won two 1m6f handicaps last term, latterly in taking style from off the pace at Royal Ascot (good to firm) when blinkered first time; slowly away on both subsequent starts, looking as if he was stretched by 2m in the Group 1 Goodwood Cup on first occasion (creditable) but tailed-off second favourite in the Ebor last time (visored; raced wide and made most); back to 1m4f, with headgear removed and needs overall improvement but the gelding operation may have helped.
runners.comment: Back down to 1m4f, headgear removed and needs overall improvement; gelding may have helped

runners.name: Lion's Pride
runners.spotlight: 7l Listed win over C&D (good to firm) last September; there are also plenty of lesser shows to be considered and, after he was not discredited in Qatar in February, he managed only fourth of six when favourite for Group 3 at Newbury 13 days ago; in the mix on peak efforts.
runners.comment: Plenty of lesser shows but in the mix on peak efforts, including 7l Listed win over C&D

races.race_name: Memberships At Ascot Paradise Stakes (Listed Race) (A Queen Anne Stakes Trial)

runners.name: Jonquil
runners.spotlight: 2025 seemed to establish that he's better over 1m than 6f or 7f and the best of those efforts, including at the top level, give him a good chance in today's company; reappearance in Abu Dhabi in February was not of the same order but he could bounce back.
runners.comment: Abu Dhabi show in February was not of the same order but best 1m efforts give good chance

runners.name: Holloway Boy
runners.spotlight: His strike-rate is an unimpressive 3-26 but he's seldom far away and last October's excellent second in a top handicap over C&D preceded an AW Listed win; came up short at Doha and Meydan early this year but showed enough to suggest he should be on the premises.
runners.comment: Strike-rate is unimpressive 3-26 but seldom far away; excellent 2nd in a top C&D handicap
runners.owner: N White, S White, T White & E Burke

runners.name: La Botte
runners.spotlight: Went close in the Britannia at Royal Ascot (1m, good to firm) last June on his fourth start; not seen again until a good fourth at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) this March, despite a slow start and having to come very wide in the straight; disappointing favourite in the Lincoln at Doncaster three weeks later, perhaps with the good to soft ground against him but overall improvement is required moving from handicaps into this Group 3.
runners.comment: Good to soft ground perhaps against him in the Lincoln but this is a move from handicaps
runners.owner: Halo Racing N Greenhalgh J Degenhardt

runners.name: Rashabar
